items.title: Session Memory service — background LLM notes file maintained during conversation
items.summary: CC's Session Memory service (`sessionMemory.ts`) auto-maintains a per-session markdown notes file in the background using a forked subagent. It runs periodically via post-sampling hook, extracting key info (decisions, context, in-progress items) from conversation without blocking the main loop. Distinct from extractMemories (which writes to ~/.claude/projects/memory) — this is an in-session scratchpad.
items.done: A `.claude/session-memory.md` is auto-populated and updated throughout the session. The notes are injected into context on /compact and session resume.

items.title: Streaming tool execution — start tools as tool_use blocks arrive in stream
items.summary: CC's StreamingToolExecutor begins executing tools as their `tool_use` blocks arrive in the streaming response, without waiting for the full response to complete. Concurrency-safe tools (Glob, Grep, Read, etc.) can start while the model is still generating. Queued tools wait for their slot. Reduces end-to-end latency for tool-heavy turns by overlapping execution with streaming.
items.done: A turn with 3 Glob calls starts executing the first Glob while the model is still streaming the 2nd and 3rd. Total wall-clock time drops proportionally.

items.title: `MessageDisplay` hook event — transform or hide assistant messages before rendering
items.summary: CC (v2.1.152) adds a `MessageDisplay` hook event that fires before each assistant message is shown. Hooks can rewrite or suppress the text — useful for operators that want to strip internal reasoning, add context, or route messages to external systems.
items.done: A MessageDisplay hook strips <thinking> blocks from displayed output. The transcript shows clean responses; raw content still reaches downstream tools.

items.title: Hooks engine — shell commands on agent events
items.summary: CC fires configurable shell hooks at key agent lifecycle events: PreToolUse (before a tool runs), PostToolUse (after), UserPromptSubmit (on user input), Stop (on session end). Hooks receive JSON context via stdin and can block/allow actions (PreToolUse exit code gates execution). Add a hooks runner in session.ts: load .vanta/hooks.json, match event + tool name, spawn shell command with JSON context, handle exit codes.
items.done: PreToolUse hooks can block tool execution; PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop hooks fire and complete without blocking. Hooks configured in .vanta/hooks.json.

items.title: `/goal` — set a completion condition; agent works until it's met
items.summary: CC (v2.1.139) adds `/goal` to set a completion condition for the session. Claude works autonomously across turns until the condition is satisfied. Works in interactive and headless sessions. Core to persistent autonomous agent loops.
items.done: Running `/goal done when tests pass` causes the agent to keep iterating until `npm test` passes without being re-prompted.
items.note: parseDoneCondition + checkGoalLoop in repl/goal-condition.ts; REPL loops via executeUserTurn (interactive-turn.ts); TUI loops via sendTurn(count) in use-agent-send.ts; 14 tests

items.title: Hook `additionalContext` — hooks return context to keep the turn going
items.summary: CC (v2.1.163) adds `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` to Stop and SubagentStop hooks. Instead of ending the turn, the hook can return feedback text that Claude receives as context and continues working. Enables hook-driven agent feedback loops.
items.done: A Stop hook returns `{additionalContext: 'Tests failed — retry with fix X'}`. Claude reads this and continues the turn without re-prompting.
items.note: fireStopHook in shell-hooks.ts; Stop hooks return {additionalContext} to continue the turn; wired into runPostTurnPipeline + TUI sendTurn; 6 new tests

items.title: Teams tool — named multi-agent squads
items.summary: CC has TeamCreateTool/TeamDeleteTool: define a named team of agents with roles, spawn them, coordinate work. More structured than Vanta's delegate/swarm. Add team_create (name + member roles + task) and team_delete; teams persist in .vanta/teams.json and can be addressed by send_message. Depends on CC-SEND-MSG.
items.done: `team_create` spins up a named squad of agents with defined roles; `team_delete` tears it down. Teams are addressable via send_message.

items.title: Background agent management — agents view, attach, logs, respawn, daemon
items.summary: [REGISTRY SLICE SHIPPED: team store (~/.vanta/team.jsonl: named workers role/model/tools/status) + team tool (define/status/list) + /team roster with blocker warnings. Runtime executor is a later slice.] CC has a full parallel-session management CLI: `claude agents` (dispatch/monitor view), `claude attach/logs/respawn/stop/rm <id>` for individual sessions, `claude daemon status/stop` for the supervisor. Vanta can spawn subagents but has no CLI for managing running background sessions.
items.done: `vanta agents` opens a live view of running sessions; `vanta attach/logs/respawn/stop/rm <id>` manage individual sessions; `vanta daemon status/stop` controls the supervisor; a `disableAgentView` setting gates the feature.

items.title: Auto permission mode — ML action classification + soft-deny preset
items.summary: CC's `--permission-mode auto` uses an ML classifier to auto-approve low-risk actions and soft-deny borderline ones without user prompts. Paired with `autoMode` settings (rules, soft_deny) and `vanta auto-mode defaults/config` CLI. Vanta's kernel uses keyword rules; it has no learned-classification layer.
items.done: `--permission-mode auto` (or `VANTA_AUTO_MODE=1`) applies ML-based classification on top of kernel rules; `vanta auto-mode defaults` prints built-in rules; `vanta auto-mode config` shows effective config with overrides; `autoMode` settings accept custom rules and a soft_deny preset.

items.title: Interactive edit-review panel — per-change keep/undo with keyboard nav
items.summary: A review surface for a turn's file edits, modeled on the reference in docs/design-refs/edit-review-panel.png: header 'N files edited · esc when done', per-file diff cards (old/new line numbers, green adds, red removes), focused-card highlight, ↑↓ moves between changes, 'a' keeps, 'z' undoes (kernel-gated restore of the pre-edit content), and an 'Add a follow-up' input that sends straight to the agent. Builds on the existing per-tool diff data (write_file diffs already render in the transcript) and the vendored fork's ScrollBox. Pairs with CC-STRUCTURED-DIFF (horizon) for the renderer; this card is the interaction loop.
items.done: /changes opens an interactive review overlay: changed files from git (changed-files.ts) with +/- stats, ↑↓ navigate, the selected file diff previews, u=undo (git checkout for tracked / delete for untracked — keypress is the per-action consent), Esc close. Per-file granularity (hunk-level deferred).

items.title: TUI design language — lipgloss-grade layout primitives (tabs, dialogs, panels, status chips)
items.summary: Visual quality bar set by docs/design-refs/lipgloss-layout-bar.png (charmbracelet/lipgloss demo): real tab rows, centered modal dialogs with focus-styled buttons, side-by-side framed list panels, colored status-bar chips (STATUS/encoding/badge), adaptive color ramps. Vanta already has Overlay/OverlayRow + theme.ts; this card grows them into a small primitive set (Tabs, Dialog, Panel, StatusChip) used by the existing overlays (skills/sessions/model pickers, approval prompt, status bar) so every surface shares one visual system under the vendored fork. No new deps — pure Box/Text composition.
items.done: Pickers, approval prompt, and the status bar render through shared Tab/Dialog/Panel/StatusChip primitives; a /theme switch restyles all of them coherently; screenshots match the reference's structure.
items.notes: Shipped 2026-06-12 (commits 78a9b94, f4db2ed, 19b152d, 13ede05, f1f4034, a266283), design language from docs/design-refs/tui-demo.html applied to the live TUI: (1) pickers + approval render the full-width themed selection bar via shared Overlay/OverlayRow, accent resolved from the active theme not hard-coded cyan; (2) the approval prompt is the kernel ASK verdict box — gated command on its own line + a 'why' line; (3) StatusChip primitive (status-chip.tsx) drives the status bar's run-state + mode chips; (4) Tabs primitive (tabs.tsx) drives the model wizard's Provider · Model step row; (5) /theme is a real live switch (was a stub) — composer + status bar restyle instantly, modal pickers re-resolve their accent on next open. Design note: Panel and Dialog are both realized by the one shared Overlay (one frame, two roles) rather than two near-duplicate components — leaner per Rule-of-3. A persistent multi-pane tab SURFACE (vs the wizard step row) belongs to TUI-V2's mission-control layout, not this primitive-kit card.

items.title: TUI v2 — mission-control surface (non-destructive)
items.summary: 3-column operator/mission-control TUI from the mockup Vanta built (docs/-model-v2.html): left durable-state rail, center goal+risk-tagged transcript, right safety-rail + working-memory + live telemetry. CRITICAL constraint: build WITHOUT overwriting the working v1 TUI — new tui/v2/ module, opt-in via VANTA_TUI=v2 (default stays v1), reuse the shared engine read-only, promote only after Jason confirms. Mostly a surface over already-built state. Detail: docs/tui-v2.md.
items.done: VANTA_TUI=v2 launches the 3-column mission-control TUI while the default v1 TUI is byte-for-byte unchanged and still default; v2 is a separate module, zero v1 files edited.
items.notes: Design references captured 2026-06-11 in docs/design-refs/ (edit-review-panel.png, lipgloss-layout-bar.png) — TUI-EDIT-REVIEW and TUI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE are the concrete first slices.

items.title: Tab navigation — focus management between UI elements
items.summary: CC's tabstops.ts + use-tab-status.ts enable Tab/Shift+Tab focus traversal between interactive elements (buttons, inputs, list items). Required for the settings UI, MCP panel, hooks config, and agent wizard to be keyboard-navigable. Vanta's TUI has no focus traversal.
items.done: Tab moves focus to the next interactive element; Shift+Tab moves back; focused elements show a visual indicator; Enter activates; all dialogs and panels are fully keyboard-navigable.

items.title: Desktop P3 — real React/Vite renderer
items.summary: Phase 3: replace the giant-string UI with a real Vite+React app (AppShell/SessionSidebar/ChatThread/Composer/RightRail) under vanta-ts/desktop-app/. Vanta operator/dossier aesthetic, not generic SaaS.
items.done: No giant-string UI; current functionality preserved in real components; builds via Vite.

items.title: Per-tool permission request UIs — dedicated dialogs per tool type
items.summary: CC has dedicated permission request components for each tool: BashPermissionRequest (shows command + options), FileEditPermissionRequest (shows diff), FileWritePermissionRequest (shows new content), NotebookEdit, PowerShell, Sandbox, Sed, Skill, WebFetch, ComputerUse. Each shows the relevant context for informed approval. Vanta shows a generic y/n prompt.
items.done: Each tool type has a purpose-built permission dialog: Bash shows the command with syntax highlight + options; FileEdit shows a diff; FileWrite shows new content; all support Allow/Deny/Always/Never rules.

items.title: Unified @-completion typeahead — files, MCP resources, agents, slash, Slack, shell history
items.summary: Single typeahead covering: @path file completions, @AgentType agent completions with color, MCP resource completions, slash-command + argument hints, #channel Slack completions (when Slack MCP active), shell history completions, directory path completions. Fuse.js fuzzy matching. Inline ghost text for best match.
items.done: Typing @ surfaces files/agents/MCP resources; typing / surfaces commands with argument hints; typing # surfaces Slack channels; ghost text shows best match inline; Fuse fuzzy matching ranks results.

items.title: Solutioning mode — research → decide what to build + how to win
items.summary: A 7th operator mode (alongside modes/builtin.ts) running the FRONT half of rocket.new's loop, Vanta-native: web research + browser + brain + goals → an evidence-backed, RANKED 'what to build and why it wins' recommendation, not just execution. Local + kernel-gated; no platform, no SaaS. Pairs with AUTO-WATCH (the 'watch what's next' half) on one shared brain. From the rocket.new 'Solve' layer; the part Jason actually wants.
items.done: From a vague objective, Vanta returns a ranked recommendation (what to build + why it wins) grounded in goals+brain, with sources, before any build action.

items.title: Plugin framework (register(ctx) + PluginContext, like Claude/Codex)
items.summary: A unified plugin framework so Vanta extends without core edits, like Claude/Codex: a register(ctx) entry + PluginContext API (register_tool/hook/command/cli/skill/provider/platform, llm.complete, dispatch_tool). Discovery precedence bundled(vanta-ts/plugins/) -> user(~/.vanta/plugins/) -> project(.vanta/plugins/, env-gated) -> npm; opt-in plugins.enabled allow-list. Ties together the pieces Vanta already has (tool registry, MCP/mount_mcp, hooks, MSG-REGISTRY, skills) under one contract. Vanta's kernel stays the boundary — every plugin tool routes through assess() (unlike other agents that run plugins unsandboxed). Builds on PLUGIN-SYSTEM (install hygiene). 'vanta plugins install/enable/disable/remove/list'. Detail: docs/plugin-framework.md.
items.done: A third-party plugin dropped in ~/.vanta/plugins/ and added to plugins.enabled registers a kernel-gated tool + a slash command that work next session; disabled/not-enabled plugins don't load.

items.title: --json-schema — structured output + JSON Schema validation
items.summary: CC's `--json-schema <file|inline>` forces the model to produce a response that validates against the provided JSON Schema, with retries on validation failure. Enables reliable structured extraction in automation pipelines. Vanta has no schema-constrained output.
items.done: `--json-schema <file>` (or `VANTA_JSON_SCHEMA`) forces JSON output and validates each response; on validation failure the agent retries with the error; the final result is printed as validated JSON to stdout in print mode.
items.note: output/json-schema.ts: loadJsonSchema/extractJson/validateAgainstSchema/validateOutput/buildSchemaInstruction; runInstruction opts.jsonSchema + VANTA_JSON_SCHEMA + sendWithSchemaRetry (3 attempts); --json-schema CLI flag in parseRunArgs; 19 tests

items.title: Hook matcher depth — all conditional hook matchers
items.summary: CC hook rules support rich conditional matchers: session start method (startup/resume/clear/compact), CLI trigger (init/maintenance), session end reason, notification type, agent type, compaction trigger, config source, literal filenames, error type (rate_limit/overloaded/auth), load reason, skill/command names, MCP server names. Vanta hooks support basic tool-name matching only.
items.done: All CC matcher categories are implemented for the Vanta hooks engine; the settings schema accepts any combination; docs show a worked example for each matcher type.
items.note: Extended ShellHookSchema with toolNamePattern/inputPattern/promptPattern/onError/sessionType conditional matchers. MatchContext type + hookMatches/matchesPattern helpers. Backward-compat: matcher deprecated alias kept; toolNamePattern absent toolName = match all (non-tool events).

items.title: API key helper script — dynamic API key via shell command or project settings
items.summary: CC supports `api_key_helper` — a shell command configured in settings or project config that is executed to dynamically retrieve an API key (e.g. from a vault, 1Password, or credential manager). `getApiKeyFromApiKeyHelperCached()` caches the result with a TTL. `prefetchApiKeyFromApiKeyHelperIfSafe()` warms the cache during startup. Also supports AWS credential export from project settings.
items.done: `api_key_helper: 'op read op://vault/anthropic/key'` in settings.json fetches the API key from 1Password at startup. Cached and refreshed automatically.
items.note: api_key_helper in settings.json (user/project/local layered) runs a shell command to fetch the API key dynamically; result cached 5min. Injected into the active provider env var at prepareRun. 13 tests.

items.title: Checkpoint + rewind code & conversation
items.summary: Claude Code snapshots files before each edit and rewinds code and/or conversation to a prior point (Esc-Esc / /rewind). Vanta has /undo (drops the last turn) but no code restore. Add per-edit checkpoints and a rewind that restores both.
items.done: /rewind lists recent checkpoints; selecting one restores the file(s) to that snapshot and optionally truncates the conversation; a checkpoint is written before every write_file.

items.title: AST code compressor (keep signatures, drop bodies)
items.summary: Follow-up to COMPRESS-NATIVE. Headroom's CodeCompressor, native: for.ts/.tsx tool outputs (file reads), use the TS compiler API (already in lint/size.ts + lsp/ts-service.ts) to keep imports/signatures/types and elide function bodies → big shrink on large file reads while the agent keeps the shape. Reversible via the existing CCR store. Only fires on detected code; text stays untouched.
items.done: Large.ts/.tsx tool outputs compress by eliding function bodies while preserving imports + signatures; reversible via retrieve_original.
items.note: ast-compress.ts uses TS compiler API to elide function bodies (≥4 lines) while preserving imports, types, and signatures. Wired into dispatch-helpers compressOutput for read_file; applyCodeCompression stashes original in CCR for retrieval. 11 tests.

items.title: Send message tool — agent-to-agent messaging
items.summary: CC has SendMessageTool: send a message to another running agent or thread (used in multi-agent orchestration). Maps to Vanta's A2A bus (a2a/local.ts). Wire the existing A2ABus into a send_message tool so orchestrators can push work to named agents. Kernel Ask (network/process side-effect).
items.done: `send_message` tool delivers a message to a named agent via the A2A bus; receiver processes it on next tick.
items.note: send_message tool routes via globalBus (a2a/bus.ts). Delivers to registered agent handlers, returns reply text. 8 tests + registry test updated.

items.title: /hooks — manage shell hooks from the REPL
items.summary: CC has a /hooks command to list, add, edit, and delete hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, UserPromptSubmit) from within the session. Vanta has no hook system at all. This card is the config surface — CC-HOOKS-ENGINE (the runtime) is a separate prerequisite card. Add /hooks to list configured hooks and open .vanta/hooks.json for editing.
items.done: `/hooks` lists configured hooks by event type; user can add/remove entries that persist to .vanta/hooks.json.

items.title: /pr-comments — view and resolve GitHub PR comments
items.summary: CC has /pr_comments to fetch PR review comments from GitHub and surface them in-session. Pair with autofix: the agent reads each comment, proposes a fix, applies on approval. Requires GITHUB_TOKEN. Add /pr-comments [PR-url|number] that fetches review threads and queues them as tasks.
items.done: `/pr-comments <PR>` fetches review comments and queues each as a task; agent works through them one at a time.

items.title: Effort levels — --effort flag, effortLevel setting, extended thinking controls
items.summary: CC supports --effort low/medium/high/xhigh/max to tune the trade-off between speed, cost, and reasoning depth. Paired with `effortLevel` (persist across sessions), `alwaysThinkingEnabled`, and `showThinkingSummaries`. Vanta has no equivalent effort-level system.
items.done: `--effort <level>` and `VANTA_EFFORT` env var are wired; `effortLevel` in settings persists the default; `alwaysThinkingEnabled` enables extended thinking by default; `showThinkingSummaries` shows abbreviated reasoning in the TUI.

items.title: Path-scoped rules system — .vanta/rules/ with YAML frontmatter + globs
items.summary: CC supports `.claude/rules/<name>.md` files with YAML frontmatter specifying glob patterns that scope the rule to matching files (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `*.md`). Rules load only when the agent touches matching files. User-level rules at `~/.claude/rules/`. Nested dirs and symlinks supported. Vanta has global VANTA.md rules but no path-scoped rule files.
items.done: `.vanta/rules/<name>.md` files with `globs:` frontmatter are parsed; matching rules are injected into the system prompt only when relevant files are in context; `~/.vanta/rules/` provides user-level rules; symlinks work.

items.title: Accept Edits permission mode — auto-approve filesystem ops without prompt
items.summary: CC has an 'acceptEdits' permission mode (distinct from bypassPermissions) that auto-allows a specific subset of filesystem commands (mkdir, touch, rm, rmdir, mv, cp, sed) without prompting, while still prompting for bash commands outside that set. Cycling through modes with Shift+Tab offers: default → acceptEdits → plan → default.
items.done: Permission mode cycle includes acceptEdits. In acceptEdits mode, filesystem commands (mkdir/touch/rm/mv/cp/sed) execute without prompt; other shell commands still prompt.

items.title: Team memory secret scanner — block file edits that introduce secrets to team memory
items.summary: When FileEditTool writes a file, CC calls `checkTeamMemSecrets` to scan the edit for secrets before syncing to team memory. Prevents accidental secret propagation (API keys, tokens) to shared team memory storage.
items.done: File edits that contain secret patterns (API keys, tokens) are flagged or blocked from team memory sync; warning shown in tool output.

items.title: LSP async diagnostic push — publishDiagnostics notifications as conversation attachments
items.summary: CC's LSP layer registers a `publishDiagnostics` notification handler. When LSP servers push diagnostics asynchronously (not just on request), they're stored in an LRU-based registry and delivered to the conversation as attachments automatically. The agent doesn't need to explicitly poll for errors — they appear as context when LSP pushes them.
items.done: After a file write, new LSP errors pushed via publishDiagnostics appear in the next turn as an attachment, without the agent having to call an LSP tool.

items.title: StructuredOutput tool — schema-validated JSON output for SDK/non-interactive sessions
items.summary: CC has a SyntheticOutputTool (`StructuredOutput`) enabled only in non-interactive/SDK sessions. When an SDK caller provides a JSON schema, the model is forced to call this tool as its final action, and the output is validated against the schema before returning. This is how workflow agents return structured data instead of free text — and how `agent({schema})` in workflows works.
items.done: SDK sessions with a schema constraint receive a validated JSON object as the agent's return value. The agent calls `StructuredOutput({...})` as its terminal action.

items.title: Durable cron jobs — persist scheduled tasks across session restarts
items.summary: CC's CronCreate tool has a `durable` flag (default false). Durable crons are written to `.claude/scheduled_tasks.json` and survive session restarts — OS-triggered cron invocations can fire them. Non-durable (default) crons are in-memory only and die when the session ends. The `recurring` flag (default true) controls one-shot vs repeating. Vanta's cron is in-memory only.
items.done: `cron_create(durable: true)` persists the job to `.vanta/scheduled_tasks.json`. After a session restart, `cron_list` shows the job and it fires at the next scheduled time.

items.title: Agent hook type — hooks backed by a full multi-turn LLM agent
items.summary: CC supports an 'agent' hook type where each hook event fires a full multi-turn LLM agent (tool-enabled, with access to a restricted toolset). The agent runs in isolation, receives the hook event as JSON input, and returns a structured JSON result via StructuredOutput. Enables complex conditional logic in hooks — e.g. 'analyze this command with a reasoning model before allowing it'.
items.done: A hook configured with `type: agent` fires a full query per event. The agent can call tools, reason across turns, and return a structured block/allow verdict.

items.title: Post-compact file restoration — re-inject top files and skills after compaction
items.summary: After CC compacts a conversation, it runs a post-compact restoration phase: up to 5 key files are re-read into context (budget: 50K tokens total, 5K per file), and active skills are re-injected (budget: 25K tokens). This prevents the agent from 'forgetting' critical files that were recently edited. `runPostCompactCleanup()` orchestrates this.
items.done: After /compact, the 5 most recently accessed files are automatically re-read into the fresh context. The agent doesn't lose track of the active codebase state.

items.title: History snipping — automatic SDK conversation pruning for long-running sessions
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `HISTORY_SNIP`) automatically snips (prunes) conversation history in long SDK sessions to bound memory. Uses `snipCompact` to truncate and `snipProjection` to maintain a view. SDK-only — the interactive REPL keeps full history for scrollback. Distinct from manual /compact: triggered automatically by token count rather than user action.
items.done: In long SDK runs, conversation history is pruned to a rolling window automatically. `snipProjection` provides a projected view for the agent without the full context.

items.title: Verification agent — built-in agent that auto-verifies task completion
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `VERIFICATION_AGENT`) registers a built-in verification agent that runs automatically after `TaskUpdate` tool calls that mark tasks complete. The verifier checks the work against the task description and flags discrepancies. Listed in `builtInAgents` and gated by subscription.
items.done: When an agent marks a TodoItem as 'completed', the verification agent runs and appends a pass/fail check to the transcript.

items.title: Reactive compact — proactive compaction triggered by context analysis warnings
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `REACTIVE_COMPACT`) adds reactive compaction: when `analyzeContext` detects that specific context categories (tool results, file reads) are consuming disproportionate tokens, it triggers compaction proactively before the context window fills. Distinct from auto-compact (threshold-based) and microcompact (cache-based).
items.done: When a large tool result is detected consuming >40% of context, reactive compact runs automatically to trim it before the agent's next turn.

items.title: `/init` command — generate a CLAUDE.md project context file
items.summary: CC has an `/init` command that analyzes the current project and generates a `CLAUDE.md` file with discovered context: tech stack, key files, coding conventions, and setup instructions. `NEW_INIT` is an improved variant that generates richer context. The file primes future Claude sessions on this project.
items.done: Running `/init` in a new project generates `.claude/CLAUDE.md` with detected stack, file structure, and conventions. Future sessions load this automatically.

items.title: Token count API — pre-compute context size before API call (Anthropic/Bedrock/Vertex)
items.summary: CC's `tokenEstimation.ts` provides `countTokensWithAPI()`, `countTokensViaHaikuFallback()`, and `countTokensWithBedrock()`. These allow pre-computing the token count of a conversation before sending to avoid context window overruns. Supports Anthropic API, AWS Bedrock, and Vertex AI backends. Used internally for context budget management and proactive compaction decisions.
items.done: Before sending a large conversation, CC calls countTokens to check if it fits. If it exceeds budget, compaction runs first. All three providers (Anthropic, Bedrock, Vertex) return token estimates.

items.title: `/bg` flag preservation — backgrounded sessions keep all startup flags
items.summary: CC (v2.1.143-144) ensures `/bg` and `←`-detach preserve all startup flags: `--mcp-config`, `--settings`, `--add-dir`, `--plugin-dir`, `--strict-mcp-config`, `--fallback-model`, `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions`. Also (v2.1.153): `/bg` while responding continues the active response in the background session instead of dropping it.
items.done: Backgrounding a session with `--mcp-config custom.json` spawns a worker with the same MCP config. An in-progress response continues streaming in the background.

items.title: Hook MCP tool invocation — `type: 'mcp_tool'` in hook config calls MCP tools directly
items.summary: CC (v2.1.118) allows hooks to invoke MCP tools directly via `type: 'mcp_tool'` in hook config. Hooks no longer need a shell command to call an MCP server — they reference the tool by server name and tool name. Enables tight hook↔MCP integration without subprocess overhead.
items.done: A PostToolUse hook with `type: 'mcp_tool', server: 'notify', tool: 'send_notification'` triggers the MCP notification tool directly.

items.title: Advisor escalation — read-only stronger model after repeated failure
items.summary: Steal rocket.new's 'Advisor Agent': after the loop fails the same fix N times (Vanta has onIterationCheck + error-detect), escalate to a stronger READ-ONLY model that returns root cause + numbered steps + a trade-off table and NEVER writes code. Wires the escalation trigger + read-only contract around the existing advisorModel config.
items.done: On the 2nd+ consecutive failed fix, Vanta consults a read-only advisor and surfaces root-cause + numbered next steps; the advisor cannot call write/edit tools.

items.title: Hook type parity — http, mcp_tool, prompt, agent hook types
items.summary: CC has 5 hook types: command (shell), http (POST to remote endpoint), mcp_tool (call a tool on an MCP server), prompt (send a prompt to Claude for evaluation), and agent (spawn a subagent with tools). Vanta's CC-HOOKS-ENGINE covers command hooks; the other 4 types are unimplemented.
items.done: All 5 CC hook types are implemented: command (exists), http (POST with headers + allowedEnvVars), mcp_tool (call a named MCP tool), prompt (evaluate with a model), and agent (spawn a tool-enabled subagent); all respect the timeout + once + statusMessage fields.

items.title: Extended hook event coverage — all 30 CC hook events
items.summary: CC defines ~30 named hook events. Vanta's hooks cover the core 8 (SessionStart/End, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact, PostCompact). Missing: TeammateIdle, TaskCreated/Completed, CwdChanged, FileChanged, ConfigChange, InstructionsLoaded, WorktreeCreate/Remove, Elicitation/ElicitationResult, MessageDisplay, UserPromptExpansion, StopFailure, PostToolBatch, SubagentStart/Stop.
items.done: All 30 CC hook events are implemented and documented; existing event hooks are unchanged; each new event fires at the correct point in the agent/session lifecycle.

items.title: ToolUseLoader — dedicated loading animation per tool call
items.summary: CC's ToolUseLoader shows a distinct animated loading state while a tool is executing (between call and result), including the tool name and a progress indicator. Vanta shows the spinner globally; individual tool calls have no per-tool loading state.
items.done: While a tool is running, its entry shows a tool-specific loader (name + animated indicator); on completion it transitions to the result; parallel calls each have their own loader.

items.title: `/tui` — toggle fullscreen flicker-free rendering mode in the same session
items.summary: Adds `/tui [fullscreen|status|exit]` slash command. `/tui status` or bare `/tui` reports the current renderer; `/tui fullscreen` confirms already in fullscreen alt-screen mode; `/tui exit` exits the session. Handler in `repl/tui-cmd.ts`, registered in catalog + handlers.
items.done: Running `/tui fullscreen` in the TUI confirms fullscreen mode. `/tui exit` exits. All 5 tests pass, tsc clean.

items.title: `/focus` command — separate focus view toggle (split from Ctrl+O)
items.summary: Adds `/focus` slash command that toggles focus mode in the TUI. Focus mode filters the transcript to user + assistant entries only — tool calls and system notes are hidden. State lives in `app-reducer.ts` (`focusMode: boolean`, `toggleFocus` action). `/focus` returns `toggleFocusMode: true` in `SlashResult`; `use-submit.ts applySlashResult` dispatches the reducer action. `app.tsx` filters `visibleEntries` accordingly.
items.done: Running `/focus` hides tool events in the TUI transcript. `/focus` again restores the full view. 3 tests pass, tsc clean.

items.title: Line-based virtual scrolling — viewport slices by rendered lines, not entries
items.summary: Shipped by vendoring the hermes-ink fork (vendor/hermes-ink, MIT, aliased as `ink`) instead of porting onto stock ink: its ScrollBox scrolls by rendered LINES with viewport culling, partial-entry rendering, and stickyScroll bottom-follow; AlternateScreen owns the alt buffer + native mouse-wheel tracking (SGR reports become wheelUp/wheelDown key events in the fork's keypress parser, consumed by use-scroll-keys.ts). The whole entry-granular machinery (AltFrame, VirtualTranscript, viewOffset reducer state, use-mouse-wheel 1007 hack) is deleted. Composer paste rides the fork's isPasted input events (no usePaste); ink-testing-library replaced by tui/test-render.tsx (renderSync + terminal-grid emulator). The fork's own 16 test files run in our vitest.
items.done: An assistant reply taller than the terminal reads top to bottom with trackpad/wheel/pgup — every line reachable, partial entries render at the viewport edges, native click/drag selection intact. 2665 tests green.

items.title: Scroll up while agent is streaming (normal mode)
items.summary: pgup/pgdn scroll was already wired in alt-screen mode (useKeybindings has no busy gate). The only missing piece was snap-to-bottom on turn complete: `handleCommit` in app-reducer now resets `viewOffset: 0` so the user auto-sees the final response. `VirtualTranscript` already shows ↑/↓ scroll indicators when content is cut. Follow-up fix: the viewport clamped scroll to total-maxVisible ENTRIES while maxVisible is derived from terminal ROWS — short transcripts with tall (multi-line) entries were unscrollable. Clamp is now total-1 (every entry reachable) and the reducer ceilings viewOffset at entries-1 so over-scroll can't strand pgdn.
items.done: User can scroll with the mouse/trackpad (SGR mouse reporting, use-mouse-wheel.ts), plain ↑/↓ while scrolled back, or pgup/pgdn/⇧↑↓ — during generation too. View snaps back to bottom when the turn completes. Native scrollback was lost when alt-screen mode shipped; mouse reporting restores it.

items.title: Syntax-highlighted code blocks — HighlightedCode with language detection
items.summary: CC's HighlightedCode renders code blocks with full syntax highlighting (language-specific token coloring) and a Fallback for unsupported languages. Vanta shows code in fenced blocks (CC-S-MARKDOWN) but without syntax-token coloring.
items.done: Fenced code blocks in the transcript are syntax-highlighted: keyword→accent, string→success, number→warning, comment→dim, plain→primary. Lightweight lossless tokenizer (ui/highlight.ts) for ts/js/json/bash/py/rust/go wired into markdown.tsx. 72 tests green.

items.title: TrustDialog — project trust + MCP server trust confirmation
items.summary: CC's TrustDialog prompts the user to confirm trust for new projects (allowing CLAUDE.md to load) and new MCP servers (before mounting their tools). Blocking trust decisions behind explicit confirmation is a key security UX. Vanta loads VANTA.md and mounts MCP without a trust gate.
items.done: First time a project is opened, TrustDialog shows its CLAUDE.md contents and asks to trust; first time an MCP server is mounted, a dialog shows its tools and asks to trust; decisions are persisted in project settings.

items.title: Sub-agent progress summaries — periodic 3-5 word status in footer pill
items.summary: Coordinator sub-agents generate a 3-5 word present-tense progress summary every 30s via a forked side-query. Summaries name the specific file or function being worked on (not vague descriptions). Shown in the footer task pill. Cache-shared with parent agent to avoid extra billing.
items.done: Running sub-agent tasks show a live progress summary in the footer pill (e.g. 'Editing auth.ts'); summaries update every ~30s; summaries are specific, not vague.

items.title: TUI v2 — state / safety / working-memory / telemetry rails
items.summary: v2 rails: left durable-state (goal/model/scope/context-budget meter), right safety-rail (live ALLOW/ASK/BLOCK for current context) + working-memory panel + live telemetry (tokens/cost/time). Composes COST-VISIBLE + EF-WORKINGMEM + AUTO-ROUTER + THINK-FOLD — makes already-built state visible.
items.done: The v2 surface shows the durable-state rail, a live safety ALLOW/ASK/BLOCK rail, the working-memory panel, and tokens/cost/time telemetry, all from real session state.

items.title: TUI image paste + clickable links and file paths (CC-CLI parity)
items.summary: Match Claude Code CLI ergonomics in the Ink TUI. Two parts: (1) paste an image directly into the composer (clipboard → ImageAttachment, like the existing /paste macOS path but on a paste keystroke) so vision works without a file path; (2) make links (http/https) and file paths (incl. file:line) in transcript output clickable — open the URL in the browser / the file in $VANTA_EDITOR (reuse editor/open.ts openInEditor + the file:line parser). OSC-8 hyperlinks where the terminal supports them, with a key-to-open fallback.
items.done: Pasting an image into the TUI attaches it for vision; clicking a link opens the browser and clicking a file path (or file:line) opens it in the editor, matching Claude Code CLI.

items.title: Vim mode — vi keybindings in the composer
items.summary: CC has /vim to toggle vi-mode in the REPL composer (normal/insert mode, hjkl navigation, dd/yy/p operations on the input buffer). Vanta's composer is custom readline with no vim mode. Add a vim mode toggle to the TUI composer backed by a state machine (normal|insert), recognizing common vim motions.
items.done: `/vim` toggles vi-mode in the TUI composer; normal mode supports hjkl, w/b, dd, yy, p, i/a/A/I/o/O to enter insert mode.

items.title: Rich status line parity — rate limits, lines diff, session name, worktree
items.summary: CC's StatusLine shows: 5-hour and 7-day rate limit utilization % + resets_at timestamps, lines added/removed in the session, session name, output style name, worktree context, vim mode state, and hooks into the hook system for custom fields. Vanta's status line (CC-S-STATUSLINE shipped) shows model + mode; missing the rest.
items.done: Status line includes rate-limit utilization bars, lines-added/removed delta, session name, worktree indicator, and a hook call point for custom status segments.

items.title: Teammate spinner tree — parallel agent visualization
items.summary: CC's TeammateSpinnerTree renders a live tree of all running agents: leader verb + token count, each teammate's name + status, selection mode for switching focus, idle text when an agent finishes. Vanta's spinner shows one agent; parallel agents are invisible.
items.done: While multiple agents run, the spinner area shows a tree: leader line (verb + tokens), one line per teammate (name + current action); Shift+arrow cycles focus between agents.

items.title: Context visualization — per-category breakdown + autocompact buffer + suggestions
items.summary: CC's ContextVisualization shows context usage broken down per category (tools, memory, messages, autocompact buffer, etc.) with a CollapseStatus showing what context-collapse did. ContextSuggestions offers proactive actions to reduce context. Vanta shows a context % number (CC-S-CONTEXT shipped) but no breakdown.
items.done: /context opens a visual overlay: per-category token breakdown (system/tools/user/assistant/tool-results) with a bar + count + % each, total vs context window in the title. contextBreakdown (pure) + ContextPanel, wired as an overlay kind reading the live convo via getContext. 178 ui tests green.

items.title: Sandbox settings UI — config, dependencies, doctor, overrides tabs
items.summary: CC's SandboxSettings UI has 4 tabs: Config (enable/disable, filesystem/network isolation), Dependencies (packages to pre-install), Doctor (health diagnostics), Overrides (per-tool sandbox rules). Pairs with CC-SANDBOX (next). Vanta has no sandbox UI.
items.done: /sandbox opens the settings UI; Config tab toggles sandbox + isolation levels; Dependencies lists pre-installed packages; Doctor diagnoses issues; Overrides lets specific tools bypass or enforce.

items.title: MCP management panel — list, tool detail, reconnect, elicitation
items.summary: CC's MCP UI has: MCPListPanel (all servers + connection status), MCPToolDetailView (tool schema + description), MCPToolListView (all tools per server), MCPReconnect (retry failed server), ElicitationDialog (MCP server requesting user input mid-call), MCPAgentServerMenu, MCPRemoteServerMenu. Vanta mounts MCP servers but has no management UI.
items.done: /mcp opens the panel; each server shows connected/error status with reconnect option; tool detail shows name/schema/description; ElicitationDialog appears when a server needs user input.

items.title: Hooks configuration menu — event/hook/matcher mode selectors
items.summary: CC's hooks UI has: HooksConfigMenu (list all hooks), SelectEventMode (pick event type), SelectHookMode (pick hook type: command/http/etc), SelectMatcherMode (configure matchers), ViewHookMode (inspect a hook). Pairs with CC-HOOKS-CMD (next) for /hooks command. Vanta will have /hooks but needs this interactive config UI.
items.done: /hooks opens the config menu; arrow keys navigate; selecting a hook opens view mode; new hook wizard walks through event → type → matcher → action; all hooks are editable without touching settings.json.

items.title: QuickOpenDialog — Ctrl+P fuzzy open for files, sessions, commands
items.summary: CC's QuickOpenDialog (Ctrl+P / Cmd+P) is a unified fuzzy picker for files, past sessions, slash commands, and skills — similar to VS Code's command palette but covering all entry points at once. Vanta has a slash palette but no unified quick-open.
items.done: Ctrl+P opens QuickOpenDialog; typing filters across files (current project), sessions (recent), commands (/), and skills; Enter opens the result; category icons distinguish the types.

items.title: Task management panel — TaskListV2, background task dialogs
items.summary: CC has TaskListV2 (live list of all tasks with status badges), BackgroundTasksDialog, BackgroundTask + BackgroundTaskStatus components, plus detail dialogs: AsyncAgentDetailDialog, DreamDetailDialog, InProcessTeammateDetailDialog, RemoteSessionDetailDialog, ShellDetailDialog. Vanta has CC-TASKS-CMD (next) for the /tasks command; this is the full panel UI.
items.done: /tasks opens the panel; each task shows type (agent/shell/remote) + status + elapsed; selecting a task opens the detail dialog with full output log; tasks can be stopped/respawned from the panel.

items.title: Teams UI — TeamsDialog, TeamStatus, task assignment messages
items.summary: CC has TeamsDialog (create/manage agent teams), TeamStatus (live team state), TaskAssignmentMessage (shows task assignment in transcript), UserTeammateMessage (teammate-to-leader comms). Pairs with CC-TEAMS (next tool). Vanta has no teams management UI.
items.done: /teams opens TeamsDialog for creating and managing agent teams; running teams show TeamStatus in the spinner area; task assignments and teammate messages appear distinctly in the transcript.

items.title: Agent management UI — AgentDetail, AgentEditor, AgentsList, AgentsMenu
items.summary: CC has AgentDetail (full agent view with model/tools/memory), AgentEditor (edit agent config inline), AgentsList + AgentsMenu (browse all defined agents), plus the full creation wizard. Pairs with CC-BG-AGENTS (next) and CC-AGENT-WIZARD (horizon). Vanta has no agent management UI.
items.done: /agents list shows all defined agents; selecting one opens AgentDetail with config; AgentEditor allows inline editing; a ColorPicker and ModelSelector are embedded within the editor.

items.title: Bash image output — base64 data URI in stdout renders as inline image
items.summary: CC detects when bash stdout contains a base64-encoded `data:image/...;base64,...` URI and renders it as an inline image in the terminal output, rather than printing the raw base64 string. Enables commands like screenshot tools, image-generating scripts, etc. to show their output visually.
items.done: A bash command whose stdout is a data URI image renders the image inline in terminal output.

items.title: AI-powered permission explanation — risk level + reasoning for tool calls
items.summary: When a tool call requires permission, CC fires a side query (sideQuery) to an LLM that analyzes the command and returns a structured explanation: risk level (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH), plain-English explanation, reasoning, and risk summary. This explanation shows in the permission dialog to help the user make informed decisions.
items.done: Permission dialogs show a risk-level badge (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) with one-sentence AI explanation of what the command does and why it's being run.

items.title: Shell tab-completion in bash input — command, variable, and file suggestions
items.summary: CC's bash input supports real-time shell tab-completion: typing a partial command shows up to 15 completions from the shell (command names, shell variable names, or file paths). The completion type is inferred from the prefix: `$` = variable, `/`/`~`/`.` = file, otherwise command. A 1-second timeout prevents UI blocking.
items.done: Pressing Tab on a partial bash command shows a completion dropdown; results adapt per prefix type (command/file/variable).

items.title: /stats — usage statistics and activity dashboard in-session
items.summary: CC has a `/stats` command that shows usage statistics and activity data for the current user within the session. Distinct from the per-turn cost footer — this provides aggregate metrics (total sessions, turns, tools used, tokens spent) with a visual summary.
items.done: `/stats` opens a usage stats view showing session/turn/cost aggregates for the account.

items.title: AskUserQuestion tool — structured multi-question UI with options, previews, multi-select
items.summary: CC's AskUserQuestion tool lets the model present 1-4 structured questions in one call, each with 2-4 labeled options (plus auto-added 'Other'), optional per-option preview content (markdown in a side panel), optional multi-select, and a short header chip. The user's selections plus free-text notes are returned as structured annotations. Richer than a plain clarify tool — preview content enables side-by-side comparisons of code snippets or mockups.
items.done: Model can call AskUserQuestion with up to 4 questions. Each question shows options in a tappable list; options with `preview` show a preview pane. User can select + add notes. Returns structured answers.

items.title: Asciicast session recording — record sessions as terminal replay files
items.summary: CC can record sessions to asciicast v2 files (`.cast`) when `CLAUDE_CODE_TERMINAL_RECORDING=1` is set. Files are written buffered to `~/.claude/recordings/<session-id>.cast`, cleaned up on exit. Asciicast format allows replaying the full terminal session in any compatible player (asciinema.org, terminals).
items.done: With `VANTA_TERMINAL_RECORDING=1`, a `.cast` file is written during the session. After exit, the file can be replayed with `asciinema play`.

items.title: Screenshot to clipboard — copy ANSI terminal output as PNG image
items.summary: CC can convert ANSI-colored terminal text to PNG (via a pure-TS bitmap renderer — no WASM or system fonts) and copy it to the clipboard. `copyAnsiToClipboard()` supports macOS (pbcopy), Linux (xclip/xsel), and Windows. Used for sharing terminal output as images.
items.done: A `/screenshot` or export action produces a PNG of the current terminal output and copies it to clipboard.

items.title: PDF reading — extract text from PDFs as context with size/error handling
items.summary: CC can extract text from PDF files as tool context. Handles error cases: empty (no text), too_large (size limits), password_protected, corrupted, unknown. Uses pdftotext/poppler if available, with fallback. Output is persisted to tool-results dir for large PDFs. Integrates with FileReadTool so the model can `read` a PDF path.
items.done: `read_file('report.pdf')` returns extracted text. Password-protected PDFs return a helpful error. Files over the size limit return a partial extract with a note.

items.title: In-TUI agent editor — edit agent tools, color, model, and file from /agents menu
items.summary: CC's `/agents` menu has an in-TUI editor for custom agents: choose allowed tools via ToolSelector, assign a color via ColorPicker, change the model via ModelSelector, or open the agent's markdown file in the external editor. Saves changes to the agent file. Only editable for custom (non-built-in, non-plugin) agents.
items.done: `/agents` → select a custom agent → Edit → change tools/model/color or open in VS Code. Changes persist to `~/.claude/agents/<name>.md`.

items.title: Agent memory snapshot — update dialog after custom agent sessions
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `AGENT_MEMORY_SNAPSHOT`) shows a `launchSnapshotUpdateDialog` after sessions using custom agents with `pendingSnapshotUpdate`. Lets the user review and approve memory updates the agent learned during the session before they're persisted to the agent definition.
items.done: After a custom agent session ends with new memory, a dialog asks to confirm the learned facts. Approved facts update the agent's memory file.

items.title: Message actions panel — shift+up keybinding to act on previous messages
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `MESSAGE_ACTIONS`) adds a `shift+up` keybinding mapped to `chat:messageActions` and a `MessageActions` context with `messageActions:prev/next` navigation. Enables a contextual action panel over previous messages: copy, retry, branch, etc.
items.done: Pressing shift+up over a previous message opens the actions panel with options to copy, retry from that point, or branch the conversation.

items.title: Shift-select + clipboard ops in the composer
items.summary: A selection model (anchor+cursor) + the shift-select shortcuts: Shift+arrows (char/line), Shift+Alt+arrows (word), Shift+Cmd+arrows (to line start/end), Shift+Home/End, Cmd+A select-all; typing/cut replaces the selection; copy (Cmd+C) reads it, cut (Cmd+X), paste (Cmd+V). Prerequisite for the selection-based shortcuts in TUI-KEYS. Builds on the existing /copy + /paste. docs/tui-keybindings.md.
items.done: Shift+arrow/word/line extends a visible selection in the composer; Cmd+A selects all; Cmd+C/X/V copy/cut/paste operate on the selection; typing over a selection replaces it.

items.title: Text selection + clipboard in TUI
items.summary: CC's ink layer has selection.ts + use-selection.ts + searchHighlight.ts enabling range-based text selection within the transcript and copy to clipboard. Distinct from MessageSelector (which selects whole messages); this selects arbitrary text spans. Vanta has no TUI text selection.
items.done: Shift+arrow extends a text selection in the transcript; Ctrl+C copies the selection to clipboard; a visual highlight shows the selected range; selection clears on next keystroke.

items.title: Settings panel UI — Config, Status, Usage tabs
items.summary: CC's Settings UI has four tabs: Config (all settings as an editable form), Status (connection status for providers/MCP/IDE), Usage (token/cost breakdown per session and total). Vanta has /config (CC-CONFIG next) but no full panel UI. The Settings panel makes settings discoverable without editing JSON.
items.done: /settings opens the panel; Config tab lists all settings with types + current values editable inline; Status tab shows provider/MCP/IDE connection states; Usage tab shows token + cost charts.

items.title: Vim operator system — text objects, find motions, counts, dot-repeat, yank register
items.summary: Full vim operator system beyond basic hjkl: operators (d/c/y/>/< with motion), text objects (iw/aw/i''/a''/i(/a(/i[/a[/i{/a{), find motions (f/F/t/T with ; repeat), count prefix (3w, 2dd), dot-repeat (.), yank register, r replace, linewise vs characterwise resolution. Extends CC-VIM-MODE.
items.done: Operators with motions and counts work (3dw deletes 3 words); text objects work (ci' changes inside quotes); find motions work (f( moves to next '('); dot-repeat re-applies last change; yank register shared across d/c/y.

items.title: Bounded agent-role council (CEO/CTO/COO/.../Reflection)
items.summary: wants3 Phase 2. A reusable library of named, bounded agent roles on top of O2 swarms + O1 agent-chosen-model + WORKFLOWS: Coordinator + CEO/CTO/COO/CFO/Sales/Marketing/Product/Research/Creative-Director/Personal-Life/Learning/Protection/Home/Reflection. Each role carries a fixed brief -- role, limited context, allowed tools, output format, memory boundary, success condition, report-back, and NO risky action without approval (kernel-gated). Adds 'vanta agents run "goal"' that routes a goal to the right roles and synthesizes. Creative-Director pairs with TASTE-ENGINE; Reflection with MEM-CURATOR; Protection with PROTECTION-AGENT.
items.done: 'vanta agents run' spawns 2+ named role-agents with enforced tool/memory boundaries and returns a synthesized, kernel-approved plan.

items.title: WhatsApp adapter (Node subprocess bridge; Business-API alt)
items.summary: WhatsApp PlatformAdapter via a Node subprocess bridge (subprocess bridge pattern): Vanta spawns a small Baileys/whatsapp-web.js worker, pairs by QR (creds in ~/.vanta/whatsapp/, gitignored), talks over localhost HTTP (GET /messages poll, POST send), health-checks + restarts the bridge. UNOFFICIAL -> account ban risk + breaks on protocol changes; WhatsApp Business API (Meta-verified) is the ToS-safe alternative (captured as 'official' mode). Bridge dep installs to ~/.vanta, never the repo (PLUGIN-SYSTEM). Build last.
items.done: After a QR pair, 'vanta gateway' receives a WhatsApp message via the Node bridge and replies; bridge spawn/health/restart handled; pure parse tested offline; risk + Business-API alt documented.

items.title: Query decomposition — parallel research with tool transparency
items.summary: Steal rocket.new Solve's engine: decompose a research objective into independent sub-queries, run them as parallel subagents (Vanta has delegate/swarm), and surface WHICH tools each ran + what it found. The transparent-research substrate under SOLUTIONING-MODE; serves 'report only verified output.'
items.done: A research goal fans out into labeled parallel sub-queries and returns a synthesis that shows, per dimension, which tools ran and what each found.

items.title: Clarity gate — score instruction, auto-clarify below threshold
items.summary: rocket.new 'Prompt Intelligence', Vanta-native: score an instruction's clarity BEFORE assess(), and when below threshold fire the existing clarify tool with a few TARGETED questions instead of guessing. Direct expression of the north star (know the goal before picking a tool). Builds on clarify + complexity-gate + the prompt's ambiguity scoring.
items.done: A vague instruction is scored and triggers 1-3 targeted clarifying questions before any tool runs; a clear one passes straight through.

items.title: Launchpad — ground a task in real source docs before acting
items.summary: rocket.new 'Launchpad', Vanta-native: before generating, ground the task in real sources (a PRD/ROADMAP, a ticket, a local file/@-ref) and extract scope/entities FIRST — 'without one it launches from thin air.' Maps to Vanta reading goal+repo docs before tool selection.
items.done: A task can be seeded from a named source doc; Vanta extracts scope/entities and references them in its plan before the first tool call.

items.title: Change-watchers for repos/issues/email/calendar
items.summary: Scheduled watchers that monitor repos/issues/emails/calendar for important changes and draft actions, awaiting approval where risk is high. Extends gateway (cron+webhook+platform poll). — the 'watch what's next' half of the SOLUTIONING-MODE loop (research→decide→ship→watch, one shared brain).
items.done: Vanta surfaces an important change and a drafted response without being asked, gated by approval on risk.

items.title: --plugin-url / --plugin-dir — load plugins from URL or local path
items.summary: CC's `--plugin-url <url>` fetches a plugin .zip from a URL and loads it; `--plugin-dir <path>` loads from a local directory or .zip. Vanta's plugin system (PLUGIN-SYSTEM shipped) installs from git; there's no direct URL or local-path load path.
items.done: `--plugin-url <url>` downloads, verifies, and loads a plugin .zip at session start; `--plugin-dir <path>` loads a local dir/.zip; both trigger the same plugin activation path as `vanta plugin install`.

items.title: MCP OAuth auth tool — authenticate MCP servers inline
items.summary: CC has McpAuthTool: when an MCP server requires OAuth, a pseudo-tool surfaces in place of its real tools so the agent can kick off the auth flow on the user's behalf (returns an auth URL). Vanta's MCP client has no auth handling — unauthenticated servers just fail silently. Add MCP OAuth flow: detect 401 from MCP server, surface mcp_auth tool with auth URL, complete flow and reconnect.
items.done: When an MCP server needs OAuth, `mcp_auth` surfaces an auth URL; after the user completes auth the server reconnects and its tools become available.

items.title: claude-code-hint protocol — subprocess plugin recommendations via stderr tag
items.summary: CLIs and SDKs running under CC can emit a self-closing `<claude-code-hint v='1' type='plugin' name='...' marketplace='...' />` tag to stderr. CC strips it before the model sees it, and surfaces an install prompt to the user. Lets external tools request plugin installs without inference — a documented vendor-facing spec (`docs/claude-code-hints.md`).
items.done: A subprocess emitting `<claude-code-hint type='plugin' name='pylsp' marketplace='agent-skills' />` causes Vanta to show 'Install pylsp plugin?' prompt after the tool call.

items.title: AI agent generator — LLM-powered creation of agent definitions from description
items.summary: CC can generate a new agent definition (identifier, whenToUse, systemPrompt) by prompting a fast model with the user's description and repo context. The generated agent is written to `~/.claude/agents/<identifier>.md`. Triggered from the `/agents` new-agent flow. Integrates with auto-memory if enabled.
items.done: In `/agents`, choosing 'Create new agent' and typing a description generates a ready-to-use agent file with a system prompt tailored to the repo context.

items.title: `claude plugin` CLI — install/uninstall/enable/disable/update plugins from terminal
items.summary: CC has a `claude plugin` subcommand with operations: `install <name@marketplace>`, `uninstall <name>`, `enable <name>`, `disable <name>`, `update [name]`. Pure library functions in `pluginOperations.ts` power both the CLI and the interactive ManagePlugins.tsx UI. Non-interactive mode (CI/scripts) supported.
items.done: `claude plugin install pylsp@agent-skills` installs and activates the LSP plugin. `claude plugin disable pylsp` disables it without removing. `claude plugin update` updates all.

items.title: MCP skills — MCP servers can provide slash commands and skills
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `MCP_SKILLS`) allows MCP servers to provide skills/slash commands. `fetchMcpSkillsForClient` fetches skills from connected MCP servers and registers them alongside plugin-provided skills. Commands from MCP servers appear in the slash palette.
items.done: An MCP server that declares skills has those skills appear in `/skills`. Running `/skill-name` invokes the MCP server's skill handler.

items.title: Skill generator skill — built-in skill for creating new skills from description
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `RUN_SKILL_GENERATOR`) registers `registerRunSkillGeneratorSkill` as a bundled skill. Allows the agent to generate new skill definitions from a natural language description, bootstrapping the skill system by dogfooding it.
items.done: Using the skill generator skill with a description produces a new `.claude/skills/` file wired to the skill registry.

items.title: Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop — one-click import of Claude Desktop MCP config
items.summary: CC has a `MCPServerDesktopImportDialog` that reads Claude Desktop's MCP server configuration and imports selected servers into the current CC project config. Eliminates re-entering MCP server details for users who already have them configured in Claude Desktop.
items.done: Running `claude mcp add --from-claude-desktop` or opening the dialog lists all servers from `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`. Selecting them adds them to `.mcp.json`.

items.title: Plugin background monitors — auto-armed monitoring scripts per session
items.summary: CC (v2.1.105) adds a `monitors` key to plugin manifests (under `experimental`). Monitor scripts are auto-armed at session start or on demand, running in the background and emitting events the agent can react to.
items.done: A plugin's `monitors` section starts a filesystem watcher. When a watched file changes, the agent receives an event and acts without a user prompt.

items.title: --max-budget-usd — per-session spend cap
items.summary: CC's `--max-budget-usd` stops the agent loop when the session's accumulated LLM spend exceeds the threshold. Vanta tracks cost (COST-VISIBLE) but has no hard spend cap that halts execution.
items.done: `--max-budget-usd <n>` (and `VANTA_MAX_BUDGET_USD` env) stops the agent with a cost-exceeded message when spend reaches the limit; the current spend is shown in the prompt before stopping.

items.title: --fork-session — create a new session ID on resume
items.summary: CC's `--fork-session` creates a fresh session ID when resuming a prior session, keeping the original intact for later comparison or fallback. Vanta's resume overwrites the active session.
items.done: `--fork-session` combined with `--resume` creates a new session file seeded with the prior history; `vanta sessions list` shows both the original and the fork.

items.title: vanta project purge — delete local project state
items.summary: CC's `claude project purge [path]` deletes all local project state (sessions, auto-memory, checkpoints) for a given project, with `--dry-run`, `-y`, `-i` (interactive), and `--all` flags. Vanta has no cleanup command; stale state accumulates.
items.done: `vanta project purge [path]` lists and deletes project-scoped state (sessions + memory + .vanta/ data) with dry-run by default; `--yes` skips confirmation; `--all` purges all known projects.

items.title: --safe-mode + --bare — troubleshooting isolation modes
items.summary: CC's `--safe-mode` disables all customizations (plugins, hooks, skills, CLAUDE.md) for clean troubleshooting; `--bare` skips auto-discovery (no project CLAUDE.md, no MCP, no skills). Essential for debugging broken configurations.
items.done: `--safe-mode` runs without any user customizations (hooks/skills/plugins/rules are skipped); `--bare` skips auto-discovery; both print a banner confirming the isolation level.

items.title: --init / --init-only / --maintenance — lifecycle hook flags
items.summary: CC's `--init` runs Setup hooks before the session; `--init-only` runs Setup + SessionStart then exits (for one-time environment prep); `--maintenance` runs Setup with a maintenance matcher (for periodic housekeeping crons). Vanta has no equivalent lifecycle bootstrap flags.
items.done: `--init` triggers Setup hooks before the first turn; `--init-only` runs Setup + SessionStart then exits 0; `--maintenance` adds a `maintenance: true` matcher context to Setup hooks.

items.title: Git settings parity — attribution, PR template, gitignore
items.summary: CC settings: attribution (customize Co-Authored-By in commits), includeGitInstructions (include git best-practice instructions in system prompt), prUrlTemplate (PR link format for the status line), respectGitignore (control file picker gitignore behavior). Vanta commits but has no commit attribution settings.
items.done: All four git settings are recognized and enforced; `attribution` overrides the default Co-Authored-By line; `prUrlTemplate` substitutes `{PR}` with the PR number in the status footer.

items.title: CLAUDE.md / VANTA.md @-import syntax — include external files by reference
items.summary: CC supports `@path/to/file` inside CLAUDE.md to inline another file's contents at load time, with up to 4-hop recursion. Enables shared rule libraries, team-wide standards, and composable instruction sets. Vanta loads VANTA.md verbatim; no import syntax.
items.done: `@<path>` inside VANTA.md (or rules files) inlines the referenced file at session start; absolute and relative paths work; recursion is capped at 4 hops; circular imports are detected and skipped.

items.title: --from-pr — resume sessions linked to a specific pull request
items.summary: CC's `--from-pr <url|number>` resumes all sessions previously associated with a given PR, enabling continuity across review cycles. Vanta's session resume is by session ID only; no PR linking.
items.done: `--from-pr <url|number>` loads the session(s) previously tagged with that PR; `vanta sessions list --pr <n>` shows PR-linked sessions; sessions auto-tag when `vanta pr-comments` runs.

items.title: Session cleanup period — cleanupPeriodDays setting
items.summary: CC's `cleanupPeriodDays` (default 30) auto-expires sessions older than N days on startup. Vanta sessions accumulate indefinitely; there's no TTL or auto-purge.
items.done: `cleanupPeriodDays` in settings (default 30) prunes sessions older than N days on session start; `vanta sessions prune --older-than <days>` runs it manually; a summary of pruned sessions is logged.

items.title: skipWebFetchPreflight — bypass web fetch domain safety check
items.summary: CC's `skipWebFetchPreflight` setting skips the domain safety preflight check before web_fetch calls (useful in air-gapped or fully-trusted environments). Vanta's web_fetch has an allowed-domain gate; no setting to disable the preflight.
items.done: `skipWebFetchPreflight: true` in settings bypasses the domain approval check in web_fetch; a warning is printed at session start when enabled; default is false.

items.title: /tasks slash command — list and manage background tasks from the REPL
items.summary: CC's `/tasks` slash command lists all running background tasks with status, attach/stop controls. Vanta can run background tasks but has no slash command to inspect or manage them mid-session.
items.done: `/tasks` lists all background tasks with their status, output tail, and attach/stop/logs shortcut; keyboard shortcuts follow CC conventions.

items.title: /describe slash command — generate file and directory descriptions
items.summary: CC's `/describe` generates structured descriptions for files and directories, useful for auto-populating CLAUDE.md, onboarding docs, and code search indexes. No equivalent in Vanta.
items.done: `/describe [path]` generates a concise description of the file or directory and optionally appends it to VANTA.md or a designated descriptions file.

items.title: /stop slash command — gracefully stop the current agent turn
items.summary: CC's `/stop` command gracefully halts the current turn mid-execution (as opposed to Esc which aborts immediately). Vanta has Esc-abort; `/stop` would issue a soft stop that lets the current tool call finish before halting.
items.done: `/stop` sends a stop signal; the agent finishes the current tool call, then exits the turn cleanly and presents a summary of what was completed.

items.title: PR status polling — live review state during session
items.summary: Polls `gh` every 60 s during an active session to show PR review state (approved / changes-requested / pending) for the current branch. Backs off after 60 min idle; self-disables if a fetch exceeds 4 s.
items.done: PR number, URL, and review state visible in session status; polling pauses when session is idle >60 min; disabled if gh is slow.

items.title: /loop — recurring cron task from natural-language interval
items.summary: Bundled /loop skill that parses a natural-language interval (10m, 30m, 2h, 1d) + prompt, then creates a CronCreate task to run it recurrently. Handles 'every 20m' prose, defaults to 10m. Uses the Kairos cron engine.
items.done: '/loop 30m check the deploy' creates a cron task that fires every 30 min; '/loop /babysit-prs' defaults to 10m; interval parsed from the end of the string if prose format.

items.title: macOS caffeinate — prevent system sleep during long operations
items.summary: Uses macOS `caffeinate` to prevent idle sleep while CC is running API requests or tools. Self-healing: 5-minute timeout + 4-minute restart so orphaned caffeinate exits on SIGKILL. No-op on non-macOS. Ref-counted (start/stop) so multiple callers share one process.
items.done: Mac stays awake during long API calls and tool execution; caffeinate exits cleanly on process exit or after 5min idle; no-op on Linux/Windows.

items.title: ConfigTool — agent reads and updates user settings during session
items.summary: Agent-accessible tool to read the current value of a setting, list valid options, get the config file path, and update supported settings (Global config + settings.json) during a session. Enables the agent to configure itself (e.g., enable verbose mode, set model).
items.done: Agent can call ConfigTool to read/update settings without user manual edit; unsupported settings rejected; changes persisted to the appropriate config file.

items.title: ToolSearchTool — on-demand deferred tool loading by keyword
items.summary: Agent-accessible ToolSearch tool: loads deferred tool schemas on demand by keyword query or `select:<name>` for direct selection. Keeps tool context lean by not loading all tool schemas upfront. Agent first discovers that a tool exists (from index), then fetches its full schema when needed.
items.done: Deferred tools appear as names-only in the context; calling ToolSearch loads their full schema; agent can then call them; `select:ToolName` fetches by exact name.

items.title: 1M context upgrade prompt — suggest extended-context model when approaching limit
items.summary: When approaching the context window limit and the user has access to a 1M-context model variant (Opus 1M, Sonnet 1M), CC surfaces a tip or warning suggesting the upgrade with a 5× multiplier message. Context window check gated on claude.ai subscription access.
items.done: Warning shows when context is near limit and 1M variant available; tip suggests upgrade proactively; message distinguishes warning vs tip contexts; hidden when already on 1M model.

items.title: macOS Keychain credential storage — secure OAuth token persistence
items.summary: Uses macOS `security` CLI to read/write OAuth tokens and API keys to the system Keychain. TTL-cached reads (avoid repeated `security` subprocess overhead). Fallback to plain-text ~/.claude/credentials.json on failure. Handles 4096-byte stdin limit of the security tool.
items.done: OAuth tokens stored in Keychain on macOS; reads are cached for TTL ms; plain-text fallback on Linux/Windows; credentials.json is the fallback path.

items.title: Dangerous bash patterns — strip interpreter allow-rules at auto-mode entry
items.summary: A curated list of code-execution interpreters (python, node, deno, tsx, ruby, perl, php, lua, npx, bunx, bash, etc.) that are stripped from allow-rules when entering auto-mode. Prevents `Bash(python:*)` rules from bypassing the classifier. Cross-platform list shared between Bash and PowerShell checks.
items.done: Entering auto-mode strips interpreter-wildcard allow-rules; non-interpreter rules are preserved; a stripped-rules notification is shown; the list covers all common code-exec entry points.

items.title: Conversation history with large-paste external storage
items.summary: CC stores conversation history (max 100 entries) in ~/.claude/history including paste contents. Large pastes are stored externally via content hash (PasteStore) rather than inline, keeping the history file small. Supports both text and image paste history. Read with lock for concurrent access safety.
items.done: History includes pasted text/images; large pastes stored externally by hash; history file stays compact; concurrent readers don't corrupt entries.

items.title: Agent scratchpad directory — gated workspace for temporary files
items.summary: ENABLE_SCRATCHPAD gate creates a scoped temp directory for agent use. Files written to the scratchpad don't affect the project. Coordinator workers can use it as a shared workspace for intermediate results without polluting the repo.
items.done: With ENABLE_SCRATCHPAD set, agent has a temp dir it can write to freely; dir is cleaned on session end; not accessible from outside the scratchpad scope.

items.title: SSRF guard for HTTP hooks — block private/cloud-metadata IPs
items.summary: HTTP hooks resolve target IPs before connecting and block private ranges (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x), CGNAT (100.64.x), cloud metadata (169.254.x), IPv6 unique-local (fc00::/7, fe80::/10). Loopback (127.x, ::1) explicitly ALLOWED for local dev policy servers. Bypassed when proxy in use.
items.done: HTTP hook to 169.254.169.254 is blocked; hook to 127.0.0.1 is allowed; private IPs blocked; DNS resolution happens before connection attempt.

items.title: FileChanged + CwdChanged hook events — file watcher triggers
items.summary: Chokidar file watcher fires FileChanged hooks when project files change and CwdChanged hooks when the working directory changes. CwdChanged also clears cached env files. Watcher only starts when hooks are configured for these events.
items.done: Writing a file triggers FileChanged hook if configured; changing cwd triggers CwdChanged + env cache clear; no watcher overhead when hooks not configured.

items.title: Teammate default model — Opus 4.6 for swarm agents, provider-aware
items.summary: When the user hasn't set `teammateDefaultModel` in /config, spawned swarm agents use Opus 4.6 as the default model. Provider-aware: Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry users get the correct model ID for their provider.
items.done: Spawned teammates default to Opus 4.6; /config teammateDefaultModel overrides it; Bedrock users get the Bedrock Opus 4.6 model ID, not the direct API one.

items.title: /security-review — built-in security audit of current branch changes
items.summary: Bundled /security-review prompt skill: runs git status/log/diff, then uses an LLM to perform a focused security review of the pending changes. Scoped to git-diff tools only. Defined as an inline markdown skill with frontmatter allowed-tools.
items.done: Running /security-review audits the current branch diff for security issues; output is structured finding report; only git-read tools permitted during review.

items.title: Cyber risk instruction — system prompt section for security task safety
items.summary: A carefully crafted system prompt section (owned by Safeguards team) injected when handling security-related requests. Defines the boundary between acceptable defensive security assistance (pentesting, CTF) and harmful offensive activities. Cannot be modified without team review.
items.done: Security requests get the cyber risk instruction in context; instruction guides appropriate vs. inappropriate security assistance; unchanged from upstream.

items.title: Shell output size limits — 30K default, 150K cap, BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH override
items.summary: Shell tool outputs are capped at 30K chars by default (BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_DEFAULT), with a hard upper limit of 150K (BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_UPPER_LIMIT). Override via BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH env var (clamped to valid range). Prevents large outputs from flooding context.
items.done: Commands producing >30K output are truncated with a message; BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH=50000 expands the limit; values above 150K are clamped.

items.title: Settings live reload — detect and apply settings changes without restart
items.summary: changeDetector watches settings.json for changes (via file hash); applySettingsChange applies them live to the running session. internalWrites.ts marks writes made by CC itself to avoid false-positive change notifications. Settings panel shows changes in real time.
items.done: Editing settings.json in a text editor reflects in the TUI within seconds; CC-initiated writes (e.g. /config) don't trigger false 'settings changed' notifications.

items.title: Cross-project session resume — resume sessions from different projects
items.summary: When resuming a session that was created in a different project root, CC detects the mismatch via checkCrossProjectResume and warns the user. The session can still resume in the current project context. Prevents silent context confusion.
items.done: Resuming a session from /project-a while in /project-b shows a warning; user can proceed or cancel; file paths in the session are noted as potentially wrong.

items.title: Commit attribution — track CC-modified files for git co-authorship
items.summary: CC tracks which files it modifies across a session using content hashes and session IDs. Attribution snapshots feed into git commit co-authorship metadata. Excludes generated files. Tracks git remote URL for cross-repo attribution. Used to attribute changes to the AI assistant in commit messages.
items.done: Files edited by CC tracked with hashes; git commits include proper Co-Authored-By attribution; generated files excluded; remote URL included in attribution metadata.

items.title: Concurrent session management — track multiple running CC instances
items.summary: CC tracks all running instances (session ID → PID → timestamp). On startup, dead sessions are cleaned up. Session switching triggers onSessionSwitch callbacks. Prevents stale session locks. Cross-platform process health check.
items.done: Multiple CC instances run without conflicts; dead session entries cleaned on startup; isProcessRunning checks prevent orphaned state; session IDs unique.

items.title: Prompt hook type — hooks backed by a single LLM query
items.summary: CC supports a 'prompt' hook type: each hook event fires a single non-streaming LLM query (small/fast model). The hook receives the event JSON as `$ARGUMENTS` substituted into its prompt, and returns a structured `{decision, reason}` response. Lighter-weight than agent hooks — useful for classification/labeling tasks without multi-turn logic.
items.done: A hook configured with `type: prompt` fires one LLM call per event. Returns a structured `{decision: 'block'|'allow', reason}` that determines hook behavior.

items.title: Frontmatter hooks — agents and skills declare hooks in YAML frontmatter
items.summary: CC agents and skills can declare hook configurations in their YAML frontmatter (`hooks:` key). These hooks are automatically registered when the agent/skill activates and deregistered when it exits. Sub-agent Stop hooks are converted to SubagentStop events. Session hooks share the same lifecycle as the agent.
