items.done: A skill with a `hooks.PreToolUse` frontmatter entry runs that hook for every tool call while the skill is active. The hook is removed when the skill session ends.

items.title: Once-only hooks — `once: true` hooks auto-deregister after first fire
items.summary: CC skill hooks support a `once: true` flag that causes the hook to automatically deregister itself after its first successful execution. Useful for one-shot setup hooks (e.g. 'on the first tool call after this skill activates, do X') without polluting subsequent tool calls with unnecessary hook processing.
items.done: A skill hook with `once: true` fires exactly once, then disappears from the hook registry. Subsequent tool calls in the same session don't trigger it.

items.title: Hook exit code semantics — exit 0 silent, exit 2 blocks + shows model, other shows user
items.summary: CC defines specific semantics for hook exit codes: exit 0 = success (stdout/stderr not shown), exit 2 = show stderr to model AND block the tool call (the model sees the message and can change strategy), any other non-zero = show stderr to user only but continue with the tool call. This gives hook authors precise control over whether the model or user sees the hook's output.
items.done: A PreToolUse hook returning exit 2 with a message causes the tool call to be blocked and the model receives the hook's stderr as a constraint.

items.title: CLI auto-updater — version notification + background update with semver comparison
items.summary: CC's AutoUpdater/AutoUpdaterWrapper components check for new CLI versions and notify the user via a bottom-right notification. `useUpdateNotification` tracks the last-notified semver to avoid repeat notifications for the same version. The update is downloaded in the background and the user is prompted to restart.
items.done: When a new version is available, a notification appears in the corner. User can dismiss or restart to apply the update.

items.title: Deferred SessionStart hooks — async hook execution without blocking REPL startup
items.summary: CC's `useDeferredHookMessages` runs SessionStart hooks asynchronously so the REPL renders immediately (~500ms savings). Hook messages are stored in a pending promise and merged into the conversation before the first API request (ensuring the model always sees hook context). The deferred pattern avoids REPL startup blocking on slow hooks.
items.done: SessionStart hooks with slow shell commands (~500ms+) don't delay the initial REPL render. Their output appears as the first hook attachment when the user sends their first message.

items.title: Memory usage monitor — high/critical heap warnings at 1.5GB / 2.5GB
items.summary: CC polls Node.js heap usage every 10 seconds via `useMemoryUsage`. At 1.5GB (high) or 2.5GB (critical) thresholds, a visible warning appears so the user can /compact or restart before hitting an OOM crash. Returns null while normal so there's no UI noise in typical sessions.
items.done: When heap reaches 1.5GB, a warning appears in the status area. At 2.5GB it escalates to critical with a recommendation to /compact.

items.title: Token budget directives — parse +500k / use 2M tokens from user messages
items.summary: CC parses token budget directives from user messages: shorthand at start/end (`+500k`, `+2M`) and verbose anywhere (`use 500k tokens`, `spend 2M tokens`). Parsed budgets are passed to workflow/agent execution as spending caps. Supports k/M/B multipliers with decimal values.
items.done: User types 'Review all these files +500k' — the session sets a 500,000-token budget ceiling. `budget.total` and `budget.remaining()` are available to orchestration code.

items.title: Parallel tool execution — concurrent read-only tool calls with configurable cap
items.summary: CC's tool orchestration batches tool calls marked `isConcurrencySafe` and runs them concurrently (up to `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY`, default 10). Read-only operations (Glob, Grep, Read, WebSearch, WebFetch) run in parallel; write/mutating tools run sequentially. Speeds up multi-file reads and search-heavy turns significantly.
items.done: A turn with 5 parallel Glob calls completes in ~200ms instead of ~1s. `VANTA_MAX_TOOL_CONCURRENCY=10` controls the cap.

items.title: VCR record/replay — record API request/response cassettes for test replay
items.summary: CC has a VCR (cassette recorder) mode that records all API requests and responses to disk when `FORCE_VCR=1` (for ants) or in `NODE_ENV=test`. Cassettes are keyed by content hash; on replay, matching requests return cached responses instantly without hitting the API. Used in integration tests and for debugging.
items.done: `VANTA_VCR=1` records a session's API calls to `.vanta/cassettes/`. Re-running with the same inputs replays from cassette without network calls.

items.title: API preconnect — pre-warm TCP+TLS during startup for 100-200ms first-request savings
items.summary: CC fires a fire-and-forget fetch to the Anthropic API endpoint during init (after CA certs and proxy are configured), warming the TCP+TLS connection pool before the first real request. Bun's keep-alive pool reuses the connection. In `-p` mode this saves ~100-200ms; in interactive mode the savings are the full 'user is typing' window. Runs AFTER settings.json env vars are applied so proxy/mTLS settings are respected.
items.done: First API request in a session completes 100-200ms faster because the connection was pre-warmed during startup.

items.title: Adaptive thinking mode — let model self-budget tokens + `ultrathink` max-budget keyword
items.summary: CC supports three thinking modes: `adaptive` (model decides how much thinking budget to use), `enabled` with a fixed `budgetTokens` value, and `disabled`. The `ultrathink` keyword in a prompt triggers maximum thinking budget (feature-gated). Users control mode via /think toggle or settings. Adaptive mode avoids token waste on simple tasks while allowing deep reasoning on complex ones.
items.done: `/think` cycles through disabled → adaptive → max. Typing 'ultrathink' in a prompt activates maximum extended thinking for that turn only.

items.title: Hook timing indicator — show elapsed time when hooks take >500ms
items.summary: CC shows a timing indicator in the hook progress display when a hook takes longer than `HOOK_TIMING_DISPLAY_THRESHOLD_MS` (500ms). Helps users identify slow hooks that are delaying tool execution, with elapsed time shown as e.g. '1.2s' in the hook progress pill.
items.done: A PreToolUse hook that takes 800ms shows '800ms' next to the hook name in the tool call progress display.

items.title: Undercover mode — safety strip for public/open-source repo contributions
items.summary: CC's 'undercover mode' activates when working in public/external repos (auto-detects unless repo is in internal allowlist, or `CLAUDE_CODE_UNDERCOVER=1`). When active: adds safety instructions to commit/PR prompts to avoid leaking internal codenames or Anthropic-internal context, and strips all commit attribution. The model is not told its own identity.
items.done: Working in a public GitHub repo, CC adds a safety caveat to generated commit messages and omits model attribution. `VANTA_UNDERCOVER=1` forces it on.

items.title: /env — set session-scoped environment variables for child processes
items.summary: CC has an `/env` command that sets session-scoped environment variables. These are stored in `sessionEnvVars` and applied only to spawned child processes (via the bash provider env overrides) — NOT to the REPL process itself. Useful for temporarily setting API keys, feature flags, or tool configuration without modifying shell profile.
items.done: `/env FOO=bar` sets FOO for subsequent bash tool calls in the session. `/env` with no args shows current session env vars. Vars are cleared on session end.

items.title: Privacy levels — default/no-telemetry/essential-traffic to control network traffic
items.summary: CC supports three privacy levels (ordered by restrictiveness): `default` (everything enabled), `no-telemetry` (disables analytics/telemetry but other network traffic allowed), and `essential-traffic` (only API calls — no telemetry, no update checks, no marketplace refreshes, no bootstrap). Configurable via settings or `CLAUDE_CODE_PRIVACY_LEVEL`.
items.done: `VANTA_PRIVACY=no-telemetry` disables all usage tracking. `essential-traffic` mode blocks all non-API network calls for air-gapped environments.

items.title: Interleaved thinking — thinking tokens between tool calls, not just before response
items.summary: CC supports an `interleaved_thinking` capability mode where extended thinking tokens are interleaved between tool calls, not just in the initial response block. This allows the model to reason after each tool result before deciding the next action. Listed alongside `thinking`, `adaptive_thinking`, `effort`, and `max_effort` as a model capability override.
items.done: With interleaved thinking enabled, thinking blocks appear in the transcript after each tool result as the model reasons about next steps.

items.title: Model tier env overrides — pin opus/sonnet/haiku to specific model versions
items.summary: CC reads `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` env vars to pin each model tier to a specific version. Combined with `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL_SUPPORTED_CAPABILITIES` to declare what capabilities the override model supports. Useful for enterprise deployments pinning to a specific API version.
items.done: `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6` pins the sonnet alias to that exact version. `/model` and startup banner reflect the override.

items.title: Shadowed permission rule detection — warn about unreachable allow/deny rules
items.summary: CC's `detectUnreachableRules()` analyzes the permission rule set and identifies rules that will never match because a broader rule with the same tool already shadows them. Surfaced as a `ContextWarning` in `/doctor`. Prevents confusion where users add rules that silently have no effect because an existing `Allow Bash(*)` already covers them.
items.done: `/doctor` reports 'Rule Allow Bash(ls) is unreachable — shadowed by Allow Bash(*)'. Helps users maintain a clean permission set.

items.title: Auto tool-search mode — defer tools only when they exceed the context threshold
items.summary: CC supports `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto` (tst-auto mode): tools are only deferred to ToolSearch when their total schema size exceeds `getAutoToolSearchCharThreshold(model)`. Below the threshold, all tools are exposed inline as usual. `auto:50` enables for 50% of sessions for gradual rollout. Prevents unnecessary overhead for simple sessions while still protecting context on complex ones.
items.done: With `VANTA_TOOL_SEARCH=auto`, simple sessions see all tools inline. Sessions with many plugins/MCP tools automatically defer the overflow to ToolSearch.

items.title: `--bare` mode — minimal output format for scripting and pipelines
items.summary: CC supports a `--bare` CLI flag that enables bare mode: minimal output formatting suitable for scripting and pipes. In bare mode, decorative UI elements (spinners, boxes, colors, status line) are stripped — only the essential response text is output. Complements `--print` / non-interactive mode.
items.done: `claude --bare 'summarize this file' < file.txt` outputs only the summary text, no terminal UI chrome. Suitable for shell pipelines.

items.title: Prompt cache break detection — detect invalidation, trigger microcompact cleanup
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `PROMPT_CACHE_BREAK_DETECTION`) detects when the Anthropic API prompt cache is invalidated (cache miss after a hit sequence). On detection, triggers cleanup: subagents are notified via `notifyCompaction`, and /compact can respond reactively. Prevents unnecessary re-billing from repeated cache misses. Feature flag: `BREAK_CACHE_COMMAND`.
items.done: When the cache breaks mid-session, a microcompact runs to shrink the prompt before the next request, avoiding a large uncached prefix resend.

items.title: --dump-system-prompt — CLI flag to print the assembled system prompt and exit
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `DUMP_SYSTEM_PROMPT`) adds a `--dump-system-prompt` flag. Running CC with this flag assembles the full system prompt (including operator/user layers, tool descriptions, context attachments) and prints it to stdout without starting a session.
items.done: Running `claude --dump-system-prompt` prints the full 3-tier system prompt to stdout. Useful for debugging operator prompt injection.

items.title: `/cd` — change working directory mid-session without breaking prompt cache
items.summary: CC (v2.1.169) adds `/cd <path>` to move the active session to a new directory. Unlike shell `cd`, it updates CC's root context and tool paths without invalidating the prompt cache, preserving accumulated context.
items.done: Running `/cd ~/other-project` mid-session switches tool resolution to the new root. The prompt cache remains intact.

items.title: `PermissionDenied` hook — fire on auto-mode classifier denials with retry option
items.summary: CC (v2.1.89) adds a `PermissionDenied` hook that fires after auto-mode classifier denials. Returning `{retry: true}` tells Claude to try an alternative approach instead of stopping. Enables operator-controlled recovery from permission blocks.
items.done: A PermissionDenied hook logs the denied action and returns `{retry: true}`. Claude rewrites the command to avoid the denied pattern.

items.title: Hook exec form — `args: string[]` spawns hook directly without a shell
items.summary: CC (v2.1.139) adds `args: string[]` to hook config (exec form). The hook binary is spawned directly without a shell, so path placeholders don't need escaping and the command can't be injection-bypassed via shell metacharacters.
items.done: A hook configured as `{command: '/usr/bin/notify', args: ['--title', '$TOOL_NAME']}` spawns directly. No shell wrapping, no injection risk.

items.title: Hook `continueOnBlock` — feed PostToolUse rejection reason back to model
items.summary: CC (v2.1.139) adds `continueOnBlock: true` to `PostToolUse` hook config. When the hook blocks a tool, instead of hard-stopping, the rejection reason is fed back to Claude as a tool result so it can adapt its approach.
items.done: A PostToolUse hook blocks `rm -rf` and sets `continueOnBlock: true`. Claude receives 'destructive rm blocked' as the tool result and chooses a safer alternative.

items.title: `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` — block specific domains under a broader allowlist
items.summary: CC (v2.1.113) adds `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` to deny specific domains even when a broader `allowedDomains` wildcard would otherwise permit them. Enables allowlist + denylist layering: allow `*.api.com` but deny `internal.api.com`.
items.done: With `allowedDomains: ['*.api.com']` and `deniedDomains: ['internal.api.com']`, requests to `internal.api.com` are blocked.

items.title: Ultracode trigger keyword — `ultracode` in prompt launches dynamic workflow
items.summary: CC (v2.1.160) renames the dynamic-workflow prompt keyword from `workflow` to `ultracode`. Typing `ultracode` in a prompt triggers the multi-agent workflow orchestration mode. The old `workflow` keyword no longer triggers a run; asking in natural language still works. A setting in `/config` can disable the keyword trigger.
items.done: Typing 'ultracode refactor this module' in the prompt triggers the workflow engine. The old 'workflow' keyword is inert.

items.title: `/bg` while responding — active response continues in background, not dropped
items.summary: CC (v2.1.153) changes `/bg` behavior during an active response. Instead of dropping the in-progress output, the response continues streaming in the background session. Users can re-attach with `claude agents` to see the result.
items.done: Pressing `/bg` while Claude is responding transfers the live stream to the background. Attaching later shows the completed response.

items.title: Hook `sessionTitle` output — `UserPromptSubmit` hooks can set the session title
items.summary: CC (v2.1.94) adds `hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle` to `UserPromptSubmit` hook output. A hook can return a title string that becomes the session's name. Useful for auto-titling sessions based on the first prompt's content or intent.
items.done: A UserPromptSubmit hook returns `{sessionTitle: 'Fix auth bug #1234'}`. The session is named that without a separate `/rename`.

items.title: grep satisfies read-before-edit — single-file grep counts as a read for Edit tool
items.summary: CC (v2.1.160) makes single-file `grep`/`egrep`/`fgrep` commands satisfy the read-before-edit check. Previously, `head`/`tail` could count; now grep does too. Avoids an extra Read call when the agent has already grepped the file.
items.done: After `grep 'pattern' src/foo.ts`, the Edit tool can modify `src/foo.ts` without a separate Read call.

items.title: `/less-permission-prompts` — scan transcript and propose read-only allow rules
items.summary: CC (v2.1.111) adds a `/less-permission-prompts` skill that scans session transcripts for repeated read-only Bash and MCP tool calls and proposes a prioritized allow list. Reduces friction from over-prompted read operations.
items.done: Running `/less-permission-prompts` analyzes the session transcript and suggests `Bash(git status:*)` and `Bash(cat:*)` as safe auto-allows.

items.title: Shell startup file write prompt — confirm before writing `.zshenv`, `.bash_login`, etc.
items.summary: CC (v2.1.160) adds a confirmation prompt before writing to shell startup files (`.zshenv`, `.zlogin`, `.bash_login`) and `~/.config/git/`. Prevents silent writes that could execute arbitrary code on every shell launch.
items.done: When an operation would modify `.zshenv`, CC shows a confirmation: 'This will run on every shell launch — allow?' Declining leaves the file unchanged.

items.title: `/proactive` alias for `/loop` — proactive autonomy mode discoverability
items.summary: CC (v2.1.105) adds `/proactive` as an alias for `/loop`. Users who search for proactive mode by its conceptual name find it without knowing the `/loop` command name.
items.done: Running `/proactive` starts the same recurring autonomous loop as `/loop`.

items.title: `.husky` in acceptEdits protected dirs — git hooks directory is write-protected
items.summary: CC (v2.1.90) adds `.husky` to the list of protected directories in `acceptEdits` mode. Prevents agents from silently modifying git hook scripts, which could allow arbitrary code execution on commit.
items.done: In acceptEdits mode, attempts to write to `.husky/pre-commit` prompt for explicit confirmation.

items.title: Monitor tool — reactive line-by-line command monitoring
items.summary: CC has a Monitor tool that runs a shell command in the background and fires a callback on each output line, enabling event-driven loops (watch a build, react to log lines, stream deploy output). Vanta runs BG tasks but has no line-by-line reactive tool.
items.done: A `monitor` tool runs a command and routes each output line as a tool result; the agent can act on each line before the process finishes.

items.title: --exec — run a shell command as a background job alongside the session
items.summary: CC's `--exec <cmd>` starts a shell command as a tracked background job concurrent with the agent session (e.g., a dev server, a test watcher). The agent can monitor its output. Vanta has no way to co-launch a background process with a session.
items.done: `--exec <cmd>` starts the command before the first turn; its output is available to the Monitor tool; the process is tracked and killed on session end.

items.title: --exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections — prompt cache optimization
items.summary: CC's `--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections` strips volatile sections (goals, time, current memory) from the cache-prefix portion of the system prompt, dramatically improving LLM cache hit rate on long sessions. Vanta's prompt has stable + volatile tiers (PROMPT-STABILITY) but no per-section exclusion flag.
items.done: `--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections <list>` (or comma-sep env `VANTA_EXCLUDE_PROMPT_SECTIONS`) moves specified tiers out of the stable cache prefix; the cost footer shows estimated cache-hit improvement.

items.title: Memory settings parity — autoMemory, excludes, plans dir
items.summary: CC settings: autoMemoryEnabled (toggle auto memory), autoMemoryDirectory (custom path), claudeMdExcludes (skip specific CLAUDE.md files by glob), plansDirectory (custom plans storage), fileSuggestion (custom file autocomplete source). Vanta's memory is always on with no per-instance controls.
items.done: autoMemoryEnabled, autoMemoryDirectory, claudeMdExcludes (glob array), plansDirectory, and fileSuggestion are all recognized in settings; they take effect at session start without a restart.

items.title: Organize-code tool — agent-callable file decomposition
items.summary: Steal rocket.new '/Organize Code': an agent-callable refactor tool that splits an oversized file into cohesive modules, operationalizing the size gate (lint/size.ts, file<=300/fn<=50) as an ACTION the agent can take, not just a warn-only lint. Behavior-preserving extraction, test-gated. (Exactly the app.tsx decomposition done by hand 2026-06-10.)
items.done: The agent can call organize_code on a file over the size limit and get back a behavior-preserving multi-file split that passes the existing tests.

items.title: Swarm permission routing — workers forward permission asks to lead agent
items.summary: Swarm workers serialise permission-ask actions and forward them to the lead agent via mailbox IPC. Poll every 500 ms for allow/reject response. Schema-validates incoming PermissionUpdate entries; drops malformed ones. Coordinator workers run hooks + classifier sequentially before falling through to the interactive dialog.
items.done: Worker agent permission asks appear in the lead agent's permission queue; lead's allow/reject propagates back to the worker within ~500 ms; malformed entries logged and dropped.

items.title: Team memory directory — shared memory namespace for swarm agents
items.summary: Separate `~/.claude/memory/team/<project>/` directory for shared team context across swarm agents. Combined prompt mode covers both private and team memory types with per-type scope guidance. Path traversal protection on all team memory writes.
items.done: Team memory files written to team directory; combined prompt includes both private and team memories with scope labels; path traversal attempts rejected with PathTraversalError.

items.title: Magic Docs — auto-maintained markdown files updated after each turn
items.summary: Any .md file starting with `# MAGIC DOC: [title]` is automatically updated by a background subagent after each turn that contains tool calls. The doc accumulates learnings from the conversation (architecture, decisions, patterns) without interrupting the main flow. Uses post-sampling hook + sequential update guard.
items.done: Files with MAGIC DOC header are updated after turns with tool calls; updates are non-blocking; concurrent updates are serialised; the doc reflects conversation knowledge after the session.

items.title: Coordinator mode — env-controlled lead-agent persona with restricted tool set
items.summary: CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE env var makes an agent run as a coordinator. Coordinator agents use a distinct tool set (ASYNC_AGENT_ALLOWED_TOOLS; internal swarm tools excluded from external API). matchSessionMode() detects and fixes mode mismatch when resuming a coordinator session.
items.done: Setting CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE spawns an agent with coordinator-only tools; resuming a session that was coordinator retains coordinator mode; mode mismatch on resume is fixed with a warning.

items.title: LSPTool full parity — call hierarchy, find references, hover, document symbols
items.summary: CC's LSPTool exposes the full LSP API: hover info (type + docs), go-to-definition, find references (with line preview), document symbols (outline), call hierarchy (incoming/outgoing callers), workspace symbols. Multi-language via the LSP server manager (not just TS). ENABLE_LSP_TOOL env var gates it.
items.done: Agent can get call hierarchy for a function, list all references with context, get hover docs for a symbol; works for any language with an LSP server configured.

items.title: Task management tools — TaskCreate/Get/List/Update/Stop/Output for agent loops
items.summary: Suite of agent-accessible task management tools for the TodoV2 task system: TaskCreate (spawn background task), TaskGet (check status), TaskList (list all tasks), TaskUpdate (update title/description), TaskStop (cancel task), TaskOutput (stream task output). Enables agent orchestration of background work.
items.done: Agent can create a background task, monitor its status, stream its output, and stop it; tasks survive context compaction; task IDs are stable references.

items.title: Shell snapshot — capture aliases, functions, PATH for shell completions
items.summary: Shell snapshot captures the user's current shell environment (PATH entries, aliases, functions) by running a probe script in the user's shell. The snapshot feeds the shell completion engine in the typeahead. Uses embedded ripgrep/bfs/ugrep via argv0 dispatch for completions.
items.done: Shell aliases defined in .bashrc/.zshrc appear in typeahead completions; PATH-based executables are suggested; snapshot updated when shell config changes.

items.title: Built-in agent types — Explore, Plan, Verification, Claude Code Guide, General Purpose
items.summary: CC ships 6 built-in agent definitions: Explore (read-only codebase search), Plan (planning with EnterPlanMode), Verification (test/verify changes), General Purpose, Claude Code Guide (meta-help), Statusline Setup. Disableable via CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_DISABLE_BUILTIN_AGENTS for SDK users. A/B gated.
items.done: 6 built-in agent types selectable in /agents or @AgentType completions; env var disables all for SDK users; coordinator mode loads coordinator-specific agents instead.

items.title: Per-agent-type memory — scoped memory dirs per agent (user/project/local)
items.summary: Each named agent type gets its own memory directory: user (~/.claude/agent-memory/<type>/), project (.claude/agent-memory/<type>/), local (.claude/agent-memory-local/<type>/). Remote memory dir for cloud (CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_MEMORY_DIR). Colons in plugin-namespaced types replaced with dashes for path safety.
items.done: Explore agent has its own memory separate from the main session; agent memories persist across invocations; remote mount supported for cloud sessions.

items.title: In-process swarm backend — agents share Node.js process via AsyncLocalStorage
items.summary: Alternative to tmux backend: agents run in same Node.js process with isolated context via AsyncLocalStorage. Share API client and MCP connections. File-based mailbox IPC. Killed via AbortController. Enables swarms without tmux dependency.
items.done: Swarm agents spawn in-process when tmux unavailable; share API/MCP connections; isolated via AsyncLocalStorage; mailbox IPC works same as tmux backend.

items.title: Read-only command validation maps — git/gh/shell safe-flag allowlists
items.summary: Comprehensive allowlist maps for read-only validation: GIT_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS (all git subcommands + safe flags + danger callbacks), GH_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS (gh CLI read-only ops), EXTERNAL_READONLY_COMMANDS (cross-shell). containsVulnerableUncPath detects Windows UNC paths that can leak credentials.
items.done: In read-only mode, only allowlisted git/gh commands pass; any unsafe flags rejected; UNC paths blocked; PowerShell and bash share the same validation logic.

items.title: Bash security blocks — process substitution, Zsh expansions, heredoc injection
items.summary: BashTool blocks dangerous shell patterns: process substitution `<()` `>()` `$()` `${}`, Zsh equals expansion `=cmd` (bypasses deny rules), heredoc in substitution, malformed token detection, PowerShell comment syntax. Each pattern has a specific error message. Defense-in-depth on top of the permission system.
items.done: Commands using `$(cmd)` are blocked with a specific error; `=curl` is blocked explaining Zsh equals expansion; all patterns surface the reason they're blocked.

items.title: Fork subagent — implicit context inheritance when subagent_type omitted
items.summary: When the Agent tool is called without `subagent_type`, it forks the current conversation: the child inherits the parent's full context and system prompt. All spawns run async for unified task-notification model. `/fork <directive>` slash command. Mutually exclusive with coordinator mode.
items.done: Calling Agent without subagent_type spawns a fork with full parent context; `/fork fix the lint errors` creates a focused fork; task-notification pattern used for all async agents.

items.title: Stalled spinner animation — visual state change when agent is stuck
items.summary: CC's spinner switches to a 'stalled' animation after a configurable idle threshold — visually distinct from the normal working state. Helps users notice when an agent hasn't progressed. Vanta's spinner runs the same animation regardless of progress.
items.done: After N seconds without a tool call or token output, the spinner switches to the stalled variant; once output resumes it returns to the normal animation.

items.title: Glimmer / shimmer text animation during processing
items.summary: CC's GlimmerMessage + ShimmerChar render animated shimmer/glimmer effects on status text while the model is generating — gives visual feedback that the agent is alive. Vanta uses a static spinner; no animated text shimmer.
items.done: While the model is streaming, status text shimmers character-by-character; `prefersReducedMotion` disables it; the animation is driven by use-animation-frame.

items.title: Hook progress message — live hook execution in transcript
items.summary: CC's HookProgressMessage shows hook execution status inline in the transcript (running, completed, error) with the hook name and statusMessage. Vanta will run hooks (CC-HOOKS-ENGINE next) but has no visual feedback for hook execution.
items.done: Running hooks show a progress line with hook name + statusMessage; on completion the line updates to success/error; multiple concurrent hooks show stacked.

items.title: Message timestamps — per-message time display
items.summary: CC renders a MessageTimestamp on each message showing elapsed time since session start and absolute time. Useful for understanding latency, reviewing session history, and correlating with external events. Vanta shows no timestamps.
items.done: Each message shows a dim timestamp (hh:mm:ss relative + HH:MM absolute); a settings key `showMessageTimestamps` toggles them; they're always on in export/headless mode.

items.title: Shell command timing — elapsed time display per command
items.summary: CC's ShellTimeDisplay + ShellProgressMessage show real elapsed time for each shell command, updating live during execution. Helps identify slow commands, understand build times, and debug performance issues. Vanta shows no timing.
items.done: Running shell commands show a live elapsed timer (HH:MM:SS); on completion the final time is shown dimmed next to the command; sub-second commands show ms.

items.title: Channel message display — incoming platform messages in transcript
items.summary: CC renders UserChannelMessage as a visually distinct message type showing the source platform (Telegram, Slack, etc.) and sender alongside the message content. Vanta's gateway handles incoming messages (E2 shipped) but they're rendered as plain user text.
items.done: Incoming channel messages (Telegram, iMessage, etc.) render with a platform badge + sender name + message content, distinct from direct user input.

items.title: Effort indicator — visual effort level callout in TUI
items.summary: CC's EffortCallout + EffortIndicator render the active effort level visually (low/medium/high/xhigh/max) with a colored indicator. Pairs with CC-EFFORT (next) for the flag/setting. Vanta has no effort-level display even once the setting is added.
items.done: The status line (or spinner area) shows the active effort level as a colored indicator; /effort <level> changes it with immediate visual feedback.

items.title: BypassPermissions confirmation dialog — explicit gate for dangerous mode
items.summary: CC shows BypassPermissionsModeDialog when entering bypassPermissions mode, requiring explicit confirmation and showing a risk summary. Prevents accidental activation of the most permissive mode. Vanta allows mode switching without a dedicated confirmation step.
items.done: Switching to bypass/bypassPermissions mode shows a modal: risk summary, list of what will be allowed without prompts, explicit confirmation; confirmed mode is shown with a persistent 'dangerous' badge.

items.title: Cost threshold dialog — alert when approaching budget cap
items.summary: CC's CostThresholdDialog alerts when cumulative session cost approaches a threshold (80% and 100% of --max-budget-usd). Pairs with CC-BUDGET-CAP (next). Without this UI, the agent silently stops at budget without user awareness.
items.done: At 80% of the budget cap, a non-blocking warning shows in the status; at 100%, a dialog offers to continue with a new cap, stop now, or ignore; actual stop behavior is configurable.

items.title: Terminal window title — session name / active task in title bar
items.summary: CC's use-terminal-title.ts sets the terminal window/tab title to the current session name or active task, so users can identify Vanta sessions in a terminal multiplexer or across multiple windows. Vanta sets no terminal title.
items.done: The terminal title updates to 'Vanta — <session name>' at session start and '<active task>' while working; resets to 'Vanta' on idle; disabled with an env var.

items.title: Status notices — notices area above the status line
items.summary: CC's StatusNotices renders a notices area above the status line for non-critical informational messages: IDE connection status, channel setup hints, keybinding warnings, LSP recommendations. Separate from the status line so notices don't clutter the primary info. Vanta has no notices layer.
items.done: A notices area appears above the status line when there are active notices; each notice has a type (info/warning/action), message, and optional dismiss shortcut; notices auto-expire after N seconds.

items.title: ThinkingToggle — dedicated expand/collapse control for reasoning
items.summary: CC has a standalone ThinkingToggle component separate from the thinking display itself — a dedicated control that toggles all thinking sections at once, with state persisted across the session. CC-S-THINKING (shipped) shows thinking collapsible; this is the persistent global toggle.
items.done: A ThinkingToggle in the status or footer toggles all thinking sections at once; state persists in settings; keyboard shortcut cycles thinking display (hidden / summary / full).

items.title: SkillsMenu — in-TUI skill browser and picker
items.summary: Bare `/skills` in the TUI now opens an interactive picker (tui/skills-picker.tsx): type-to-filter by name/description, ↑↓ navigate, ⏎ invokes the skill through the agent, Esc closes. New 'skills' OverlayKind in use-overlays (openSkills/invokeSkill + skillList state); use-submit routes bare /skills to the overlay (with-arg falls through to the list handler). ChromeSkills wired in app.tsx BottomChrome.
items.done: /skills opens SkillsMenu; arrow keys navigate; Enter invokes the skill; search filters by name/description. Suite green, tsc clean, size gate clean.

items.title: Advisor message — stronger-model reviewer response in transcript
items.summary: CC's AdvisorMessage renders the response from an advisor (stronger model) in a visually distinct message type in the transcript — different border/color from regular assistant messages. The advisor pattern is used by CC's built-in code reviewer. Vanta calls the advisor but renders its output identically to other tool results.
items.done: When the advisor tool returns, its response renders in a distinct bordered block with an 'Advisor' label; the model name appears dimmed; the content renders as full markdown.

items.title: ShutdownMessage — graceful session end display
items.summary: CC's ShutdownMessage shows a structured session-end summary: what was accomplished, shutdown reason (user exit / budget exceeded / context full / error), and a handoff note. Vanta exits without a session summary in the transcript.
items.done: On session end, a ShutdownMessage appears showing the shutdown reason, a brief session summary (goals accomplished, files changed, cost), and any handoff note for resuming.

items.title: Auto mode opt-in dialog — confirmation flow for ML permission mode
items.summary: CC's AutoModeOptInDialog walks users through enabling auto mode with an explanation of how ML classification works, what actions it auto-approves, and a soft_deny preview. Pairs with CC-AUTO-MODE (next). Without this dialog, auto mode activation has no UX scaffolding.
items.done: Enabling auto mode shows AutoModeOptInDialog: explains ML classification, shows the soft_deny preset, previews which actions would be auto-approved in the current session, then confirms.

items.title: KeybindingWarnings — conflict detection and resolution hints
items.summary: CC's KeybindingWarnings detects and surfaces conflicting keybindings (e.g. user-defined bindings that shadow built-ins) with a notice and suggestions to resolve. Pairs with CC-TMUX (horizon) and TUI-KEYS (next). Without conflict detection, users silently lose bindings.
items.done: On session start, KeybindingWarnings checks for conflicts; StatusNotices shows a warning for each conflict with the conflicting keys and a resolution hint; /keybindings shows the full conflict report.

items.title: Resource update message — MCP resource change in transcript
items.summary: CC's UserResourceUpdateMessage renders MCP resource subscription updates (when a subscribed MCP resource changes) as a distinct message type in the transcript, showing what changed and from which server. Pairs with CC-MCP-RESOURCES (next).
items.done: When an MCP resource changes, a UserResourceUpdateMessage appears in the transcript: server name, resource URI, and a summary of what changed; these can be filtered from the transcript view.

items.title: Keybinding contexts — 17+ named UI contexts for context-sensitive bindings
items.summary: Bindings are scoped to named contexts: Global, Chat, Autocomplete, Confirmation, Help, Transcript, HistorySearch, Task, ThemePicker, Settings, Tabs, Attachments, Footer, MessageSelector, DiffDialog, ModelPicker, Select, Plugin. Context registers/unregisters at React mount/unmount; resolver applies priority by active context set.
items.done: Bindings only fire in their declared context; Global bindings work everywhere; context stack updates on dialog open/close; conflicts within a context surface a warning.

items.title: Custom output styles via .claude/output-styles/ — markdown-file-defined styles
items.summary: Users and projects define custom output styles as .md files in ~/.claude/output-styles/ (user) or .claude/output-styles/ (project). Each file = one named style; frontmatter provides name/description; `keep-coding-instructions: true` preserves coding behavior while applying the style. Plugin styles also load here.
items.done: Custom output styles appear in /output-style picker; project styles override user styles; styles from plugins load alongside user/project styles; keep-coding-instructions flag respected.

items.title: /copy — copy last response as markdown, HTML, or RTF to clipboard
items.summary: The /copy command copies the last assistant response to the system clipboard in the user's chosen format: plain markdown, rendered HTML, or RTF. Uses OSC clipboard sequence for terminal clipboard access.
items.done: Running /copy shows a format picker; selected format is copied to clipboard; markdown copies raw text; HTML converts markdown to rendered HTML; RTF for rich text editors.

items.title: Project onboarding wizard — first-run step-by-step setup for new workspaces
items.summary: When opening CC in an empty workspace or a workspace without CLAUDE.md, a step-by-step onboarding wizard guides the user through creating a project (ask Claude to create an app, clone a repo, etc.). Steps track complete/completable/enabled state and persist to project config.
items.done: Empty workspace opens with onboarding wizard; steps complete as user progresses; CLAUDE.md creation step marks complete when file appears; wizard dismissed after completion.

items.title: TodoItem activeForm field — track current action verb in todo items
items.summary: Each TodoItem has a Zod-validated `activeForm` field (e.g. 'Building', 'Testing') that stores the current progressive tense of the action being performed. Displayed in the task list UI next to in_progress items as a live status line.
items.done: In-progress todo items show their activeForm (e.g. 'Building auth.ts'); completed items show a checkmark; pending items show content only.

items.title: Teammate color coding — unique color per agent in swarm UI
items.summary: Each spawned teammate is assigned a unique color from the AGENT_COLORS palette in round-robin order. Colors persist per session and are displayed in the TUI teammate tree, footer pills, and @-completion suggestions to distinguish agents at a glance.
items.done: Each teammate shows with a distinct color in the TUI; same agent keeps same color throughout session; colors cycle across the palette for large swarms.

items.title: MCP server reconnect + enable/disable toggle in live session
items.summary: useMcpReconnect() and useMcpToggleEnabled() hooks allow reconnecting a failed MCP server or toggling it enabled/disabled without restarting the session. Accessible from the MCP settings panel in the TUI.
items.done: Failed MCP server shows a Reconnect button; toggling a server off stops its tools from appearing; toggling back on reconnects; no session restart required.

items.title: Custom spinner verbs — user-configurable loading message verbs
items.summary: Spinner loading messages use verbs from a configurable list (default: 'Accomplishing', 'Actioning', 'Actualizing', etc.). Users can replace the list entirely or extend it via `spinnerVerbs` setting with `mode: 'replace'` or `mode: 'extend'`. Empty replace uses built-in list as fallback.
items.done: Setting spinnerVerbs.mode='replace' uses only user verbs; mode='extend' adds to built-ins; empty replace falls back safely.

items.title: /terminal-setup — install Shift+Enter keybinding for terminal newlines
items.summary: CC has a `/terminal-setup` command that configures the user's terminal to support Shift+Enter for newlines in the input composer. It detects the terminal emulator (Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, WezTerm, Apple Terminal, others) and installs the appropriate keybinding. The command is hidden for terminals with native CSI-u/Kitty keyboard protocol support.
items.done: `/terminal-setup` detects terminal emulator and configures Shift+Enter (or Option+Enter on Apple Terminal) for multi-line input. Shows instructions for unsupported terminals.

items.title: Away summary — auto-generate context recap when terminal regains focus after 5+ min
items.summary: CC detects terminal focus loss via `subscribeTerminalFocus`. If the terminal has been unfocused for 5+ minutes and no away summary exists since the last user turn, CC auto-generates a `away_summary` system message summarizing recent activity. The model sees this as context when the user returns, reducing the need for manual context re-establishment.
items.done: After 5+ minutes away, returning to the terminal shows an auto-generated 'While you were away...' summary of recent turns injected as a system message.

items.title: OS system notification — desktop alert after long tool runs when terminal is inactive
items.summary: CC sends an OS-level system notification (macOS/Linux/Windows) when a tool response completes and the user hasn't interacted with the terminal for 6+ seconds. Uses `sendNotification` via the system notifier. Also respects terminal focus events via `useTerminalNotification`.
items.done: After a long bash command finishes while the user is in another app, a macOS/OS notification appears. Clicking it focuses the terminal.

items.title: Copy-on-select — auto-copy terminal text selection to clipboard (iTerm2-style)
items.summary: CC's `useCopyOnSelect` mirrors iTerm2's 'Copy to pasteboard on selection': when the user finishes a drag-select or multi-click selection in the terminal, the selected text is automatically copied to clipboard. The selection highlight remains visible. Only active in alt-screen (Ink) mode.
items.done: Dragging to select text in the Vanta TUI automatically copies it to clipboard, matching iTerm2 behavior.

items.title: Clipboard image hint — notification on focus when clipboard has an image
items.summary: When the terminal regains focus and the clipboard contains an image, CC shows a transient notification hinting 'Press /paste to attach image'. The hint has a 30-second cooldown to avoid spam on repeated focus changes. Pairs with the /paste command for clipboard-to-context image workflow.
items.done: After copying a screenshot and switching to the Vanta terminal, a hint appears: 'Image in clipboard — /paste to attach'. Dismisses after 30s.

items.title: AI session title — auto-generate session name via Haiku from conversation content
items.summary: CC uses a dedicated Haiku query to auto-generate a short, descriptive session title from conversation content. `sessionTitle.ts` is the single source of truth shared by: teleport (6-word title + branch name), /rename (kebab-case name), and SDK control requests. Titles are generated asynchronously and don't block the session.
items.done: After the first exchange, the session gets an auto-generated title (e.g. 'fix-auth-token-refresh') visible in /sessions, status bar, and teleport UI.

items.title: In-session transcript search — full-text search across conversation messages
items.summary: CC supports full-text search within the current session transcript. `transcriptSearch.ts` flattens messages to lowercased searchable text (WeakMap-cached for efficiency), filtering out sentinel interruption strings that would cause phantom matches. Used by the search UI (`SearchBox.tsx`, `QuickOpenDialog.tsx`).
items.done: Typing in the transcript search box highlights matching turns. Filtering by query jumps to the first match and shows match count.

items.title: Standalone agent name/color — custom identity for non-swarm agent sessions
items.summary: CC supports assigning a custom name and color to a standalone agent session (non-swarm). `getStandaloneAgentName()` returns the name only when not in a swarm team. Used by SDK/headless launchers to give sessions a recognizable identity in the UI (e.g. 'researcher', 'code-reviewer') without forming a full team.
items.done: Launching with `--agent-name=reviewer --agent-color=blue` shows the agent badge in the terminal and in task lists.

items.title: Auto-mode denial history — /permissions tab showing recently blocked commands
items.summary: CC tracks commands recently denied by the auto-mode transcript classifier (up to 20, newest-first) via `recordAutoModeDenial()`. These are surfaced in a 'RecentDenials' tab in the `/permissions` UI, showing tool name, display description, reason, and timestamp — helping users understand and tune what the classifier blocked.
items.done: `/permissions` has a 'Recent Denials' tab. When auto-mode blocks a command, it appears here with the classifier's reason.

items.title: Chrome automation GIF recorder — capture multi-step browser interactions as GIF
items.summary: During Chrome browser automation (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*` tools), CC can record multi-step browser interactions as animated GIFs via `mcp__claude-in-chrome__gif_creator`. The agent is prompted to capture extra frames before/after actions for smooth playback, and to name the file meaningfully. GIFs are saved locally for review or sharing.
items.done: After a multi-step browser automation, a named GIF file appears in the working directory showing the full interaction replay.

items.title: Smart first-run example commands — git-history-aware prompt suggestions on empty input
items.summary: On first launch or empty-state input, CC generates contextual example commands by sampling from git history (recent commit messages, changed files) filtered through a pattern list that removes lock files, build artifacts, generated files, and config. Examples like 'Explain the changes in <recent-file>.ts' are shown as tappable suggestions instead of generic placeholder text.
items.done: Empty input shows 3-4 smart example commands drawn from the current repo's git history. They update per-project, not per-install.

items.title: Auto-run /issue — proactive GitHub issue creation with ESC cancellation window
items.summary: When CC detects conditions that warrant filing a GitHub issue (bug, unexpected behavior), it shows an `AutoRunIssueNotification` banner proposing to auto-run `/issue`. The user has a brief window to press ESC to cancel; otherwise the issue command runs automatically. The banner shows the reason for the proposed issue.
items.done: After a crash or repeated error, a banner appears: 'Running /issue: unexpected tool failure [ESC to cancel]'. On ESC timeout, `/issue` runs and opens a draft in the browser.

items.title: External prompt editor — open $EDITOR to compose multi-line input
items.summary: CC lets users press a keybinding to open the current prompt input in their external editor (`$VISUAL`, `$EDITOR`, or detected IDE). Supports VS Code (`code -w`), Sublime Text (`subl --wait`), and any GUI editor. Pasted content refs from the editor are expanded back into the prompt on save. Enables composing complex multi-line prompts outside the terminal.
items.done: Pressing the editor keybinding opens the current prompt draft in VS Code. Saving and closing inserts the edited text back into the input.

items.title: Shell output JSON auto-format — pretty-print JSON lines in bash output
items.summary: CC's shell output renderer (`OutputLine`) attempts to parse each output line as JSON and re-serializes it for readability when successful. Checks for precision loss on large integers before substituting. Turns compact JSON API responses in bash output into human-readable formatted output without user action. [MODULE BUILT — formatJsonInOutput shipped + tested (term/json-format.ts); wiring deferred: Vanta summarizes tool output rather than showing raw lines, so there is no high-value display surface yet — needs a raw-output/expand view first.]
items.done: A bash command that outputs `{"status":"ok","count":42}` renders as nicely formatted JSON in the transcript instead of raw string.

items.title: Terminal hyperlinks — OSC 8 clickable links in tool output and messages
items.summary: CC creates OSC 8 terminal hyperlinks (`createHyperlink`) in shell output and messages. In supporting terminals (iTerm2, WezTerm, Kitty, modern terminals), URLs become clickable links that open in the browser. The feature is guarded by `supportsHyperlinks()` for terminals that don't support OSC 8.
items.done: File paths and URLs in bash output are rendered as clickable links in iTerm2/WezTerm. Clicking opens the file in the browser or editor.

items.title: /stats sparkline charts — ASCII activity charts and heatmap in stats view
items.summary: CC's `/stats` view renders ASCII sparkline charts using `asciichart` for activity over time (sessions/day, tokens/session), plus a terminal heatmap (GitHub-style). Navigation with j/k/arrows. The stats are aggregated from transcript JSONL files across all sessions.
items.done: `/stats` shows an ASCII line chart of daily activity and a 12-week heatmap of session counts, navigable with arrow keys.

items.title: History picker — searchable input history overlay in the prompt bar
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `HISTORY_PICKER`) adds a searchable history picker in the prompt input. Unlike simple up-arrow cycling, the history picker shows a scrollable list of past prompts filtered by typing, triggered by a keybinding while the input is active.
items.done: A keybinding opens a searchable list of past prompts. Typing filters the list; Enter selects and inserts into the input.

items.title: MCP rich output — enhanced rendering for large MCP tool results
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `MCP_RICH_OUTPUT`) replaces plain text rendering of large MCP tool outputs with `MCPTextOutput` — a richer component with truncation handling and expand/collapse. For smaller outputs, falls back to `OutputLine`. Improves readability of verbose MCP responses.
items.done: Large MCP tool results are truncated with an expand button. The output renders with proper line wrapping and JSON formatting.

items.title: Native clipboard image — capture images without external tools (xclip/pbpaste)
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `NATIVE_CLIPBOARD_IMAGE`) uses native OS clipboard APIs for image capture instead of shelling out to `xclip`/`xsel` (Linux) or `pbpaste` (macOS). Faster, works in environments where clipboard tools aren't installed, and avoids subprocess overhead.
items.done: Pasting an image from clipboard works on Linux even without xclip installed. `/paste` and auto-detect both use the native API path.

items.title: Auto theme — dynamic OS-aware theme switching ('auto' option in theme settings)
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `AUTO_THEME`) adds an `auto` option to theme settings. The `ThemeProvider` subscribes to OS appearance changes and switches between light/dark automatically. Without the flag, only named themes (dark, light, etc.) are available.
items.done: Setting theme to 'auto' in `/config` makes CC follow the OS dark mode toggle in real time.

items.title: Idle return dialog — re-engagement prompt after session inactivity
items.summary: CC shows an `IdleReturnDialog` when the user returns to an idle session. Distinct from the away summary (CC-AWAY-SUMMARY), which generates a recap text — the idle return dialog is an interactive prompt offering to resume, start fresh, or see what happened while away.
items.done: After 30+ min idle, returning to the terminal shows a dialog with 'Resume session', 'Start fresh', and 'Show what happened' options.

items.title: Sandbox violation expanded view — detailed breakdown of blocked sandbox actions
items.summary: CC has a `SandboxViolationExpandedView` component that renders an expanded view of sandbox policy violations. When the kernel blocks an action in sandbox mode, the expanded view shows which policy was violated, the command attempted, and context for the decision.
items.done: When a sandboxed tool call is blocked, the TUI shows an expandable violation panel listing the blocked command, rule violated, and why it was stopped.

items.title: MCP server multiselect dialog — batch-enable project MCP servers
items.summary: CC shows a `MCPServerMultiselectDialog` when a project has multiple pending MCP servers in `.mcp.json`. Users can space-select which servers to enable before confirming. More efficient than approving servers one by one through `MCPServerApprovalDialog`.
items.done: Opening a project with 3 MCP servers shows a checklist dialog. Selecting 2 and pressing Enter enables those servers for the project.

items.title: MCP project server approval — per-project approval dialog for .mcp.json servers
items.summary: CC auto-shows `MCPServerApprovalDialog` for project-scoped servers listed in `.mcp.json` when they haven't been approved yet. The `handleMcpjsonServerApprovals()` call in setup runs before the session starts, collecting approvals for all pending project servers.
items.done: When opening a project with `.mcp.json`, CC shows approval dialogs for unapproved servers before the session starts. Approvals are persisted per-project.

items.title: Shell history ghost text — inline completion from zsh/bash history in prompt input
items.summary: CC reads shell history (zsh/bash) and shows ghost text suffix completions in the prompt input. When the user types the start of a previously-run command, the matching completion appears as dimmed text that can be accepted with the right-arrow key. A 60s LRU cache prevents repeated disk reads.
items.done: Typing 'git push' in the prompt shows 'git push origin main' as grey ghost text if that's in shell history. Right arrow accepts.

items.title: File path completion — tab-autocomplete for directory and file paths in prompt
items.summary: CC has a `directoryCompletion.ts` module with LRU-cached directory listing that feeds path autocomplete in the prompt input. Typing `/` or `./` triggers path suggestions from the cwd. Completions appear in the `PromptInputFooterSuggestions` component.
items.done: Typing './src/' in the prompt shows a dropdown of files in that directory. Selecting one inserts the full path.

items.title: `/undo` as alias for `/rewind` — consistent session rollback UX
items.summary: CC (v2.1.108) makes `/undo` an alias for `/rewind`, so the more intuitive command name works. Both now open the rewind checkpoint picker.
items.done: Typing `/undo` opens the same rewind menu as `/rewind`. No behavioral difference — purely a discoverability improvement.

items.title: `/usage` — merged `/cost` and `/stats` into tabbed view
items.summary: CC (v2.1.118) merges `/cost` and `/stats` into `/usage` with tabs. Both `/cost` and `/stats` still work as shortcuts that open the relevant tab. Includes per-model and cache-hit breakdown for subscription users.
items.done: Running `/usage` opens a tabbed panel with Cost and Stats tabs. `/cost` and `/stats` jump directly to their respective tab.

items.title: Status line GitHub PR info — repo and PR data in status line JSON input
items.summary: CC (v2.1.145) adds GitHub repo name and PR number/title to the status line JSON input when detected from the git remote and current branch. Status line scripts can now show the active PR inline.
items.done: A status line script reads `git.repo` and `git.pr` from the JSON input and displays `owner/repo#42` next to the branch name.

items.title: `Ctrl+U` clears entire input; `Ctrl+Y` restores — readline kill-buffer update
items.summary: CC (v2.1.111) changes `Ctrl+U` from 'delete to start of line' to 'clear entire input buffer'. The cleared content goes into the kill ring and can be restored with `Ctrl+Y`. Matches zsh/bash Emacs kill-line behavior.
items.done: Pressing `Ctrl+U` on a multiline prompt clears all input. `Ctrl+Y` restores it.

items.title: Display/UX settings parity — spinner tips, turn duration, away recap, etc.
items.summary: CC settings include: spinnerTipsEnabled, spinnerTipsOverride (custom tips), spinnerVerbs (append/replace verbs), showTurnDuration (show elapsed ms), awaySummaryEnabled (show recap on return), syntaxHighlightingDisabled, prefersReducedMotion, autoScrollEnabled, preferredNotifChannel. Vanta has themes and accessibility settings (TUI-THEME) but not this full UX settings layer.
items.done: All listed UX settings are recognized in `~/.vanta/settings.json`; their default behaviors match CC semantics; `vanta config` surfaces them in the settings UI.

items.title: Plan approval message — rendered plan + approve/reject in transcript
items.summary: CC renders plan-mode plans inline in the transcript with a dashed box border + plan file path reference + approve/reject controls. The approval response also appears as a message. Vanta's plan mode (CC-PLAN-MODE-REAL next) needs this visual layer to be complete.
items.done: Plan-mode plans render in a boxed 'planMode' border with markdown content; approve/reject updates the transcript with a response message; plan file path shows dimmed.

items.title: Message selector — select and copy transcript messages
items.summary: CC's MessageSelector lets users select individual messages or ranges in the transcript and copy them to the clipboard. Essential for sharing outputs, extracting code, and referencing prior turns. Vanta has no message-level selection.
items.done: A key (e.g. Ctrl+Shift+C or configurable) enters selection mode; arrow keys move the cursor, Shift extends the selection; Enter copies selected messages to clipboard; Esc cancels.

items.title: Distinct bash input/output message types
items.summary: CC renders bash calls as two distinct message types: UserBashInputMessage (the command, with syntax highlighting) and UserBashOutputMessage (the result, scrollable). Vanta shows shell tool results as plain text inside a generic tool result block.
items.done: Bash commands render with a $ prompt and syntax highlighting; output renders in a distinct scrollable block with a line count; long outputs show a truncation hint with expand option.

items.title: Memory management UI — file selector + update notifications
items.summary: CC has MemoryFileSelector (browse + select memory files for edit/delete) and MemoryUpdateNotification (inline notification when a memory was saved or changed). Vanta has memory (MEM-* shipped) but no in-session memory management UI.
items.done: /memory opens the file selector; selecting a file opens it in the editor; MemoryUpdateNotification appears in the transcript when memory is written; notifications include file name + change type.

items.title: Export dialog — conversation export UI
items.summary: CC's ExportDialog lets users export the transcript in multiple formats (markdown, JSON, plain text) with options to include/exclude tool calls, thinking, and metadata. Vanta has /export (CC-S-EXPORT shipped) but no format-selection dialog.
items.done: /export opens a dialog with format options (markdown/JSON/text), include/exclude toggles for tool calls and thinking, and destination (file/clipboard); a preview shows the first N lines of the export.

items.title: Session preview — visual session content preview in resume picker
items.summary: CC's SessionPreview renders a thumbnail-style preview of a session's content (first user message, message count, last activity, model) in the session resume picker. Vanta's session list (/resume) shows ID + timestamp only.
items.done: /resume shows a list with session previews: first prompt (truncated), message count, last-active timestamp, model used; arrow keys navigate; Enter resumes; sessions are searchable by content.

items.title: SearchBox — in-transcript search with highlight and navigation
items.summary: CC's SearchBox is a reusable in-TUI search component: renders a search input overlay on the transcript, highlights all matches using searchHighlight.ts, and supports arrow-key navigation between matches with a match count indicator. Used by VirtualMessageList and GlobalSearchDialog.
items.done: A SearchBox can be bound to any scrollable content; it highlights all matches inline; arrow keys jump between matches with a '3 of 14' indicator; Esc closes and clears highlights.

items.title: Background file index — lazy singleton for @-mention completions
items.summary: Lazy singleton FileIndex built from git ls-files (tracked) + ripgrep (untracked), respecting .gitignore. Chunks work with event-loop yields. Emits a signal on build completion so typeahead upgrades partial results. Also surfaces CLAUDE.md / rules files as suggestion sources.
items.done: @path completions return git-tracked and untracked files; .gitignore entries excluded; partial results upgrade to full after index completes; CLAUDE.md paths surfaced.

items.title: User keybinding customization — ~/.claude/keybindings.json with hot-reload
items.summary: CC loads user keybindings from ~/.claude/keybindings.json (schema-validated via Zod, hot-reloads via chokidar). Users define context-scoped bindings with named action IDs (app:interrupt, history:previous, etc.); default bindings ship as a documented template. /doctor reports validation errors.
items.done: Users can add/modify keybindings in ~/.claude/keybindings.json; changes reload without restart; invalid entries reported by /doctor; generating the template pre-fills all defaults.

items.title: Chord bindings — multi-keystroke sequences (e.g. ctrl+k ctrl+s)
items.summary: Keybinding parser handles chord sequences (two or more keystrokes). Resolver tracks pending-chord state; results include chord_started, chord_cancelled, match, none. Platform modifier aliases normalised (ctrl/alt/meta/cmd/super).
items.done: Multi-keystroke chords defined in keybindings.json fire correctly; partial chord shows pending state; invalid followup cancels without side effects; modifier aliases all work.

items.title: Memory relevance via Sonnet side-query — LLM-selected memory files per turn
items.summary: Each turn, CC scans memory-file headers (frontmatter name+description only), then fires a dedicated Sonnet side-query to pick up to 5 most-relevant files for the current user query. Excludes API/usage docs for tools already in use. Returns file paths + mtime for staleness annotation.
items.done: Only relevant memory files are loaded per turn (not all memories); recently-used tool docs excluded from selection; up to 5 files selected; selection logged for debugging.

items.title: Session backgrounding — Ctrl+B to background/foreground running queries
items.summary: Ctrl+B backgrounds the current in-progress query into a background task, freeing the TUI for a new query. Foreground a running background task and sync its messages back. Enables multi-query session management without cancelling work in flight.
items.done: Ctrl+B while a query is running moves it to background; user can continue chatting; /tasks or the task panel shows background task progress; foreground a task to see its messages.

items.title: In-TUI next-prompt suggestions — tappable predicted prompts after each turn
items.summary: After each model response, CC generates 3 predicted next prompts via a forked side-query and shows them as tappable options below the input. Controlled by `promptSuggestionsEnabled` setting. Disabled in swarm/teammate mode and non-interactive sessions.
items.done: 3 suggested prompts appear after a model response; selecting one submits it; suggestions update per turn; disabling in settings removes them.

items.title: Auto-dream — periodic background memory consolidation across sessions
items.summary: Background service that consolidates memories across sessions via forked agent. Fires when: time since last consolidation >= minHours AND session count >= minSessions. File-based lock prevents parallel runs. Made visible in task list as DreamTask (phase: starting/updating, files touched, turn summaries).
items.done: After accumulating sessions, auto-dream fires; progress visible in footer pill; lock prevents duplicate runs; files touched tracked; consolidation updates MEMORY.md.

items.title: Detailed session cost tracker — per-model usage, cache tokens, tool+API duration, lines changed
items.summary: CC tracks: total cost USD, per-model token usage, API duration (with/without retries), tool duration, cache creation/read tokens, web search request count, lines added/removed, unknown-model cost flag. All aggregated per-session and per-turn for the cost footer and /stats display.
items.done: Session end shows total cost, model breakdown, cache efficiency, API vs tool time split, lines changed; /stats shows all counters; reset on /clear.

items.title: Agentic session search — LLM-powered semantic search across past sessions
items.summary: The /resume picker uses agenticSessionSearch to find relevant sessions by content, not just title. Scans up to 2000 chars of transcript per session (max 100 sessions), then side-queries a small/fast model to rank sessions by relevance to the search query.
items.done: Typing a search term in the resume picker finds sessions by content (not just name); LLM ranks results by relevance; limited to 100 sessions to stay fast.

items.title: WaitForMcpServers tool — block until MCP connections are ready
items.summary: CC exposes WaitForMcpServers so an agent can block at session start until all pending MCP server connections resolve, instead of racing or silently missing tools. Vanta currently has no readiness barrier for MCP mounts.
items.done: `wait_for_mcp_servers` blocks until all configured MCP servers are connected (or timeout), then returns a per-server status map.

items.title: /reload-plugins — activate plugins installed during the current session
items.summary: CC's `/reload-plugins` activates any plugins installed since session start without requiring a restart. If you `vanta plugin install` during a session, the new plugin isn't active until `/reload-plugins` or restart. Vanta has no in-session plugin reload.
items.done: `/reload-plugins` re-scans the plugin directory, loads any newly installed plugins, and prints a diff of what was added; existing plugins are not re-initialized.

items.title: Plugin install hints via <claude-code-hint> — stderr-driven plugin prompts
items.summary: CLIs and SDKs emit `<claude-code-hint plugin='slug' />` tags to stderr. CC intercepts, deduplicates (show-once per plugin ever), and surfaces a one-time notification to install the plugin from the marketplace. Shown/dismissed state recorded in config.
items.done: Running a tool that emits a hint tag shows a one-time install prompt; dismissing or installing prevents re-prompt; disabling hint recommendations suppresses all future hints.

items.title: Skill companion files — bundled assets extracted to disk on first use
items.summary: Skills can declare `files: Record<string, string>` of companion text assets. On first invocation CC extracts them to a per-skill base directory and prepends a 'Base directory: <dir>' line to the prompt so the model can Read/Grep them on demand.
items.done: Skill with declared files extracts them on first run; subsequent runs find them already present; model can reference them by path without knowing the extraction location.

items.title: allowedTools + disableModelInvocation in skill frontmatter
items.summary: Skills can restrict their tool palette via `allowedTools: string[]` in frontmatter. `disableModelInvocation: true` makes a pure-tool skill that executes its tool list without an LLM turn. Pairs with hooks and agent fields to compose complex skill behaviors.
items.done: Skills with allowedTools only expose those tools during execution; skills with disableModelInvocation run tool calls without an LLM round-trip; /doctor reports invalid tool names.

items.title: Plugins can ship LSP server configs — auto-mount language servers
items.summary: A plugin can declare LSP server configurations in its manifest. CC reads the lsp/ directory from the plugin package, validates the config schema, and mounts the LSP server automatically when the plugin is enabled.
items.done: Installing a plugin that includes an LSP server config makes that language server available; CC's LSP manager mounts it; /doctor shows the LSP server status.

items.title: /init-verifiers — create project-specific verifier skills
items.summary: Bundled /init-verifiers command: analyzes the project and creates one or more verifier skill files that the Verify agent can use to automatically check code changes. Supports multiple verifiers for different subsystems (API + web UI). Progress tracked via TodoWrite.
items.done: Running /init-verifiers creates appropriate verifier skill files; multiple verifiers generated for multi-subsystem projects; output tracked with todos.

items.title: Skill usage tracking — 7-day half-life ranking by usage count + recency
items.summary: Records usageCount and lastUsedAt per skill invocation (debounced 60s to avoid write spam). Persisted in globalConfig. Ranking uses a 7-day half-life decay. Skills used more recently and more often rank higher in the skill picker and @-completions.
items.done: Frequently-used skills appear first in completions; recently-used skills surface above rarely-used ones; usage decays over time (7-day half-life).

items.title: Conditional skill activation on file edit — skills auto-load for matched paths
items.summary: When FileEditTool writes a file, CC calls `activateConditionalSkillsForPaths` and `discoverSkillDirsForPaths` to automatically activate any skills that declare a path match for the edited file. Skills can conditionally register themselves when specific files are touched, enabling project-aware tool sets.
items.done: Editing a file in a directory with a matching skill definition triggers that skill to activate for subsequent calls in the session.

items.title: Official MCP registry — prefetch Anthropic's server registry for trust lookups
items.summary: CC fire-and-forget prefetches the official Anthropic MCP registry at startup. This populates a URL set used to identify whether a connected MCP server is 'official' (from Anthropic's registry) vs. third-party. Used to show trust indicators in the MCP connection UI and to gate certain behaviors on official servers.
items.done: MCP connection list shows an 'official' badge for servers from `api.anthropic.com/mcp-registry`. Trust lookup is instant (in-memory Set after prefetch).

items.title: Plugin recommendation engine — proactive install suggestions for LSP and hint plugins
items.summary: CC has a `usePluginRecommendationBase` shared state machine that powers proactive plugin install recommendations. Sources include: LSP server plugins (for detected file types) and claude-code-hint plugins (from skill frontmatter). The machine handles gates (remote mode, already-showing, in-flight), async resolution, and shows success/failure notifications. Users can install from the suggestion.
items.done: Opening a `.py` file when no Python LSP is installed shows a 'Install pylsp plugin?' suggestion. Accepting installs and activates it.

items.title: Plugin auto-update — background marketplace and plugin update check on startup
items.summary: CC calls `autoUpdateMarketplacesAndPluginsInBackground()` during startup housekeeping. Official marketplaces with `autoUpdate: true` are refreshed automatically. Plugin updates are downloaded in the background without blocking the session start.
items.done: On next launch after a plugin update is released, the update is applied silently. A notification appears if a restart is needed.

items.title: `/reload-skills` — re-scan skill directories without restarting the session
items.summary: CC (v2.1.152) adds `/reload-skills` to re-scan `~/.claude/skills/` and plugin skill dirs in-session. A `SessionStart` hook can also return `reloadSkills: true` to auto-reload after installing new skills.
items.done: After a hook installs a new skill, `/reload-skills` makes it available immediately. No session restart needed.

items.title: Plugin `bin/` executables — plugins ship binaries invocable from Bash tool
items.summary: CC (v2.1.91) allows plugins to ship executables under `bin/`. These are added to the PATH for Bash tool subprocesses, so agents can call plugin-provided tools as bare commands without absolute paths.
items.done: A plugin ships `bin/my-tool`. The agent calls `my-tool --flag` in Bash without knowing the install path.

items.title: MCP `alwaysLoad` option — skip tool-search deferral for trusted servers
items.summary: CC (v2.1.121) adds `alwaysLoad: true` to MCP server config. All tools from that server are loaded immediately at startup instead of waiting for a tool-search lookup. Useful for MCP servers with a small, always-relevant tool set.
items.done: A high-frequency MCP server configured with `alwaysLoad: true` has its tools available from message 1, without a ToolSearch call first.

items.title: MCP result `maxResultSizeChars` — allow up to 500K chars via `_meta` annotation
items.summary: CC (v2.1.91) adds MCP tool result persistence via `_meta['anthropic/maxResultSizeChars']` annotation (up to 500K chars). MCP tools can annotate their results to persist large outputs — e.g. database exports, full file reads — beyond the default truncation limit.
items.done: An MCP tool returning a 200K-char result with `_meta['anthropic/maxResultSizeChars']: 300000` has the full result persisted and available to the model.

items.title: `skillOverrides` setting — control per-skill visibility to model and menu
items.summary: CC (v2.1.129) makes `skillOverrides` work: `off` hides a skill from both the model and `/`-menu; `user-invocable-only` hides from model but keeps it in the menu; `name-only` collapses the description to save context tokens.
items.done: Setting `skillOverrides: {my-verbose-skill: 'name-only'}` keeps the skill available but strips its description from the system prompt.

items.title: Skill `\$` escape — include literal `$` before a digit in skill command bodies
items.summary: CC (v2.1.163) adds `\$` escape syntax in skill command bodies to include a literal `$` before a digit. Without escaping, `$1` is interpreted as a command argument placeholder.
items.done: A skill with `command: 'echo \$1.00'` outputs the literal string `$1.00` instead of substituting argument 1.

items.title: MCP allowlist/denylist settings — per-session server access control
items.summary: CC settings: allowedMcpServers (allowlist by name/glob), deniedMcpServers (denylist), enableAllProjectMcpServers (auto-approve project-level .mcp.json servers), enabledMcpjsonServers / disabledMcpjsonServers (per-server overrides). Vanta mounts all configured servers without granular per-session control.
items.done: allowedMcpServers and deniedMcpServers gate which servers are mounted at session start; enableAllProjectMcpServers auto-approves .mcp.json servers; the per-server overrides take effect without prompts.

items.title: Skill context budget + management settings parity
items.summary: CC settings: skillListingBudgetFraction (max context fraction for skill index, default 0.01), maxSkillDescriptionChars (per-skill description limit), disableBundledSkills (turn off shipped skills), disableSkillShellExecution (block shell exec in skills), skillOverrides (per-skill visibility), disableWorkflows (disable dynamic workflows). Vanta clips skill descriptions (PROMPT-STABILITY) but lacks the full settings layer.
items.done: All listed skill settings are in the schema and enforced at skill-index build time; disableWorkflows gates the Workflow tool; skillOverrides accepts a name→mode map.

items.title: /skillify — capture current session as a reusable skill
items.summary: Bundled /skillify skill that reads session memory + user messages, then generates a new skill file (frontmatter + prompt) encoding the session's repeatable process. User describes the skill name; the model produces a ready-to-commit skill file.
items.done: Running /skillify after a session produces a valid skill .md file with frontmatter and a prompt that recreates the session's workflow; the file is saved to the skills directory.

items.title: MCP elicitation — in-session user input prompts from MCP servers (2025-11-05 spec)
items.summary: Handles MCP `elicitation/create` requests from servers. Two modes: form (surfaces a dialog for user text input during tool execution) and url (opens browser URL, waits for completion notification via `notifications/elicitationComplete`). Pre/post hooks. Part of MCP 2025-11-05 spec.
items.done: MCP tool calling elicitation/create surfaces a dialog; user submits input; server receives the response; url mode opens browser; completion notification resolves the elicitation.

items.title: .dxt extension packages — single-file MCP server installer
