items.title: Full composer keybindings (readline/Emacs + macOS Cmd)
items.summary: Build every known line-editing shortcut in the composer (tui/composer.tsx). Shipped: Ctrl+U/W, Esc, up/down history, Shift+Enter. To build: cursor movement (Ctrl+A/E start/end, Ctrl+B/F char, Alt+B/F + Ctrl+arrows word, Cmd+arrows line, Cmd+up/down top/bottom, Alt+up/down move-line), deletion/kill (Ctrl+D/Delete forward, Alt+Backspace/Ctrl+Backspace word-before, Alt+D word-after, Ctrl+K kill-to-end, Cmd+Backspace/Delete to line start/end), edit/kill-ring (Ctrl+Y yank, Alt+Y yank-pop, Ctrl+T/Alt+T transpose, Alt+U/L/C case, Ctrl+_/Cmd+Z undo, redo), control (Ctrl+L clear, Ctrl+R reverse-history-search, Ctrl+G cancel). Full matrix + status in docs/tui-keybindings.md. Detect terminal capability + degrade gracefully (some chords need kitty-keyboard/Cmd forwarding).
items.done: The composer supports the full readline/Emacs editing set + macOS Cmd conventions per docs/tui-keybindings.md, verified against a capable terminal; unsupported-chord terminals degrade without breaking.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent, agent-verified + parent re-verified). tui/composer-edits.ts: pure Ink-free cursor/string transforms (wordLeft/wordRight, killToStart/killToEnd/killWordBack, deleteForward, yank) + 29 unit tests. Wired into composer.tsx: Ctrl+B/F (char nav), Alt+B/F (word nav), Ctrl+D (forward-delete; empty-input no-op, never exits), Ctrl+Y (yank from a single-slot kill ring that Ctrl+U/W/K now feed). Ctrl+A/E/U/W/K already existed — A/E unchanged, U/W/K enhanced to record the kill ring (onChange output byte-identical, existing tests green). Insert-mode only (vim normal-mode early-returns before the new keys). Multiline = whole-value ops (matches the shipped A/E/K convention). +31 tests; full suite 2184 green, tsc clean. (Composer fn size/complexity is pre-existing debt — out of scope; new helper file is clean.)

items.title: Two-network brain: salience network + executive control network (separate)
items.summary: PMC4455841 neuroanatomy: two DISTINCT intrinsic connectivity networks. (1) Executive Control Network (DLPFC, parietal, basal ganglia, thalamus, cerebellum) — planning, working memory, inhibition, set shifting. (2) Salience Network (frontal insula, anterior cingulate, ventromedial PFC, limbic) — determines WHAT MATTERS: emotional/social relevance, interoceptive state monitoring (internal load, stress level), attention allocation. Frontotemporal dementia specifically destroys the salience network, not the executive control network — this is why social behavior breaks first. Vanta currently conflates these: brain/mood is vaguely salience-adjacent but not architected as a separate system. BRAIN-NEURO must implement both, with explicit interaction: salience filters what enters executive processing; executive control determines how to act on what salience flagged. ND fit: salience network degradation = inability to determine priorities (everything feels equally urgent).
items.done: Vanta has a named salience subsystem that determines attention allocation separate from the executive planning subsystem; mood/stress state modulates what enters active processing.

items.title: Alternate terminal screen — fullscreen mode for dialogs
items.summary: CC's AlternateScreen component switches the terminal to the alternate screen buffer for fullscreen dialogs and views (settings, MCP panel, diff viewer, etc.), restoring the main screen on exit. Vanta's dialogs render inline, pushing content down.
items.done: Full-screen views (settings, MCP panel, diff, agent wizard) use the alternate screen; main transcript is preserved and restored on exit; Esc always exits the alternate screen.
items.notes: VirtualTranscript renders viewport slice (maxVisible=termRows-12) in alt-screen mode. scrollBy/scrollReset actions + pgup/pgdn keys. Static kept for non-alt-screen. All three resolved by one implementation. 2026-06-10: resize ghost-frame regression killed + banner restored via tui/alt-frame.tsx (always-overflow blank-filler viewport forces Ink's clearTerminal full-rewrite path instead of the cursor-relative eraseLines path that under-erases after a resize rewrap; a redraw nonce defeats log-update's identical-string skip) + tui/use-term-size.ts (reactive rows/cols + debounced resize redraw). Banner unified as a title-bordered two-column card across both screen modes. Commits a07f84c + 8b10fa6; see ERRORS.md 2026-06-10 pt 2.

items.title: ScrollBox — proper scrollable content containers
items.summary: CC's ScrollBox provides bounded scrollable regions with keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Page Up/Down, Home/End) independent of terminal scroll. Used for file pickers, MCP tool lists, permission dialogs with long content. Vanta relies on terminal scroll only.
items.done: ScrollBox renders a bounded height region; arrow keys scroll content without scrolling the whole terminal; scroll position is shown as a mini scrollbar; Home/End jump to top/bottom.
items.notes: VirtualTranscript renders viewport slice (maxVisible=termRows-12) in alt-screen mode. scrollBy/scrollReset actions + pgup/pgdn keys. Static kept for non-alt-screen. All three resolved by one implementation.

items.title: Live operator dashboard (brand dossier aesthetic)
items.summary: A unified live surface: active goals, current plan, blockers, recent actions, pending approvals, suggested next move, memory highlights - rendered in the Vanta Brand Style Guide dossier/control-room aesthetic (left status rail, operation cards, signal colors). Subsumes brand-TUI; the seed for the DESKTOP app.
items.done: One glance shows goals, plan, blockers, approvals, next move, and memory in the brand aesthetic.

items.title: SQLite knowledge graph (goals x entities x decisions)
items.summary: MemPalace knowledge_graph.py adapted for Vanta: temporal entity-relationship graph in ~/.vanta/graph.db. Entities=people/projects/tools, relationships=worked-on/decided/depends-on with timestamps. Enables cross-session queries: what goals mention tool X, what did we decide about Y last week. Ties MEM-VERBATIM + MEM-STRUCT together. ND fit F2+F5.
items.done: vanta graph query returns goals/sessions/decisions connected to an entity; graph updates on session close.

items.title: Multi-dimensional brain (5D: type x time x strength x relations x decay)
items.summary: Replace the 7 flat brain region.md files with a typed, timestamped, confidence-scored, graph-connected, decay-aware entry model. Dimensions: (1) type/region, (2) createdAt/updatedAt, (3) strength 0-1, (4) relatedIds cross-references, (5) forgetAfter decay. Store in ~/.vanta/brain.db (SQLite). AgentMemory Memory type as the reference model. Each brain write now carries strength + timestamp; the brainDigest injection selects by strength+recency, not just all text. Auto-decay stale entries (7d unless reinforced). ND fit F6 (Vanta has an honest, maintained self-model, not a stale dump).
items.done: Each brain entry has a strength score; brainDigest injects top-K by strength*recency; stale entries decay; conflicting beliefs show version history.

items.title: Full neurocognitive brain architecture (12-axis memory space)
items.summary: Beyond 5D: a complete brain modelled on human cognitive architecture. 12 axes per memory node: (1) type (fact/skill/preference/pattern/insight/plan/emotion), (2) temporal (created/modified/accessed), (3) confidence 0-1 (epistemic uncertainty), (4) strength 0-1 (consolidation score), (5) salience (relevance to current context), (6) associative (graph edges by type: relates-to/caused-by/contradicts/extends), (7) provenance (source session + tool call + user utterance), (8) forgetting curve (half-life, Ebbinghaus decay), (9) goal linkage (which goal this serves), (10) granularity (atom/pattern/insight/principle), (11) activation state (working/long-term/archived), (12) distinctiveness (dedup score vs nearest neighbors). Implementation: ~/.vanta/brain.db SQLite + vector index. Synthesis layer generates insights by combining high-strength nodes. Contradiction detector flags conflicting beliefs. Prospective memory (future plans) injected separately from episodic. This is BRAIN-5D extended -- implement 5D first, then layer remaining axes.
items.done: Vanta has a living, self-maintaining belief graph: memories decay unless reinforced, contradictions surface, insights crystallize from recurring patterns, provenance is always traceable.

items.title: Virtual message list — scrolling, in-transcript search, jump navigation
items.summary: CC's VirtualMessageList (1081 lines) renders only visible messages, supports Ctrl+R search-within-transcript, jump-to-match navigation, and sticky prompt headers when scrolling up through history. Vanta renders all messages eagerly — long sessions slow down and have no search.
items.done: Long conversations render only visible rows; Ctrl+R searches within the transcript with match highlighting and arrow-key navigation; sticky prompts show context when scrolling up.
items.notes: VirtualTranscript renders viewport slice (maxVisible=termRows-12) in alt-screen mode. scrollBy/scrollReset actions + pgup/pgdn keys. Static kept for non-alt-screen. All three resolved by one implementation.

items.title: Pre-send self-evaluation rubric
items.summary: Before sending, score the draft -- useful? grounded? too verbose? did I actually answer? did I avoid the real work? -- then improve it (wants1 #9). Extends B2 post-turn nudges + B3 background-review into an explicit pre-send rubric on the cheap route. Feeds ANTI-SLOP + TRUST-LABELS.
items.done: A weak draft is caught by the rubric (e.g. flagged 'avoided the real work') and revised before it reaches Jason, logged with the rubric verdict.

items.title: Code-based DM pairing (consent over allowlists)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. gateway/pairing.ts: generateCode (unambiguous 28-char alphabet), requestPairing (reuse unexpired code), verifyCode (expired/wrong/locked after MAX_ATTEMPTS=5), approvePairing, isApproved, listPairings. Wired into pollPlatform via home param — unknown senders get a code-gate flow. vanta pairing [list|approve <chatId>] CLI. 17 unit tests; tsc clean.
items.done: An inbound message from an unknown chatId triggers a one-time pairing code; only after the owner approves does that sender reach the agent; expired/over-limit codes are refused.

items.title: Per-model capability matrix
items.summary: From the Claude Code survey (claude-code-haha modelSupportOverrides) + Goose issue #9529. A small declarative map of which optional params (thinking/reasoning_effort/etc.) a model accepts, so free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama model IDs don't get sent params they 400/500 on. Lives alongside providers/catalog.ts. ND fit F6 (reliability removes vigilance burden).
items.done: Free-typing an OpenRouter/Ollama model that lacks thinking/effort no longer errors — Vanta omits unsupported params per a capability lookup.

items.title: Stall recovery / bounded-retry
items.summary: Today a verify-fail discards the slice with no recovery. Adapt Ouroboros stagnation detection (spinning/oscillation/no-drift/diminishing-returns, bounded <=N iterations) + OctopusGarden wonder->reflect (high-temp diagnose -> surgical fix). Replace single-shot discard with bounded iterate + a strategy switch on stall.
items.done: A stalled cycle detects the stall pattern and retries with a changed strategy within a bounded budget instead of discarding immediately.

items.title: Diagnostic repro bundle
items.summary: When something fails, assemble a clean redacted diagnostic packet: git status, relevant logs, package/tool versions, current provider/model, recent changes, and a concise failure summary. Safe to paste into another agent or issue.
items.done: `vanta repro` emits a redacted repro bundle plus summary; secrets are excluded and the bundle includes enough context to reproduce the failure.

items.title: Personal model scorecard
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. bench/model-bench.ts: BenchResult schema (model/provider/taskKind/latencyMs/cost/qualityNote), saveBenchResult, loadBenchResults, formatBenchScorecard (avg latency+cost+task count per model), buildRoutingRecommendations (fastest per task kind). vanta models bench CLI. 6 tests; 1737 total; tsc clean.
items.done: `vanta models bench` records latency, cost, and quality notes per model/task type, then recommends routing defaults for main and auxiliary functions.

items.title: Anti-slop filter (detect + flag AI-ish drift)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. repl/anti-slop.ts: detectSlop (6-rule PATTERN_RULES: sycophancy/ai-magic/corporate/empty-filler/fake-closing/overcautious) + formatSlopNote. antiSlopAfterText exported from session.ts (lazy import, VANTA_ANTI_SLOP=0 disables). Wired into interactive.ts (console note) and use-agent-send.ts (TUI dispatch note). 12 unit tests; 1639 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: Vanta flags a slop pattern in its own draft and offers a tightened rewrite before the message is sent.

items.title: Plugin lifecycle hooks (pre/post tool + LLM + session)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. plugins/hooks.ts: HookBus (on/fire/count/clear), 9 HookEvents, HookContext per event, pre_gateway_dispatch returns allow/skip/rewrite. globalHookBus singleton. Best-effort: throwing hooks logged+skipped. 7 unit tests; 1762 total; tsc clean.
items.done: A plugin can register a post_tool_call hook that fires after each tool with (name, params, result), and a pre_gateway_dispatch hook that can skip/rewrite an inbound message.

items.title: Checkpoints + /rollback
items.summary: Ported concept from a prior checkpoint_manager.py reference. Shadow-git auto-snapshot before destructive ops + a /rollback to restore. Vanta's kernel BLOCKS risky ops but doesn't snapshot/restore — an undo safety net lowers anxiety around letting the agent act. ND fit F6 (trust). Opt-in, dedup store.
items.done: Vanta snapshots before a destructive op; /rollback restores the prior state; never touches kernel-protected paths.

items.title: Model escalation + per-cycle cost ledger
items.summary: Answers Willison's ~$1k/day cost caution and serves the frugality drive. Start a cycle on a cheap/local model; escalate to a stronger model after N non-improving iterations, demote after improvements (OctopusGarden pattern). Add per-cycle cost/token accounting (Vanta has budgets but no escalation or cost ledger). Ties to routing/model-router + delegate.
items.done: A factory cycle starts frugal, escalates only when stalled, and records per-cycle token/cost; cheap work never pays premium-model cost.

items.title: vanta update — safe self-updater with rollback
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. cli/update.ts: runUpdateCommand — autostash local changes, save pre-update snapshot to ~/.vanta/snapshots/pre-update.json, git pull --ff-only, cargo build, npm install, skill sync, service restart. --rollback flag restores last snapshot. Already-up-to-date short-circuits cleanly. 1 export test; 1652 total green; tsc clean. Live: needs a tracking branch + network.
items.done: `vanta update` fetches, writes a pre-update snapshot, refreshes deps + skills + config additively, restarts the service, and reports a clean diff; `vanta update --rollback` restores the last snapshot.

items.title: Provider fallback chain on failure
items.summary: `fallback` (add/list/remove/test): when the primary model errors/rate-limits/times-out, it transparently retries the next provider in a configured chain. Vanta has task ROUTING (cheap/expensive via VANTA_MODEL_CHEAP/_EXPENSIVE) but NO failure-fallback — a dead provider just fails the turn. Add an ordered fallback chain (env or config), tried on retryable errors (429/5xx/timeout/circuit-break), with a one-line note in the transcript when it switches. Complements AUX-MAP (per-function model map). Manifesto-aligned: keeps the operator working when one backend is down.
items.done: `vanta fallback` configures an ordered provider chain; a retryable primary failure transparently falls through to the next, surfaced as a transcript note, and the choice survives restart.

items.title: vanta security audit — dependency/supply-chain scan
items.summary: `security audit` scans the venv against OSV.dev advisories and flags known-compromised packages (it caught the Shai-Hulud PyPI worm). Vanta has a hard SAFETY kernel for ACTIONS but zero visibility into its own DEPENDENCY supply chain (npm). Add `vanta security audit`: run `npm audit --json` + check installed versions against an advisory feed, report severity-ranked findings, fail-loud on critical. Manifesto-aligned (safety is the differentiator) and cheap — Vanta already pins versions and runs gitleaks pre-commit. Folds the security posture from action-time to install-time.
items.done: `vanta security audit` reports severity-ranked dependency advisories from npm audit + an advisory feed and exits non-zero on a critical finding.

items.title: Local OpenAI-compatible proxy over OAuth providers
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. proxy/server.ts: startProxyServer (HTTP, /v1/chat/completions + /v1/models), handleChatCompletion (maps OpenAI messages → Vanta provider). vanta proxy [port] CLI. OPENAI_API_KEY=vanta OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:7791/v1. 4 tests; tsc clean.
items.done: `vanta proxy` serves a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint backed by Vanta's resolved OAuth providers; a standard client completes a request through it without an API key.

items.title: Long-run resilience — resume an unattended run after a mid-flight drop
items.summary: Jason's #1 reliability pain on the autonomous path (Codex mining): he demands 'don't stop until they're all done / don't ask permission' (34+ delegations) but long runs die mid-flight — 'u keep bugging out › continue ■ Error: stream disconnected before completion'. He also hand-babysits long data pipelines (~20 near-identical 'check progress, if 600+ done queue X, if finished commit/push' messages) — manually playing the supervisor loop the agent should own. Vanta has checkpoints; this is auto-resume: a run that crashes/disconnects resumes from its last checkpoint without a manual 'continue', with progress + stall surfaced. Builds on checkpoints + STALL-UNBLOCK + the autonomy ladder.
items.done: An unattended run that dies mid-flight (crash/stream drop) auto-resumes from its last checkpoint and finishes the backlog without the user typing 'continue'; progress and any true stall are surfaced.

items.title: Pay down the 85 size-gate violations CODE-SIZE-GATE surfaced
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Fixed 15+ violations: providers/index.ts (resolveProvider cx24→cx5 via PROVIDERS map), agent.ts (runTurn refactored with TurnState + processToolCalls), memory/relevance.ts (cx13→cx3 pattern table), memory/curator.ts (56→split), sessions/search.ts (cx14→cx5), factory/compartments.ts (isSkeleton extract), projects/radar.ts (classifyStack 58→22), repl/self-eval.ts (cx15→cx4), repl/brief-cmd.ts (buildBrief split), repl/repro-cmd.ts (54→32), repl/dashboard-cmd.ts (split). 66→51 violations. Remaining: session.ts, factory/run.ts, interactive.ts, tui/*.tsx (larger refactors).
items.done: vanta lint reports 0 violations on src/ (tests excluded); VANTA_LINT_BLOCK=1 can be enabled without blocking normal commits.

items.title: Enforced read-only plan mode
items.summary: Today /planmode injects a PLAN_MARKER into the prompt (advisory). Claude Code's plan mode restricts the agent to read-only tools until the plan is approved. Make plan mode a real capability gate so the plan-before-act guarantee is enforced, not requested.
items.done: In plan mode the agent can only call read tools; any write/shell tool returns a 'blocked: plan mode' result until the user approves the plan, after which normal kernel gating resumes.

items.title: Background shell tasks
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. tools/bg-tasks.ts: spawnBackground (detached spawn →.vanta/bg-tasks/<id>.{log,json}), readBgTask, listBgTasks, readBgLog. Two new tools: bg_list (filter by status) + bg_status (with optional log tail). shell_cmd gains background: bool param. 1665 tests green; tsc clean.
items.done: shell_cmd accepts a background flag; a backgrounded command returns a task id immediately, keeps running, writes output to a file, and /tasks (or Ctrl+T) lists running/finished tasks with status.

items.title: Layered settings.json
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. settings/store.ts: SettingsSchema (allowedTools/blockedTools/env/gates/ui), loadSettings (user ~/.vanta/settings.json → project./.vanta/settings.json → local./.vanta/settings.local.json deep-merge), applySettingsEnv, isToolAllowed/Blocked. Wired into prepareRun (session.ts). vanta settings [show|paths] CLI. 13 tests; 1665 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: Vanta reads + merges settings.json from user/project/local scopes at startup (local > project > user), zod-validated; a project setting overrides a user setting, verified by a test.

items.title: Multi-directory roots (/add-dir)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. tools/writable-zones.ts: addSessionDir + getSessionDirs via VANTA_EXTRA_DIRS runtime env var; resolveWritableZones + resolveReadableZones now append extra dirs. /add-dir slash command (repl/add-dir-cmd.ts) validates path + updates session scope. Kernel still gates each individual access. tsc clean; 1665 tests.
items.done: /add-dir <path> adds a directory to the session scope; read_file/write_file then resolve within any active root; the kernel scope check treats each added root as in-scope; removing a dir re-narrows scope.

items.title: Print/headless mode + output formats
items.summary: Claude Code's -p runs non-interactively and --output-format text|json|stream-json structures output for piping/scripting. Vanta has `vanta run` (one-shot) but no structured output. Add -p print mode and --output-format for scripting and SDK use.
items.done: `vanta run -p '<prompt>' --output-format json` prints a single JSON result (no TUI); stream-json emits one JSON event per line; text stays the default human output.

items.title: Remote MCP servers (HTTP + OAuth)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. mcp/http-transport.ts: httpTransport (POST JSON-RPC, Bearer token, response streaming), resolveToken (VANTA_MCP_TOKEN_<SERVER> env var). mount.ts: ServerSchema now accepts url+token+headers OR command+args. mountMcpServers detects url→httpTransport, command→stdioTransport. 1709 tests; tsc clean.
items.done: An MCP server configured with an http URL mounts over HTTP; its tools register and call successfully; an OAuth-protected server completes auth and persists the token.

items.title: Deferred MCP tool schemas
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. tools/tool-search.ts: tool_search tool (keyword search → name+description+schema for up to 20 tools). When VANTA_MCP_DEFER=1: mcpToolToVantaTool strips parameters from schema (deferred flag) + adds [schema deferred] hint. The agent calls tool_search to expand. mcp/mount.ts passes deferred=true when env flag is set. tool_search added to ALL_TOOLS + registry test. 1665 tests; tsc clean.
items.done: With many MCP tools mounted, only tool names (not full schemas) sit in the system prompt; the agent fetches a tool's schema on first use; prompt token count drops measurably vs eager registration.

items.title: Skill frontmatter parity
items.summary: Vanta skills use flat -style frontmatter. Claude Code skills support $ARGUMENTS substitution, @file imports (inject file contents at invocation), context:fork (run in an isolated subagent), and user-only invocation. Add these for cross-compatibility.
items.done: A skill body with $ARGUMENTS receives the post-command text; @path imports inline file contents at run; a context:fork skill runs in a subagent and returns only its summary; parser + run-path covered by tests.

items.title: Worktree-isolated subagents
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. worktree/manager.ts: createWorktree (git worktree add on new branch), worktreeDiff, mergeWorktreeBranch, cleanup. delegate tool gains isolation: "worktree" — spawns agent in a fresh worktree, runs it, cleans up after. Two parallel isolated agents can edit without conflicting. tsc clean; 1666 tests.
items.done: delegate/subagent accepts a worktree-isolation flag; an isolated subagent runs in a fresh git worktree, its changes land on a branch, and the worktree is cleaned up after merge or discard; two parallel isolated agents don't conflict.

items.title: Native context compression (Headroom concept, zero-dep)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Rewrote Headroom's idea in pure TS instead of bolting on its Python proxy (keeps local-trusted-operator thesis: no external service/API key/egress). src/compress/: pure content router (json|logs|text|binary) → compressors. binary detection elides base64/blobs to a pointer (top real-data win: 20k-token screenshot → 12 tokens); json-crush keeps head+tail of object arrays + truncates strings; log-squash collapses blank-line runs/dupes/deep stacks. CCR store stashes original under content hash; retrieve_original tool expands on demand. Wired into agent.ts dispatchTool: compress each tool output ONCE before history (never system prefix, never re-compress). VANTA_COMPRESS (default on), best-effort. Proven on REAL session payloads = 22% overall (dominated by binary elision). 28 tests; tsc+lint clean.
items.done: Fat tool outputs (JSON/logs/binary) compress 50%+ before entering history, reversibly via retrieve_original, behind VANTA_COMPRESS — pure TS, zero new deps.

items.title: Diagnostic baseline tracking — surface only new LSP errors after file edits
items.summary: CC's `DiagnosticTrackingService` captures a baseline of IDE diagnostics before each file edit, then computes a delta after the write. Only errors and warnings that appeared after the edit are surfaced to the agent, preventing false noise from pre-existing issues. Tracks per-file diagnostic state with timestamps for right-file (cursor-context) diagnostics.
items.done: After writing a file, the agent sees only diagnostics introduced by that edit (new errors/warnings). Pre-existing issues are filtered as baseline.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent verified-integration, OPT-IN). lsp/diagnostic-delta.ts: pure diffDiagnostics(before, after) (keys on message+category so a line-shifted pre-existing diag stays baseline) + formatNewDiagnostics. lsp/diagnostic-note.ts: beginDiagnosticDelta(abs, isExisting) straddles a write (baseline read from disk BEFORE, delta AFTER — getDiagnostics reads disk) → appends "⚠ N new diagnostic(s)" to the write_file/edit_file result. PARENT DECISION: gated behind VANTA_DIAGNOSTIC_DELTA=1, DEFAULT OFF — getDiagnostics runs a full semantic TS build, so straddling costs TWO per .ts write (too much latency to impose by default; verified ts-service.buildService creates a fresh language service per call). Agent's sandbox couldn't run tests; parent verified (typecheck + 14 module/gate tests + full suite 2118 green). Best-effort, never blocks the write; .d.ts/test exempt. Documented in .env.example.

items.title: Per-function aux-task model map
items.summary: Generalize AUX-VISION into a function->model/provider map (vision, summarize, title, embed), one resolver extending routing/model-router.ts, surfaced in /status + a /aux command + the setup wizard. Full 'auxiliary tasks' dashboard parity.
items.done: Each auxiliary function can be bound to its own model independent of the main agent model.

items.title: Reflection + learning from correction (extract the rule)
items.summary: The grows-over-time loop (wants1 #18; wants2 #2/#7; wants2 build-order step 4). When Jason says 'not like that' / 'too generic' / 'should have asked first', extract a reusable rule (e.g. 'keep technical-dossier aesthetic, avoid mascot softness'), store it as a reflection memory, and auto-apply it later. Extends B3 background-review + B4 skill-curator + the reflection layer of BRAIN-NEURO.
items.done: A correction in chat produces a stored reflection rule that demonstrably alters a later response without being restated.

items.title: Protection agent (scams, privacy, unsafe actions, agent overreach)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. protection/agent.ts: scanForThreats (6 rule kinds: scam/privacy/unsafe-cmd/manipulation/agent-overreach/contract-trap) + formatThreatReport. protect tool registers in ALL_TOOLS. 9 unit tests; 1696 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: Given a risky input (e.g. a manipulative contract clause or an unsafe command), the protection agent flags it with the specific risk and a recommended action.

items.title: Born-small codegen (registry-by-default + 300 gate)
items.summary: Make the dark factory GENERATE small, independently-repairable units by default (the broken-leg property without later refactoring). Doc: docs/factory-evolution.md. 3 human-authored slices on factory/*.ts (skeleton): (1) verifier HARD GATE — pure checkNewFilesUnderLineLimit on NEW source files only (<=300; new-files-only is universally safe vs false-positiving bug fixes to pre-existing large files) + vanta-ts/CONVENTIONS.md (3-tier taxonomy: HARD GATE/GUIDANCE/CONVENTION DOC + the Tools-registry template); (2) planner gene-transfusion — prepend a PROVEN-PATTERNS block (spec wins on conflict) to buildInstruction for roadmap+parked only; (3) executor wiring — inject per-dir CLAUDE.md from plan.touchedDirs (closes the loop: factory requires those docs but never reads them back). Canonical template = tools/index.ts ALL_TOOLS + buildRegistry; mirrored by repl/handlers HANDLERS.
items.done: Factory refuses to birth a >300-line unit, instructs new-feature work to land as a registry unit (new file + one entry, no switch edit), and feeds each dir's own CLAUDE.md back to the agent.

items.title: Intent-satisfaction LLM-judge gate
items.summary: The biggest concrete gap found in the audit: the verifier proves code is CORRECT (tests pass, tsc clean) but never that it DID WHAT THE ITEM ASKED — a slice can pass every test and not fulfill the work item. Add an LLM-as-judge gate scoring intent-satisfaction against the work item; keep BOOLEAN for the deterministic checks (boolean is correct for did-the-build-break — do NOT fuzz those), judged only for subjective intent.
items.done: A slice that passes tests but doesn't address the work item's intent is rejected by the intent judge; deterministic gates stay boolean.

items.title: Ambiguity-gated preflight (unifies with ND clarify)
items.summary: From Ouroboros (Ambiguity = 1 - sum(clarity*weight), gate before codegen) — take the lightweight GATE, not the 9-minds/Double-Diamond ceremony. Before running a cycle, assess whether the work item is clear enough; if not, clarify or skip rather than burn tokens + drift. One mechanism serving BOTH the factory preflight AND the ND ND2 clarify / ND3 plan-first items.
items.done: The factory refuses to execute a too-vague item and routes it to clarify; clear items proceed. Shared with the ND clarify/plan-first surface.

items.title: Auto-spec + fan-out a roadmap card into child tasks
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. roadmap/decompose.ts: buildProposal (splits done-criteria bullets → child cards), childId, formatProposal, findCard, applyProposal. vanta roadmap decompose <id> [--apply] — preview-first (no --apply = dry run), writes children on --apply. 5 tests; 1671 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: `vanta roadmap decompose <id>` proposes ordered child cards with dependencies from a parent card's done-criteria, writes them to roadmap.json on approval, and regenerates the board.

items.title: Holdout author-separation validation
items.summary: From OctopusGarden + StrongDM + Willison (triple-confirmed). The codegen agent must NOT author its own acceptance check — today the same cycle writes code + test, so it can write a weak test (self-preferential-bias / reward-hacking). Hold a scenario/acceptance set the executor never sees; a separate implementation-blind agent (or pre-existing scenario) judges it. This is Vanta's SECOND validation layer (the kernel is the first) — NOT a kernel replacement. Adapt the idea, not the Digital-Twin-Universe machinery (irrelevant to self-improvement).
items.done: A factory slice is accepted only against criteria the implementing agent never saw; a slice that games its own test is caught.

items.title: Open file:line in the user's editor
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. editor/open.ts (pure): parseFileLine (file / file:line / file:line:col), resolveEditor (VANTA_EDITOR > VISUAL > EDITOR, default code), editorCommand (VS Code-family → 'code -g file:line'; vim/nano/emacs → '+line file'; subl → 'file:line'; unknown → flags+file) + openInEditor (detached spawn). `vanta open <file[:line]>` CLI + `/open` slash (repl/open-cmd.ts). 6 unit tests; all new files pass the size gate; full suite 1216 green; tsc clean. (Desktop anchors = CC-LINKS, still open.)
items.done: Clicking/invoking a file:line opens it at that line in the configured editor.

items.title: Claude Code Stop/PreCompact hooks for Vanta memory
items.summary: MemPalace-inspired: install Claude Code Stop + PreCompact hooks (scripts/vanta-memhook.sh) that fire when Claude Code stops or compacts context. Stop hook runs vanta memory mine in the background every N turns (VANTA_MEMHOOK_INTERVAL, default 10); PreCompact hook runs synchronously before context is lost. Closes the loop: Vanta preserves its own working memory across Claude Code sessions. Zero tokens in chat.
items.done: Claude Code Stop hook mines the Vanta transcript into memory every 10 turns, in background, zero tokens in chat. PreCompact hook runs synchronously.

items.title: Real-time error detection (anterior cingulate analog)
items.summary: PMC4455841 neuroanatomy: the anterior cingulate cortex plays a critical role in REAL-TIME error detection — it fires during execution when an error is being committed, not after. Vanta has reviewAfterTurn (post-hoc, like a retrospective audit) and EF-SELFMONITOR (pre-action sanity check). The missing piece is mid-execution error detection: detecting when a running tool chain has drifted from the goal WHILE it is running, not just before or after. Implementation: between tool calls within a single turn, a lightweight check (not an LLM call — a heuristic) tracks whether the sequence is still converging toward the stated goal. If N consecutive tool calls produce no apparent progress toward the goal, surface an interrupt opportunity. Pairs with EF-INHIBIT (which is pre-action) and EF-SELFMONITOR (which is pre-execution). ND fit F6 (catch errors before they compound).
items.done: Mid-execution drift is detected between tool calls; after N non-converging calls, Vanta surfaces an interrupt opportunity rather than continuing on autopilot.

items.title: Research convergence gate (pattern: research spiral)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: research spiral. Started implementation sprint, pivoted to 6+ research threads with no convergence. Inhibition failure: new discoveries reinforce continuation (reward loop). Counter-measure: after N consecutive research/analysis turns without an actionable commit or shipped item, surface a gentle gate: the turn count since last concrete output, the original stated goal, one question: want to pick one finding to build now? Not a block. A pattern interrupt that fires at the right moment. VANTA_RESEARCH_GATE_TURNS env (default 8). ND fit F1 (task initiation when stuck in exploration mode).
items.done: After 8 research turns without actionable output, Vanta surfaces the spiral pattern and offers a one-question redirect to execution.

items.title: Inline AI response editing
items.summary: /edit slash command loads the last assistant message into the composer for inline editing. TUI: yellow border + edit-mode placeholder; submitting replaces the message in convo history. REPL: response injected into readline via rl.write(). loadIntoComposer + editMessageIndex added to SlashResult.
items.done: User can type /edit and modify the last AI response inline without leaving the composer.

items.title: `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` — opt into flicker-free alt-screen rendering globally
items.summary: CC (v2.1.89) adds `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` to start every session in the fullscreen alt-screen renderer. Reduces terminal flicker, enables virtualized scrollback, and improves rendering performance on slow terminals.
items.done: With `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` in `.env`, all CC sessions start in fullscreen mode. `/tui` can still toggle back.
items.notes: Alt-screen enter/leave in launch.tsx; VANTA_NO_FLICKER=1 + CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1 both accepted. Documented in .env.example.

items.title: Esc interrupts the running agent
items.summary: Pressing Esc while the agent is running should abort the current turn (set the AbortSignal). Currently Esc has no effect mid-run, which means the only escape is Ctrl+C (kills the whole process). Should fire the existing abortRef in use-agent-send.ts and show a cancellation note.
items.done: Pressing Esc while busy=true cancels the current agent turn gracefully. A note appears confirming the interrupt. The composer becomes active again.
items.notes: Signal now flows through getCompletion -> provider.complete/stream. Codex uses fetch signal. OpenAI/Anthropic SDKs use RequestOptions signal. Abort checks every iteration + mid-stream chunk. Esc fires abortRef.abort() immediately.

items.title: Auto vague-goal to next action
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Extracted buildNextStepResend + isVagueGoal from repl/next.ts (no duplication — /next and the auto-fire share the same prompt builder). The /goal handler now, after setting a goal, runs isVagueGoal (short, or abstract-marker + no concrete anchor like a path/CARD-ID/#issue/code) and if vague auto-returns the single-micro-step resend so a concrete next action surfaces WITHOUT the user typing /next. Opt-out via VANTA_GOAL_ACTION=0. Wired in both hosts via the existing SlashResult.resend path. 7 unit tests (isVagueGoal + goal auto-fire via HANDLERS); tsc clean; handlers.ts still under the 300-line cap.
items.done: Setting a vague goal surfaces one concrete named next action without the user typing /next.

items.title: Detect stalled goals, propose smallest unblocker
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. repl/stall.ts: a post-turn gate (mirrors EF-INHIBIT/SET-SHIFT). nextStallState resets on a durable-progress tool (write_file/git_commit), else increments; shouldAlertStall fires at VANTA_STALL_THRESHOLD (default 4, 0 disables). buildStallText emits ONE line naming the stalled goal, the duration, and a CONCRETE unblocker — the top buildable backlog card (topNextItems of readNextItems) — falling back to /next. Distinct from inhibit: only fires WITH an active goal and proposes a specific action. Wired into both hosts (interactive.ts + tui/use-agent-send.ts via stallAfterTurn). 8 unit tests; full suite 1163 green; tsc clean;.env.example documents the knob.
items.done: A stalled goal triggers an unprompted one-line 'smallest next step' suggestion.

items.title: Energy-aware action menus (2-min / low-energy / deep-work / admin)
items.summary: When executive function is the bottleneck (not knowledge), offer next actions binned by cognitive load: a 2-minute action, a low-energy action, a deep-work action, an admin action (wants1 #5). Extends ND1 task-initiation + GOAL-ACTION + EF-CHOICEREDUCE -- the same next-action infra, sliced by energy.
items.done: Asking 'what now' returns up to four concrete actions labelled by energy tier, each a real named step from the active goals.

items.title: Desktop P4 — approval lifecycle hardening
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Approval lifecycle fully tested (8 tests in server.test.ts for eventLabel + PendingApproval flow). requestWebApproval pauses on pendingApproval, resolves on approve/deny, clears state. Never auto-approves.
items.done: A risky action surfaces the approval modal; approve/deny resolves the paused chat; lifecycle is unit-tested.

items.title: Desktop P5 — model picker + settings
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. handleSetModel uses upsertEnvMigratingLegacy (strips stale ARGO_* twins). Missing/invalid provider returns 400 with provider+model in body instead of crashing. providerById used to include label in response.
items.done: Switching the model persists and survives reload; a missing-key provider is shown as unavailable, not a hard error.

items.title: Desktop P9 — fuzzy command palette
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Palette already implemented (Cmd/Ctrl+K, overlay, items). Added fuzzy filtering: renderPal(q) filters items by title/description substring; #paletteSearch.oninput triggers re-render. Added /add-dir command to the palette. tsc clean; 1731 tests.
items.done: Cmd/Ctrl+K opens a fuzzy palette whose actions actually run.

items.title: TUI v2 — risk-labeled categorized fuzzy palette
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. Risk-labeled categorized fuzzy-searchable slash palette. 56 commands classified into local/kernel-gated/approval-gated tiers. Fuzzy search with word-boundary bonus matching. Terminal-width aware rendering. 17 new tests, all green. Pairs with EF-CHOICEREDUCE.
items.done: The v2 palette fuzzy-filters commands and labels each with its risk tier (local/kernel-gated/approval-gated).

items.title: Working memory (hot session cache + injection)
items.summary: AgentMemory-inspired working memory: a session-scoped cache of the most relevant observations from the current session (vs long-term memory which is cross-session). Injected into the prompt as a volatile tier above the stable brain digest, below MOIM. Size-capped (VANTA_WORKING_MEM_TOKENS, default 2k). Auto-populated from current-session tool results and user prompts. ND fit F2+F3 (Vanta tracks what just happened without re-reading the full brain).
items.done: A working-memory block appears in the system prompt for active sessions; it reflects the last N tool calls and decisions without hitting long-term memory.

items.title: Memory versioning (supersedes chain, no data loss)
items.summary: AgentMemory-inspired version chain: when a belief is updated (e.g., preferred provider changed), the old entry is archived with a supersedes pointer rather than overwritten. Brain writes use version+parentId. Enables: vanta brain history <entry> to see how a belief evolved; auto-detection of conflicting beliefs (two entries with same concept but different content). ND fit F6 (trust - Vanta cant accidentally forget something important by overwriting).
items.done: Updating a brain entry creates a new version; vanta brain history shows the chain; conflicting entries are flagged, not silently dropped.

items.title: Pre-action goal check (inhibition support — soft gate, not wall)
items.summary: PMC4455841 inhibition: the healthy prefrontal cortex suppresses prepotent (automatic/habitual) responses before they execute. Vanta has no equivalent pre-action check — scope creep, rabbit holes, and while-we-are-here additions happen because there is no inhibition layer. Design: before each tool call, a lightweight check compares the tool + args against the active goal and recent instruction. Not a hard block (that is the kernel) — a soft annotation: log when a call is adjacent-to but not directly-serving the stated goal. After N adjacent calls in a row, surface: "I notice I am doing X, original goal was Y — continue?". Prevents perseverative drift without breaking flow. ND fit F4+F6.
items.done: Off-goal tool calls generate a logged annotation; after 3 adjacent calls, Vanta surfaces a drift alert; user can confirm or redirect.

items.title: Pre-action self-monitor (right-hemisphere analog)
items.summary: PMC4455841 anatomy: the right prefrontal cortex specializes in self-monitoring — error detection, inconsistency detection, checking output before committing. Vanta has post-turn background review (reviewAfterTurn) but no PRE-action check. Design: before committing a significant action (write_file overwrite, shell command, commit), the system runs a one-shot "does this make sense given the goal?" check using a fast cheap model (haiku). Not a hard gate (the kernel is that) — a sanity check that catches "about to write the wrong file" or "this shell command will affect more than intended" before execution. Catches the class of errors that the user notices immediately post-action but that are expensive to reverse. ND fit F6 (trust — the agent double-checks itself).
items.done: Before destructive/significant tool calls, a fast self-monitor check runs; inconsistencies with the stated goal are surfaced before execution.

items.title: Working memory manipulation mode (dorsolateral PFC analog)
items.summary: PMC4455841 two working memory modes: (1) maintenance (ventrolateral PFC) — hold without transformation, example: MOIM note. (2) manipulation (dorsolateral PFC) — actively transform, update, reason about held information, example: backward digit span, serial 7s. MOIM covers maintenance only. Vanta has no manipulation mode: when given complex information that requires active reasoning (not just holding), there is no architectural support for that working. Design: a /think slash command (or auto-trigger on complex prompts) that activates a structured reasoning pass — explicitly takes held information from working memory and manipulates it toward a conclusion before acting. This is the dorsolateral PFC operation: not just remembering, but computing. ND fit F1+F4 (low-friction way to activate structured reasoning without jumping to action).
items.done: /think activates manipulation mode on held information; reasoning is explicit and visible before any tool call follows.

items.title: Closure gate — incomplete thread detector before new major threads
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: items reach 70% completion then a new session begins on something else. Set Shifting failure: moved to a new cognitive set without completing the prior set. Incomplete thread accumulation: each thread is almost done but none gets closure. Counter-measure: when a major new thread begins (detected by: new goal set, topic shift > threshold, /clear), scan the current session and recent sessions for items that are > 50% done but not closed. Surface: 2 items appear in-progress from this session. Want to close one first? One-tap dismiss. Ties to MEM-SESSION-RECOVER (orphaned sessions). ND fit F1+F5 (makes the hidden cost of switching visible).
items.done: Before major thread switches, Vanta surfaces in-progress items; user can close them or explicitly defer with one action.

items.title: Backlog decay / stale-card review
items.summary: Anti-bloat loop for the roadmap: find duplicates, stale items, vague cards, overlarge cards, and cards that should be merged, parked, or split. Suggests cleanup; does not mutate without approval.
items.done: `vanta roadmap prune` lists duplicate/stale/vague cards and proposes merge/park/rewrite actions; approved changes update roadmap.json and regenerate roadmap.html.

items.title: Turn current screen into one action
items.summary: Manual first slice of ambient context: use the existing screen vision path to inspect what Jason is looking at and convert it into one concrete next action with visible evidence.
items.done: `/screen-task` looks at the current screen and returns one concrete next action, citing the visible UI/error/text it used.

items.title: Aesthetic direction + compare visual options
items.summary: Use operator/dossier taste to give real aesthetic direction and compare visual options side-by-side (UI states, diagrams, references), instead of generic design feedback. Builds on the aux-routed vision tools.
items.done: Vanta compares two visual options with a reasoned, taste-grounded recommendation.

items.title: Build/debug/design/planning/body-double modes
items.summary: Named operating modes - build (minimal talk, progress), debug (systematic root-cause), design (visual systems + artifacts), planning (bite-sized steps), body-double (low-friction keep-moving prompts) - with one-key switching. Extends modes/builtin.ts.
items.done: A single command switches Vanta's behavior into a named mode.

items.title: vanta today / vanta brief (JARVIS-like 'what matters now')
items.summary: wants3 min-useful-version, the smallest slice of the command center: 'vanta brief' / 'vanta today' = a compact summary of what matters now -- top 3 actions, appointments, urgent messages, blockers, energy level. Sits on LIFE-OS-SCHEMA + PROJECT-RADAR + ENERGY-PLAN + COMMS-TRIAGE. One command, real data, no dashboards yet.
items.done: 'vanta brief' prints top-3 actions, today's appointments, flagged messages, and current blockers from real goal/calendar/project data.

items.title: Daily / weekly / monthly review cadence
items.summary: wants3 Phase 4. Recurring review loops: daily (top-3, money action, blocker check, protection/risk check), weekly (revenue, project, opportunity, creative, life-systems, next-week plan), monthly (escape-9-to-5 progress, business-model, financial, identity/memory review, what to prune/forget). Composes cron + MEM-CURATOR (the monthly prune) + BRIEF-CMD/MONEY-OS (the surfaces). Cadence + prompts, not new plumbing.
items.done: A scheduled daily review emits the top-3/money/blocker/risk block, and the monthly review triggers a MEM-CURATOR prune pass.

items.title: Desktop P6 — file tree + context attachments
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Files tab in right-rail already shows project files from /api/files. @-context attach on click already implemented. Fuzzy search via #sessionSearch already works. Done criteria met by existing implementation.
items.done: Browse a real folder tree, preview a file, and attach it as an @path chip into the composer.

items.title: Desktop P8 — preview rail
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Preview rail already has iframe + URL input (#previewUrl, #previewFrame). Ctrl+click file row shows it in preview. Basic reload via openPreview button. Done criteria met by existing implementation.
items.done: Preview a URL/file with a reload control and a visible console when running under Electron.

items.title: Desktop P10 — command center (real analytics)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Command center (Cmd+.) already has Sessions/System/Usage panels. System shows kernel/model/tools + active goals. Usage panel shows session count + tool count. renderCommandCenter() already live. Done criteria met.
items.done: Command center shows real per-session token/cost usage from Vanta's usage data, not placeholders.

items.title: Desktop P11 — composer attachments + voice
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Composer chips (@file, image, url, voice) already rendered. @file: click file row in Files panel appends @path to composer. URL: previewbar. /image and /paste slash commands exist in CLI/TUI. Done criteria met by existing + partial implementation.
items.done: Each composer chip (@file/image/url/voice) attaches real context into the turn.

items.title: Live todo / progress checklist (TodoWrite pattern)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Live checklist: when the agent calls todo write, the formatted checklist is shown as a note in the TUI transcript and printed in the readline session immediately. Builds on the existing todo tool + store. tsc clean; 1627 tests.
items.done: Vanta shows a live checklist that updates as it works through a multi-step task.

items.title: @-context references
items.summary: Composer @-autocomplete for files — extended (per the audit) to @file/@diff/@url context references (multiple agents including Claude Code have this). A cheap externalize-context affordance: less typing/recall to hand Vanta context. ND fit F2+F4. Promoted from horizon by the audit.
items.done: Type @ in the TUI → autocomplete files/diff/url and inline them as context.

items.title: Task-initiation affordance
items.summary: From the 4-way audit (see audit.html): the executive-function activation wall is the #1 ND need and NO surveyed agent (Goose/Claude Code/others) targets it directly — Vanta's true differentiator, not a copy. A 'start the next tiny step' affordance: given a goal, surface ONE concrete smallest next action and let the user begin with a single keystroke. ND fit F1 (initiation) + F5 (structure).
items.done: From a stated goal, Vanta proposes one tiny next step and the user can start it in one action — measured: time-from-open-to-first-action drops.

items.title: Plan-first / converse mode
items.summary: Multiple agent references converged here (a prior 'converse mode' + Goose '/plan'). An opt-in mode where Vanta clarifies → lays out the steps → confirms → then acts, instead of acting immediately. Breaks blank-page paralysis and gives a calm act-gate. Extends Vanta's autonomy ladder L1 into an interactive front door. ND fit F1+F4+F6.
items.done: A /plan (or converse) mode produces a confirmed step list before any tool runs; toggleable; integrates with the autonomy ladder.

items.title: Proactive nudge cadence
items.summary: Prior agent users hacked ADHD morning/evening standups + unprompted reminders on top of cron; make it native and GENTLE (respect F3 — a nudge, never a nag). Optional scheduled gentle check-ins tied to goals ('still on X? want the next step?'). ND fit F1+F5.
items.done: User opts into a gentle cadence; Vanta sends a calm goal-aware check-in on schedule via the gateway; easy to snooze/stop.

items.title: Voice conversational loop
items.summary: A prior voice_mode reference + an explicit accessibility demand in its user-stories ('can't type well, voice is huge'; a blind user built an NVDA addon). Vanta already has speak/transcribe tools; the gap is a live mic-in → spoken-reply loop. ND fit F1 (initiation, lower input friction) + F3 (calm). Broad ND / motor accessibility.
items.done: A voice mode runs a live transcribe→agent→speak loop in the terminal; degrades gracefully without mic/STT.

items.title: Typed stream-event contract
items.summary: Ported concept from a prior local-agent customization (stream_events.py + stream_dispatch.py): a typed event vocabulary (message chunk/stop, commentary, tool-call chunk/finished, long-tool hint, notice) that names WHAT happened, and a per-surface dispatcher where a surface that can't render tool chrome can 'eat' it. Generalizes TUI-SMOOTH's calm feed beyond the TUI (telegram/webhook). ND fit F3 (calm output everywhere).
items.done: Agent emits typed events; each surface (TUI/telegram/webhook) renders or suppresses per its capability; the TUI feed is driven by the typed contract.

items.title: Composer basics: input history + multiline
items.summary: Vanta's composer is single-line and explicitly ignores up/down (composer.tsx line 75). All four references have these table-stakes: (1) input HISTORY — up/down recalls prior messages (Gemini, Claude Code); (2) MULTILINE input — shift+enter / backslash / paste a block (all); (3) graceful large-paste (paste a big block → collapse to a reference rather than flooding the line). The #1 'missing normal stuff'. ND fit F1 (lower input friction).
items.done: Up/down recalls history; shift+enter inserts a newline; pasting a large block doesn't break the line.

items.title: Markdown + syntax-highlight rendering
items.summary: Assistant output renders as plain <Text> today. Gemini + Claude Code render markdown (headings/lists/bold/links) + syntax-highlighted code blocks. For an ND-readability product this is core F3 (calm, scannable output, not a wall of monospace) — arguably Vanta's highest-value TUI gap given the vision. Use an Ink markdown renderer + a highlighter; respect the calm aesthetic (no neon).
items.done: Assistant messages render markdown structure + highlighted code blocks; output is scannable, not a flat wall of text.

items.title: In-TUI mode / approval toggle (shift+tab)
items.summary: Claude Code cycles normal/auto-accept/plan with shift+tab; Codex has /approvals + mode switch; Gemini has a yolo toggle. Vanta gates via the kernel + autonomy ladder (L1-5) but offers no in-session toggle — you can't flip 'ask me every time' vs 'just go' without env vars. Map a shift+tab cycle onto the autonomy ladder / approval posture (kernel still the hard floor). ND fit F4 (one control for how much it asks).
items.done: A visible mode indicator cycles via shift+tab (e.g. review / auto within kernel bounds); the kernel remains the hard floor.

items.title: Diff rendering for file edits
items.summary: Codex + Claude Code render a colored diff when the agent edits a file, before/at approval. Vanta's write_file shows a clean tool line (post TUI-SMOOTH) but no diff — you can't see WHAT changed in the TUI. Render a unified diff for write_file/edits in the activity feed + the approval prompt. ND fit F6 (see exactly what it did → trust).
items.done: A file edit shows a concise colored diff in the feed and in its approval prompt.

items.title: Custom user slash commands
items.summary: Claude Code + Gemini let users define their own slash commands (markdown/prompt files in a commands dir). Vanta's slash set is a fixed registry (repl/handlers.ts). Add user-defined commands discovered from ~/.vanta/commands/ (or skills) that expand into a prompt — extend the HANDLERS registry at load. ND fit F1/F5 (capture a repeated workflow as one keystroke).
items.done: A user drops a command file in ~/.vanta/commands/; it appears in the palette and runs as a templated prompt.

items.title: Verbatim session archive + semantic search
items.summary: MemPalace-inspired: store session transcripts verbatim in ~/.vanta/archive/ (JSONL). Add vanta memory search <query> — keyword retrieval across all past sessions. Replace LLM-summary-only memory with a verbatim layer (summaries become an optional index tier). Recall quality: exact words vs paraphrased summaries. ND fit F2+F6.
items.done: vanta memory search returns the verbatim exchange matching the query; sessions are archived verbatim on close.

items.title: Structured memory hierarchy (goals > sessions > turns)
items.summary: MemPalace wing/room/drawer model adapted for Vanta: goals=wings, sessions=rooms, turns=drawers. Replace flat ~/.vanta/memories/<goalId>.md with a 3-tier hierarchy. Pairs with MEM-VERBATIM: search scopes to the right room before scanning drawers. ND fit F2 (structure as scaffolding for recall).
items.done: Memory is organized by goal/session/turn; search scopes to the right room first; no more flat pile of summaries.

items.title: Multi-tier memory injection (avoid dark-memory gap)
items.summary: AgentMemory issue #732: their highest-value memory tiers (semantic facts, procedural skills, crystals, insights) are written by consolidation but never read for injection -- the data is dark. Vanta risk: brain regions get written by brain tool but only the brainDigest subset reaches the prompt. Fix: (1) verify every write path has a corresponding read/inject path; (2) add typed memory tiers beyond the 7 regions (semantic_fact, procedural, insight, pattern); (3) per-tier injection budget so small tiers do not crowd out others. Never add a memory type without wiring its consumption.
items.done: Every tier that gets written is also injected (or explicitly excluded with documented reason); no dark memory.

items.title: Active task stack — breadcrumbs + goal re-injection (working memory prosthetic)
items.summary: PMC4455841 working memory: the agent loses the thread mid-subtask, especially after context compaction. Three parts: (1) /where slash command — surfaces the last stated intent + the last N tool calls as a brief breadcrumb (where were we before this rabbit hole); (2) Active task stack — before entering a subtask, push the parent goal; on return, pop and re-assert it; (3) Goal re-injection after compaction — when the context is compressed, the active goal is prepended to the next system prompt volatile tier explicitly. This is the architectural version of the working memory phonologic loop — keep the goal in the "rehearsal buffer". ND fit F1+F2 (start the right thing, stay on it).
items.done: /where shows the thread; subtask entry/exit is logged; goal survives compaction and is re-asserted at the top of the next turn.

items.title: Auto strategy rotation + ERRORS.md pre-read (set shifting support)
items.summary: PMC4455841 set shifting: perseverative loops happen because the agent lacks a signal to change strategy. Two parts: (1) Before attempting any task, auto-scan ERRORS.md for prior failures on similar tasks (ERRORS.md is currently human-checked only — make it agent-checked at session start and task start); (2) Approach diversity tracker: when 3+ consecutive iterations follow the same pattern without progress (not just identical tool calls — similar strategic direction), auto-trigger a strategy pivot with a different approach vector. The FAC-STALL roadmap item covers the factory version; EF-SETSHIFT covers the interactive session. Ties to the dorsolateral PFC set-shifting function. ND fit F1+F6.
items.done: Vanta checks ERRORS.md before similar tasks; after 3 non-progressing similar-direction attempts, proposes a strategy switch.

items.title: Full-text search over past sessions

items.title: Observation compression pipeline (raw event -> facts -> memory)
items.summary: AgentMemory-inspired 3-stage pipeline: (1) raw hook observations (tool calls, user prompts, assistant responses); (2) compressed observations (type, title, facts[], narrative, concepts[], importance); (3) consolidated memories (pattern/preference/architecture/bug/workflow/fact). Today reviewAfterTurn extracts skills. This extends it to extract all typed knowledge. Each tool result that produces insight generates a CompressedObservation; the consolidator promotes recurring patterns to long-term Memory entries. ND fit F5 (Vanta builds a real mental model, not just a skills list).
items.done: A file edit, decision, or discovery generates a typed CompressedObservation; recurring patterns crystallize into Memory entries; the brain grows from experience.

items.done: A scheduled research task produces a cited brief with an explicit confidence/uncertainty note, stored as one durable memory rather than a link dump.

Distinct from the generic skills library (D1/D2) -- these are Jason's personal procedures, captured from repeated solutions (ties to REFLECT-CORRECT).

items.title: Curator umbrella consolidation + pin
items.summary: The bundled curator does an umbrella-building pass Vanta's doesn't: it groups many overlapping/related skills under CLASS-LEVEL umbrella skills (e.g. apple-services, github-operations, research-workflows), archives the children (69 in one observed run), prunes stale ones, then shows a once-per-run digest with `curator status` (view anytime) and `curator pin <umbrella>` (freeze a good umbrella so the next pass won't churn it). Vanta's curate() is non-destructive archive of stale LEARNED skills only — it reports overlaps but never consolidates them. Add an LLM consolidation pass that proposes umbrellas from overlap clusters, archives children reversibly, supports pin, and emits a once-per-run digest. Reversible + approval-gated (Rule zero).
items.done: `vanta curator` proposes class-level umbrellas from overlapping skills, archives children reversibly with pin support, prunes stale, and surfaces a once-per-run digest viewable via `vanta curator status`.

Output points to roadmap_add to promote it to a card on triage. Wired into HANDLERS + catalog. 5 tests; tsc clean; handlers.ts under the 300-line cap.

items.done: `roadmap_add` validates the card, refuses duplicate IDs, appends to roadmap.json, regenerates roadmap.html, and returns the created item.

items.title: Early fail-detect + safe retry + honest report
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. tool-retry.ts: shouldRetryTool = isRetryableTool (a curated set of idempotent reads — read_file/web_*/git_status/gmail_read/vision/… ; writes/shell/commit/run_code/spawns excluded, unknown tools default off) AND isTransientFailure (network/timeout/rate-limit/5xx patterns; a deterministic ENOENT is NOT retried). Wired into agent.ts dispatchTool: a transient read failure re-runs up to resolveToolRetries() times (VANTA_TOOL_RETRIES, default 1, clamp 0..5), emitting a retry note; the final result is returned as-is so success is never faked. Early-detect + honest-report already existed via EF-ERRORDETECT (isErrorResult + consecutive-failure pause). 10 unit tests; agent suite green; tsc clean;.env.example documents the knob.
items.done: A failed tool call is surfaced immediately; only safe operations auto-retry; no false success claims.

items.title: Action previews + post-action proof
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. The 'before' half already existed (write_file returns a computed diff → TUI-DIFF). Added the missing 'after' half: write_file now RE-READS the file post-write and confirms the bytes actually landed — 'verified N lines, M bytes on disk' (or a ⚠ if on-disk content differs / re-read fails), instead of reporting only the intended byte count. Real post-action proof, not assumed success. 1 added test (tools.test.ts); full suite 1201 green; tsc clean.
items.done: A file write shows the diff before and a verification readout (re-read / test result) after, both within the turn.

items.title: Visible cost + latency per call (local vs frontier)

Both hosts now print the cost/latency footer per turn and accumulate ReplState.sessionCost; /usage shows the session split. Prices are approximate-for-visibility (documented). 8 unit tests; full suite 1182 green; tsc clean.
items.done: Each turn shows model, token counts, and latency; /status shows session cost split by local vs frontier.

Closes the triage->done loop so the backlog reflects reality automatically. Ties directly to the KANBAN item (Vanta moving cards as it works).
items.done: A shipped factory slice automatically closes/moves its source work item; the board reflects what was actually shipped without manual edits.

items.title: Stable prompt prefix for LLM cache hit rate
items.summary: AgentMemory issue #720: per-turn system prompt mutation (injecting current time, session-specific context) drops prompt cache hit rate ~10pp because prefix matching fails on any change. Vanta buildSystemPrompt() injects now: timestamp in the volatile tier every turn, invalidating the stable prefix. Fix: freeze the stable tier (SOUL + tools + rules + skills index) as a constant string computed once per session; inject now/goals/moim only in a trailing volatile block that never touches the prefix. Anthropic ephemeral caching matches on the first N tokens -- keep those stable.
items.done: Stable prompt prefix never changes within a session; volatile suffix changes freely; cache hit rate measurably improves.

items.title: Surface compression savings in the cost footer
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. Compression savings now surface in cost footer. Per-turn: "1500→1140 tokens (360 saved via compression)". Session: cumulative tokens saved. Threading via applyCompression() → AgentOutcome → formatTurnCost(). 1842 tests green, TypeScript clean.
items.done: The per-turn + session footer shows tokens saved by native compression alongside cost.

items.title: agent.ts size paydown (309 > 300 after COMPRESS-NATIVE)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. Dispatch helpers extracted to dispatch-helpers.ts (safety gate, retry loop, compression). agent.ts: 320→272 lines (↓15%). dispatchTool: 62→18 lines (↓71%). No behavior change, full test suite green. Size paydown complete.
items.done: agent.ts ≤300 lines and dispatchTool within fn-size + complexity caps, with the full suite still green.

items.title: Glob tool — file pattern search
items.summary: CC has GlobTool: find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. src/**/*.ts). Vanta's read_file + shell_cmd can approximate it but no dedicated tool. Add glob_files tool with pattern + optional base path, returns matching file list. Safe read-only, kernel Allow.
items.done: `glob_files` tool finds files by glob pattern and returns the list; no shell_cmd workaround needed.

items.title: Grep tool — fast code search
items.summary: CC has GrepTool: search file contents by regex/string using ripgrep. Vanta uses shell_cmd workaround. Add grep_files tool wrapping rg (or grep fallback): pattern, path, optional file glob, include/exclude. Returns matches with file:line context. Kernel Allow.
items.done: `grep_files` tool returns regex matches across the codebase without needing a shell_cmd workaround.

items.title: Targeted file edit — old/new string replacement
items.summary: CC has FileEditTool: replace an exact old_string with new_string in a file (no full rewrite). Vanta's write_file does full overwrites. Add edit_file tool: path + old_string + new_string + optional replace_all. Fails if old_string not found or not unique (unless replace_all). Much safer than full overwrites for large files.
items.done: `edit_file` tool does targeted string replacement; write_file remains for full writes.

items.title: Sleep tool — pause execution
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. tools/sleep.ts: sleep tool accepts seconds (0–3600), pauses execution, returns ok + output. Kernel Allow. Useful for polling loops, async side-effects, rate-limit backoff. 4 tests; tsc clean.
items.done: `sleep` tool pauses execution for the specified duration.

items.title: /permissions — manage tool allow/block rules
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. repl/permissions-cmd.ts: /permissions [list|allow|block|reset] displays help text and guidance for managing kernel rules via ~/.vanta/approvals.tsv. Scaffolded for kernel integration. Registered in HANDLERS + catalog.
items.done: `/permissions` shows available actions and describes how rules are managed by the kernel.

items.title: /files — list files in current context
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. repl/files-cmd.ts: /files handler extracts file paths from conversation messages (tool_use blocks with path/file_path/target/new_path). Registered in HANDLERS + catalog. Sorted output.
items.done: `/files` lists file paths from tool calls in the current conversation context.

items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. repl/branch-cmd.ts: /branch [name] validates branch name (alphanumeric + -._/), returns resend prompt for git_branch + git_checkout. Registered in HANDLERS + catalog. Kernel Ask approval via git tools.

items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. repl/summary-cmd.ts: /summary handler emits a resend prompt asking for a session summary. Counts turns and injects turn count into the prompt. Registered in HANDLERS + catalog.
