items.title: Be a server (serve)
items.summary: vanta mcp serve — exposes Vanta tools over MCP stdio, kernel-gated + read-only allowlist. console.log→stderr keeps the protocol stream clean.
items.done: Live-verified: real McpClient drove vanta mcp serve subprocess — handshake, tools/list, allow→execute, out-of-scope refused, non-allowlisted refused. Claude Code integration untested.

items.title: Cohesive brain — one unit composing regions + structured scored memory
items.summary: Consolidate the brain into one cohesive unit (brain/brain.ts facade of small functions): the md regions stay the auditable layer; the dormant brain5d store and neuro 12-axis scoring are rebuilt into brain/entries.ts (typed, strength-scored, decay-aware, contradiction-penalized, crystallizing on retrieval). One composed brainDigest in the prompt; brain tool gains remember/recall (recall reinforces); per-layer best-effort so a broken layer degrades instead of breaking her; corrupt store quarantined, legacy brain5d.json migrated. v2 self-evolving substrate stays parked.
items.done: brainDigest injects regions + top structured memories; brain remember/recall round-trips and reinforces; corrupt entries.json degrades to regions-only and is quarantined; brain5d.ts/neuro.ts deleted (absorbed); full suite green.

items.title: Brain association — auto-linked memories + spreading-activation recall
items.summary: Ideas connect like a brain: memories auto-link to their most similar neighbors at write time (zero-dep token-overlap similarity, sparse capped graph), and recall is spreading activation — direct hits surface their linked neighbors at damped strength (primed, not reinforced). A relevance floor keeps one-word-overlap noise out of direct hits; weak matches arrive only via association.
items.done: remember() wires bidirectional relatedIds; recall(query) returns direct hits plus ↪-marked associated neighbors ranked below them; direct hits reinforce, associations do not; tests prove linking, spreading, damping, and noise floor.

items.title: Brain consolidation — gist merging, decay sweep, hard entry budget
items.summary: The sleep pass: near-duplicate memories in a region merge into one stronger gist (strength/links/reinforcement carried, marked crystallized), decayed entries fall away, and a hard budget (VANTA_BRAIN_MAX_ENTRIES, default 400) drops only the weakest-scored — entries.json stays bounded on disk forever. Links healed after every pass (absorbed ids remapped, dangling dropped). Auto-runs when over budget during the session digest. Scoring also now counts ACCESS recency: a recalled memory stays warm, not just a re-written one.
items.done: Duplicate merge carries strength+retrievals+links and remaps references; consolidate enforces the budget weakest-first with healed links; maybeConsolidate fires only over budget from brainDigest; recalled-recently outranks untouched at equal strength; tests cover all of it.

items.title: Brain auto-learning — grows with the user, forms her own personality
items.summary: After a busy turn (or every Nth), a background pass distils the conversation into 0-3 durable memories and remembers them: the user behaviors/preferences/EF patterns (where they stall, what unblocks them, step sizing — ND-first, which helps everyone) into user_model; facts into semantic; events into episodic; and observations about Vanta OWN forming personality into identity/reflections. Re-learning strengthens (upsert), association links it, consolidation keeps it bounded. Both hosts surface a learned line. VANTA_BRAIN_LEARN[_EVERY|_MIN_TOOLS]; on by default, best-effort, never blocks a turn.
items.done: A busy turn writes inference-sourced entries into the brain unprompted; identity/reflections receive personality observations; re-learned memories strengthen instead of duplicating; malformed model output writes nothing; hosts print learned-line; tests cover gating/parse/roundtrip.

items.title: Turn-abort hardening — transcripts self-heal, kernel-down fails closed gracefully
items.summary: Incident fix (ERRORS.md 2026-06-11): a turn aborting mid-dispatch (kernel death, network drop) left a dangling assistant tool_call that 400d every later request — bricking the session and its saved file. sanitizeMessages now synthesizes stub results for dangling calls (heals new aborts AND existing bricked sessions on resume); applySafetyGate returns a graceful blocked result when the kernel is unreachable instead of throwing through the loop; approval-queue bookkeeping is best-effort.
items.done: A kernel-down tool call yields a blocked result and the turn continues; a transcript with a dangling call gets a stub and the next request succeeds; tests cover both + no-duplicate-stub.

items.title: TUI scroll keys — shift-arrows fine scroll + clearer hint
items.summary: Alt-screen has no native scrollback, so keys are the only scroll. Added shift+up/down single-entry scroll alongside pgup/pgdn (fn+arrows on Mac); hint now reads (shift-arrows/pgup scroll). Composer already has opt+backspace kill-word and ctrl+U kill-to-start; real cmd+backspace requires a terminal mapping (documented).
items.done: shift-arrow scrolls the virtual viewport by one entry in alt-screen; hint shows the keys; existing pgup/pgdn unchanged.

items.title: Loop engine — first-class loops: trigger + stages + rubric + stop rules
items.summary: The operator designs loops; Vanta runs them. A .vanta/loops/ registry of durable loop objects: goal, trigger (cron/heartbeat/event), discover-plan-execute-evaluate-improve stages, eval rubric, stop rules (pass condition / max iterations / no-progress). vanta loop add/list/run/pause/kill. Executes on the existing gateway tick; subsumes ad-hoc standing-loop prompts. From the June-2026 loops-over-prompting shift (playbook + CC dynamic workflows).
items.done: vanta loop add registers a loop that wakes on its trigger, runs its stages, evaluates against its rubric, and stops on its stop rule — surviving restarts.

items.title: Loop state — durable per-loop memory across wakes
items.summary: Each loop owns persistent state: last-run, in-progress items, escalations (only a human clears), lessons (append-only). Read at wake, written at exit, re-injected after compaction — kills goal drift and re-derivation. Steals loop-state + paperclip checkpoint + gstack retro.
items.done: A loop resumes mid-list after a restart; escalations persist until cleared; lessons accumulate.

items.title: Loop gates + budgets — objective advance gates, kill switches, accepted-rate ledger
items.summary: Engine-enforced (not prompt-promised): a gate command must exit 0 (or health score over floor) before a loop advances/commits; budgets per loop (max iterations, wall-clock, tokens); no-progress kill after 3 zero-delta wakes; accepted-change-rate ledger so a losing loop is visible. loop.killed events. Steals ship-preflight, hill-climb stop rules, terminal-bench-loop, gstack health score.
items.done: A failing gate blocks advancement; a loop with 3 zero-delta wakes halts with a named blocker; budgets enforce.
items.note: Shipped 2026-06-11: maxWallMs/maxTokens/healthScoreFloor/minAcceptRate/logKilled — pure StopRules extensions + acceptance-rate ledger in LoopState. 2437 TS tests green, tsc clean.

items.title: Native verification primitives — adversarial fan-out, tournament, generate-filter
items.summary: The maker never grades its own work: checker subagents run isolated from the maker context with a refute posture and evidence requirements (N skeptics, majority rule); tournament (generate N, judge by rubric, pick winner) and generate-filter as engine primitives any loop stage can invoke. Steals adversarial-verify + content-engine tournament + playbook eval gates.
items.done: A loop stage can declare verify: adversarial(n=3) or tournament(n=5, rubric) and the engine runs isolated judges.
items.note: Shipped 2026-06-11: adversarial fan-out (n skeptics, majority-refute), tournament (n candidates + judge scoring, pick winner), generate-filter (n candidates + binary pass/fail predicate). Stage.verify field in StageSchema. runStageWithVerify wired into runStages. 2454 tests green, tsc clean.

items.title: Scoped wakes — wake reason + delta context instead of full history
items.summary: Gateway wakes inject {wake_reason, goal_id, approval_id?, since} so a loop reads only what changed since last wake; resolved approvals become first-class wake triggers reviewed before other work. Cuts long-loop context cost sharply. Steals the paperclip heartbeat model.
items.done: A cron/webhook wake carries its reason + delta; an approval resolution wakes the owning loop first.

items.title: Goal dependency graph — blocked_by/blocks + auto-start scheduler
items.summary: Goals declare blocked_by/blocks; the scheduler auto-starts any goal whose blockers are done and holds blocked ones. Parallel branches fall out of the graph instead of hand-coded fan-out. Steals paperclip plan-to-tasks decomposition.
items.done: Completing a blocker auto-wakes its dependents; vanta goals shows the graph state.

items.title: Decision classifier — mechanical vs taste vs user-challenge HITL routing
items.summary: Before any HITL gate: classify the decision. Mechanical (one right answer) auto-decides silently; taste (viable alternatives) auto-decides but surfaces at the final gate; user-challenge (overriding the operator stated direction) NEVER auto-decides. Stops loops from over-asking and over-assuming at once. Steals autoplan decision classification.
items.done: Loop checkpoints route by class; user-challenge decisions always reach the operator; taste decisions batch at the final gate.

items.title: Operator profile — declared vs inferred autonomy preferences + drift detection
items.summary: A declared profile (autonomy level, scope appetite, detail, risk tolerance) beside an inferred one built from observed overrides; drift over threshold surfaces a one-line flag. Per-question ask-preferences (never-ask / always-ask / ask-only-one-way); one-way doors override never-ask unconditionally. Lives in the brain user_model region (rides BRAIN-AUTOLEARN). Steals plan-tune.
items.done: Approval prompts respect per-question preferences; declared-vs-inferred drift is flagged; one-way doors always ask.

items.title: Per-project learnings index — typed insights with staleness + contradiction checks
items.summary: Loop/session completions log typed learnings (pattern / pitfall / preference) scoped to the project; top-K inject at session start; staleness (referenced file gone) and contradiction (same key, opposing insight) detection keeps it honest. Implemented as project-scoped brain entries — reuses entries + consolidation built 2026-06-11. Steals gstack learn.
items.done: A session starts with the 3 most relevant project learnings; stale/conflicting entries get flagged.

items.title: Velocity + closure tracking — captures:ships ratio and finish-before-start, cross-session
items.summary: Native rolling-7-day captured-vs-shipped counters and completion-percent tracking so the closure gate can truly say two items are over 50 percent done — finish one first. The EF gates exist as prompts; the counters they need only the engine can keep. ND-first; helps everyone. Steals nd-velocity-check + nd-closuregate.
items.done: Registering a new goal when ratio exceeds 5:1 surfaces the ratio + top unfinished items, cross-session.

items.title: Kernel liveness watchdog — every loop/goal in a named state, stalls surfaced
items.summary: Kernel-side state machine: every running loop/goal must terminate each tick in live / waiting / blocked / terminal; violations (silent stall, unbounded in-progress, recursive recovery) surface as events and halt further spawns until resolved. The hard backstop under all loop autonomy. Steals diagnose-why-work-stopped. Kernel work — sequenced after the engine proves out.
items.done: A silently-stalled loop is detected and surfaced within one tick; runaway spawn recursion is halted by the kernel.

items.title: Rubric engine — weighted per-category rubrics with confidence-weighted scoring
items.summary: GRAPE distilled (Patel/Meta 2025): per-task-category rubrics of weighted items, each VERIFIABLE (scored against ground truth/checks) or non-verifiable (atomized into verifiable parts + a critic prompt). Every judge returns reasoning + score + CONFIDENCE; aggregation is confidence-weighted, and a persistently low-confidence rubric item is flagged as a bad item for operator review. Upgrades LOOP-VERIFY from flat rubric to a self-auditing scoring system.
items.done: A loop stage scores candidates against a category rubric; verifiable items run checks, non-verifiable run critics; aggregate is confidence-weighted; weak rubric items get flagged.
items.note: Shipped 2026-06-11: GRAPE-distilled confidence-weighted scoring. RubricItem gains kind+critiquePrompt. parseJudgment(SCORE+CONFIDENCE+REASONING). scoreByRubric: Promise.all over items, confidence*weight aggregation, weakItems flagged at <0.3 confidence. Wired into evaluate stage in runStages — rubric score replaces parseScore when items present. 2462 tests green, tsc clean.

items.title: Group-relative evaluation — advantage over the batch mean, not absolute thresholds
items.summary: GRPO/GRAPE advantage at the agent level: judge each candidate RELATIVE to the group mean of its batch/category rather than an absolute pass bar — robust when a category is systematically hard or easy, and removes the need for hand-tuned thresholds in tournaments.
items.done: Tournament/judge primitives expose relative scoring; the winner is the max-advantage candidate; ties to RUBRIC-ENGINE aggregation.
items.note: relativeAdvantage() in verify.ts; tournamentVerify winner = max-advantage with batch-mean in reason; 4 new tests

items.title: Critique-reuse improve stage — judge reasoning feeds the next generation
items.summary: GRAPE feature made native: the evaluate stage emits critique + reasoning per candidate; the improve stage consumes that critique to produce the next iteration (improver prompt = response + critique -> improved response). Iterations mix fresh generation with critique-driven improvement. Closes the loop the playbook calls evaluate -> improve with concrete plumbing.
items.done: A loop iteration after evaluate receives the structured critique; the improved candidate measurably consumes it; fresh-vs-improve mix is configurable.
items.note: critiqueDriven: boolean on Stage; parseReasoning extracts REASONING: lines from evaluate output; runner injects critique as ## critique section into critiqueDriven stage prior; default 5-stage loop sets improve.critiqueDriven=true; 8 new tests (4 parseReasoning + 2 integration)

items.title: Preference capture — accept/reject/edit decisions become chosen-vs-rejected pairs
items.summary: DPO-style data from daily use, no training required yet: approvals granted/denied, /retry, /undo, edits to Vanta output, tournament winners — each captured as a preference pair (chosen vs rejected, with context) in ~/.vanta/preferences.jsonl. Feeds OPERATOR-PROFILE inference and the brain user_model immediately, and accumulates a fine-tune-ready dataset for PERSONAL-MODEL-TUNE later.
items.done: Operator decisions persist as preference pairs with provenance; profile inference reads them; export to JSONL works.

items.title: Best-of exemplar library — winners become few-shot context for similar tasks
items.summary: Rejection sampling at the context level: tournament/loop winners are stored as exemplars (brain entries, sourceType crystallized) and the top-K similar exemplars inject as few-shot context when a similar task arrives — the model behaves better without weight updates. Rides the brain association layer for similarity matching.
items.done: A won tournament stores an exemplar; a later similar task gets it as few-shot; recall reinforcement strengthens used exemplars.

items.title: Process supervision — verify intermediate stages, not just final output
items.summary: PRMs-over-ORMs distilled (Lightman 2023 via the RLMT paper): a correct final artifact can hide broken reasoning. Loop gates extend to intermediate stages — plan quality, step soundness, constraint adherence — so credit/blame lands on the step that earned it and flawed-logic-lucky-outcome runs get caught.
items.done: A loop can declare per-stage gates; a failing intermediate stage blocks advancement with the step named, even if the final output looks right.
items.note: already implemented by LOOP-GATES-BUDGETS + LOOP-VERIFY: Stage.gate (shell cmd) + Stage.verify (adversarial/tournament/filter) apply to every stage; gateFailedAt carries the intermediate stage name; test at runner.test.ts 'gate failure' describe block confirms

items.title: Personal model tuning — local LoRA from accumulated preference data
items.summary: The real-training path, when the data exists: export the PREFERENCE-SIGNALS dataset (SFT pairs + DPO preferences) and fine-tune a small local model (Ollama/MLX on the M4 Pro) into a personal model that bakes in learned voice, preferences, and patterns. GRAPE-style rubric scoring doubles as the eval harness for tune quality. Local-first, private, hers.
items.done: vanta tune exports the dataset and runs/validates a local LoRA; the tuned model is selectable as a provider and beats the base on the personal rubric eval.

items.title: Cockpit loop panel — live loops + escalations in the kernel dashboard
items.summary: The kernel cockpit (src/server.rs INDEX) has panels for goals/approvals/assess but none for the .vanta/loops/ registry shipped in LOOP-ENGINE/LOOP-STATE. Add a Loops panel: each loop row shows id, status, trigger, iterations, last score, in-progress flag, and open escalations; controls to clear an escalation (human-clear-only — the cockpit is a legitimate human surface) and pause/resume/kill a loop. Backed by new kernel endpoints GET /api/loops (read), POST /api/loops/clear|pause|resume|kill. Read path is an open call: the kernel (zero-dep Rust) can read .vanta/loops/*.json directly the way it reads goals.tsv/approvals.tsv, but the JSON is nested — decide hand-rolled parse vs. a thin TS-served proxy panel. Mirrors the existing approvals/goals panel pattern and their refresh-on-action UX.
items.done: The cockpit renders a Loops panel listing each .vanta/loops/ entry with status/trigger/iterations/last-score and open escalations; a human can clear an escalation and pause/resume/kill a loop from the page; backed by /api/loops* endpoints; loops with no escalations and paused/active states render correctly.

items.title: Cockpit redesign — self-explaining boundary dashboard
items.summary: Rewrote the kernel cockpit (src/server.rs INDEX) around the question every panel must answer: what is this for? Header states what the kernel IS + live status dot polling /api/status. Hero = Test the boundary: one action input, three clickable examples teaching the three verdicts, verdict rendered as a color chip (ALLOW mint / ASK amber / BLOCK red) with a plain-English meaning line + kernel reason — no more raw JSON dumps. Approvals panel finally exposes the per-row approve/deny endpoints as buttons; pending is first-class, decided history collapsed to last 5 dimmed with a count. Goals get add input + per-row Done. Bridge demoted to a footer status line. Zero-dep inline HTML/CSS/JS, data rendered via textContent (no innerHTML injection), focus-visible outlines, aria-live regions, labeled inputs.
items.done: Cockpit explains itself: verdict tester with examples + color-coded chip; approvals show pending with working approve/deny buttons and collapsed history; goals add/complete inline; live status strip; verified by headless-browser screenshot against the running kernel.

items.title: Full-feature demo (Claude-Code grammar) — TUI north-star + feature tracker
items.summary: docs/design-refs/vanta-full-demo.html — every subsystem in Claude Code's CLI grammar (✻ welcome · ⏺ bullets · ⎿ results · rounded input) in Vanta's cyan palette. 10 tabs: session, commands(75), kernel, goals, skills+loops, memory, models, tools(67), web/vision, comms/agents/mcp. The visual spec the live TUI is built toward; each tab maps to the cards below.
items.done: Demo opens; all 10 tabs render the real inventory; each surface is a tracked roadmap card with a status.

items.title: Claude-style approval menu — numbered, arrow-selectable, with always-allow
items.summary: Replaced the horizontal [a]·[A]·[d] hint with a vertical numbered menu (Do you want to proceed? ❯1 Yes / 2 Yes,don't ask again / 3 No) — ↑↓ select, Enter, 1-3 quick, Esc deny. Option 2 persists a tool-scoped allow rule (grantAlways→permissions) so future asks auto-confirm; kernel block stays immovable. ui/approval-prompt.tsx + ui/grant.ts.
items.done: Out-of-root/system actions surface the numbered menu; always-allow writes a permissions rule; 108 ui tests green.

items.title: Rounded-border input box (Claude shape) replacing bare ─ rules
items.summary: Composer was two horizontal Rule lines around the input; now a single round-bordered Box with a > prompt — Claude Code's signature input shape. ui/composer.tsx.
items.done: Composer renders as a rounded box; prompt is >; ui tests green.

items.title: Truecolor hex default theme matching the design reference
items.summary: Default theme used ANSI color names, so the render inherited each terminal's washed-out palette. Switched to exact hex (cyan #56c8db, gold #e0b341, etc.) so the TUI matches the design refs on any truecolor terminal; light terminals still fall back to high-contrast. term/theme.ts.
items.done: resolveTheme returns hex; theme tests assert the hex values; renders identically across truecolor terminals.

items.title: Anti-ghosting: commit streamed text before a tool call
items.summary: Reducer only committed assistant text on turnEnd, so a long preamble followed by an approval-gated tool held the full text in the redrawing live region — every frame leaked into scrollback (~40 copies). Now text commits to <Static> the moment a tool call follows it (commitText/flush). ui/reducer.ts. Logged ERRORS.md.
items.done: Streamed-text-then-tool no longer ghosts; reducer tests cover the path; 110 ui tests green.

items.title: Clean responses — drop the Goal/Expected preamble + per-turn token dump
items.summary: Prompt rule 1 told the model to PRINT which goal a tool serves + what it expects, producing a verbose Goal:/Expected: block before every action (contradicting rule 10a). Made the goal discipline internal, not printed. Also removed the per-turn N tok·N in·N out note (context already lives in the status bar). prompt.ts + ui/use-agent.ts.
items.done: Responses read clean like Claude Code; no preamble, no token dump; prompt + ui tests green.

items.title: Main-session chrome — active-goal line · compact PLAN bar · status chips
items.summary: Demo tab 1 shows chrome the live TUI lacks: an active-goal line under the banner, a compact PLAN ▰▰▱▱ bar (vs the multi-line todo panel), and richer status chips (elapsed timer · MCP ✓ · kernel verdict). Mostly real data (kernel goal + todos in state). NOTE: the demo's autonomy line is intentionally NOT built — conflicts with Rule Zero.
items.done: Active-goal line (◇), compact PLAN bar (▰ meter + ✓◐○ counts), AND the status chips: a live 1 Hz session timer (◷ Xm YYs via useClock) + MCP ✓ chip (one-shot readMcpConfig at mount). Both are status-bar segments that drop first when the terminal narrows.

items.title: Rich /goals ledger view — active/done + plan + working memory
items.summary: Demo tab 4: /goals is currently a plain list overlay; build the styled ledger (active ● / done ✓ goals, plan-for-active with progress, working-memory block) shown in the demo. Backed by the kernel goals API + todo state.
items.done: /goals now renders the demo ledger: active goals (●) before done (✓), each with status + #id, under a Goal ledger header (formatGoalLedger, pure + tested). Was a flat active-only [id] text list.

items.title: Styled /sessions + /model pickers (vs plain list overlays)
items.summary: Demo tabs 6-7: /sessions and /model render via the generic list overlay; upgrade to the styled pickers in the demo (mark glyphs, key chips, current-● indicators, advisor/vision/routes footer). /skills already shipped (CC-SKILLS-MENU).
items.done: OverlayRow gained a `mark` field rendered in its own column (● current) — separate from the ❯ selection cursor; model + theme pickers mark the current entry instead of baking › into the label. overlays.test + render tests green.

items.title: Render tool calls as ⏺ Tool(args) / ⎿ result (Claude grammar)
items.summary: Demo renders each tool call Claude-style: ⏺ Read(src/x.ts) then ⎿ Read 48 lines. The live TUI groups a run under one ⏺ header (⏺ read, wrote · 2 actions). Track switching to (or offering) the per-call Claude grammar. Design choice — evaluate against the grouped header.
items.done: Tool calls render Claude-style: ⏺ Verb(detail) over a dim ⎿ result, no group header; render tests updated; ui suite green.

items.title: Blinking composer cursor — the alive/ready idle cue
items.summary: A blinking inverse-block cursor in the input box (empty + typing states), driven by a self-contained useBlink(~530ms) hook — the canonical terminal ready signal Claude has and Vanta lacked. Composes with the 1 Hz session clock so the TUI feels alive idle, not frozen.
items.done: Cursor blinks in both empty and typing states; useBlink toggle is tested; 111 ui tests green.

items.title: Shift+Tab autonomy mode cycle — normal / auto-accept / plan (Claude parity)
items.summary: Shift+Tab cycles normal → auto-accept → plan → normal with a ▶▶/⏸ indicator above the composer. Auto-accept auto-approves the kernel Ask tier (writes/out-of-root) while the Block tier (destructive/exfiltration) stays kernel-refused upstream — safe by construction. Plan reuses the real /planmode enforcement (write+shell blocked until /planmode approve). Reverses the earlier Rule-Zero deferral: the hard floor is intact because only Asks reach the prompt.
items.done: Shift+Tab cycles modes; auto-accept resolves Ask approvals without prompting; plan toggles /planmode; ModeLine indicator renders; cycleMode + ModeLine tested; 115 ui tests green.

items.title: Loops dashboard — live loops + escalations (TUI)
items.summary: /loops opens a read-only dashboard over the real loop store: each loop with a status glyph (▶ active/in-progress · ⏸ paused/escalated · ✓ done · ✗ killed), goal, iter + score, and an open-escalation badge. listLoopSummaries composes listDefs + loadState; no backend invented — the loop engine already persists def+state under .vanta/loops.
items.done: /loops renders the live loop state; listLoopSummaries + LoopsPanel tested; integrated as an overlay kind.

items.title: Dive-into-Claude-Code — design-space reference
items.summary: docs/research/dive-into-claude-code.md — extract of arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code): 5 values, 13 design principles (Table 1), 7-component + 5-layer architecture, OpenClaw contrast, 6 future directions. The design-space map Vanta is read against; the PAPER-* cards trace to it.
items.done: The summary doc exists; PAPER-* cards reference it.

items.title: Self-audit Vanta against the 13 Claude Code design principles
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) Table 1 maps 13 principles to design questions. Score Vanta against each (deny-first, graduated trust, defense-in-depth, externalized policy, context-as-scarce-resource, append-only state, minimal-scaffolding, values-over-rules, composable extensibility, reversibility-weighted risk, transparent file config, isolated subagents, graceful recovery) — find the real gaps, not assumed ones.
items.done: docs/research/vanta-design-audit.md — Vanta scored vs all 13 principles (8 HAVE / 4 PARTIAL / 1 GAP). Surfaces the impact-ranked backlog: #10 reversibility (GAP, needs sign-off), #5 context pipeline (biggest PARTIAL), #12 subagent isolation, #4 hooks.

items.title: Reversibility-weighted risk in the kernel — lighter gate for reversible actions
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) Table 1 reversibility-weighted risk: Claude Code gives reversible/read-only actions lighter oversight, irreversible ones heavier. Vanta assess() is allow/ask/block by keyword+scope only. Add a reversibility dimension so read-only/reversible ops lean allow and irreversible ones (delete/overwrite/push/migrate) escalate — without loosening the Block floor.
items.done: assess() factors reversibility; reversible ops get a lighter verdict, irreversible escalate; Block stays immovable.

items.title: Experiential memory tier — an evolving cross-session playbook
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §12.2 + the evolving-playbook idea: between static CLAUDE.md and transient transcripts sits a durable, auto-curated tier of reusable strategies learned across sessions. Vanta has brain (semantic/episodic/user_model); add/wire an experiential playbook that accumulates + curates procedures across runs and re-injects them on relevant tasks.
items.done: A cross-session playbook store accumulates reusable strategies and is injected on matching tasks.

items.title: Generator-evaluator separation + tool-call trace anomaly detection
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §12.1: silent mistakes dominate (78% of failures invisible); agents confidently praise mediocre work. Close the observability-evaluation gap with harness scaffolding: separate the generator from an independent evaluator, post-hoc verification, and anomaly detection over tool-call traces (pass^k). Vanta has self-monitor + /verify + advisor; formalize the split + a trace-anomaly pass.
items.done: A turn can be independently scored by a separate critic; anomalous tool-call traces are flagged.

items.title: Five-layer graduated compaction pipeline
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §3.6 / Table 6: Claude Code runs 5 sequential compaction shapers cheapest-first, cache-aware, with read-time projection (budget reduction, snip, microcompact, context collapse, auto-compact). Vanta has compress/CCR. Deepen to an ordered multi-layer pipeline; each layer a different cost/benefit, full history reconstructable.
items.done: Context pressure is managed by ordered shapers (cheap to costly), reversible, history reconstructable.

items.title: Deferred tool schemas — names first, full schema on demand
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §3.6 ToolSearch: tools expose only names initially; full schemas load on demand, saving context across many tools. Vanta has tool_search + mount_mcp; carry tool names + 1-line descriptions in the base prompt and fetch full schemas when a tool is selected — frees context for Vanta 65 tools.
items.done: Tool schemas load on demand; the base system prompt carries only names + summaries.

items.title: Optional ML auto-mode classifier as an advisory tighten-only stage
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §3.5 yoloClassifier: a two-stage ML classifier (fast filter + chain-of-thought) evaluates tool safety beyond rules. Vanta kernel is deterministic by design. Add an OPTIONAL advisory classifier that can only ESCALATE a verdict (allow to ask/block), never loosen — catches what keywords miss while keeping the deterministic floor. Off by default.
items.done: An optional classifier can tighten a kernel allow to ask/block, never loosen; disabled by default.

items.title: Optional shell sandbox — restrict fs/network for approved commands
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §3.5 shouldUseSandbox: approved shell may still run in a sandbox restricting filesystem + network. Vanta gates shell via the kernel but runs approved commands unsandboxed. Add an optional macOS sandbox (sandbox-exec/seatbelt) mode for shell_cmd.
items.done: A VANTA_SHELL_SANDBOX mode runs approved shell commands with fs/network restrictions.

items.title: Subagent sidechain transcripts + summary-only return
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §8: subagent conversations are stored in separate sidechain files; only a summary returns to the parent, preventing context inflation. Vanta has delegate/swarm; ensure summary-only return and persist full subagent transcripts to .vanta sidechain files for audit.
items.done: delegate/swarm return only a summary; full subagent transcripts persist to .vanta sidechain files.

items.title: Focus-aware proactive heartbeat (KAIROS) with economic throttling
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §11.6 KAIROS: a persistent background agent injects tick prompts when idle and decides act-or-sleep, bound to terminal-focus awareness (act when the user is away) + token economics (cache expires ~5min, so sleep/wake is a cost optimization). Vanta has loops + ScheduleWakeup; add focus-aware, cost-throttled proactivity.
items.done: An idle Vanta can proactively pick up queued work when the user is away, throttled by a cost/cadence policy.

items.title: Regulator-facing audit export (EU AI Act-aware)
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §12.5: emerging regulation (EU AI Act fully applicable Aug 2026) constrains logging/transparency/oversight. Vanta has events.jsonl; add an externally-auditable export of the action log — every gated action, its verdict, who decided, resolution — in a regulator-facing format.
items.done: An audit export summarizes every gated action + verdict + resolution for external review.

items.title: Long-term human-capability preservation as a first-class surface
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §12.6 + the vibe-check protocol: overreliance atrophies the skills needed to supervise the agent (comprehension -17%); treat the sustainability gap as a design problem, not a metric. For Vanta trusted-operator + ND angle: comprehension-preserving surfaces — explain-before-act on request, periodic do-you-follow-this probes on risky/large changes, cognitive-offloading awareness.
items.done: An optional mode surfaces what changed + why in human-graspable terms and probes understanding on risky/large changes.

items.title: Map the hook engine against the 27 Claude Code event types
items.summary: arXiv:2604.14228 (Dive into Claude Code) §3.3/§6: Claude Code hook pipeline spans 27 event types (5 safety, 22 lifecycle/orchestration). Vanta has a shell-hooks engine; audit coverage vs the 27 and add the high-value missing lifecycle events.
items.done: Vanta hook event catalog is mapped against the 27; valuable missing events are added.

items.title: Anatomy-of-an-Agent-Harness — practitioner reference
items.summary: docs/research/anatomy-of-an-agent-harness.md — extract of The Anatomy of an Agent Harness (Pachaar, 2026): the 12 harness components, the Ralph Loop, the 7 design decisions, co-evolution / future-proofing. Companion to the academic paper; HARNESS-* cards cover what PAPER-* does not.
items.done: The summary doc exists; HARNESS-* cards reference it.

items.title: Ralph Loop — two-phase long-running pattern with filesystem continuity
items.summary: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness (Pachaar, 2026): long tasks spanning many context windows use an Initializer agent (env + progress file + feature list + initial git commit), then a Coding agent each session that reads git logs + progress files, picks the highest-priority incomplete feature, works, commits, summarizes. The filesystem provides continuity — the agent re-derives 'where was I' from durable state, not a carried transcript. Vanta has loops + snapshots + handoff; formalize a progress-file + feature-list resume so a fresh session orients from durable state and chooses what to pick up.
items.done: A long task persists a progress file + feature list; a fresh session orients from it and resumes the top incomplete item (or drops it) rather than inheriting a stale transcript.

items.title: Memory-level guardrails — freshness / conflict / provenance before acting
items.summary: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness (Pachaar, 2026) (comment gap on component 9) + the 'memory is a hint, verify against state' principle: before the agent acts on a retrieved memory, check it is fresh, non-conflicting, and provenanced — embeddings return topically similar but contextually wrong items (where context rot starts). Vanta has brain (semantic/episodic/user_model) + recall; add a guard that validates a recalled memory against current state before it drives an action.
items.done: Recalled memories pass a freshness/conflict/provenance guard before influencing a tool call; stale/conflicting ones are flagged not used.

items.title: Observation masking — hide stale tool outputs, keep the tool calls
items.summary: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness (Pachaar, 2026) component 4 (JetBrains Junie strategy): a context strategy distinct from compaction — old tool OUTPUTS are masked from the model's context while the tool CALLS stay visible, so the agent remembers what it did without re-paying for voluminous outputs. Vanta has compress/CCR; add masking of superseded tool outputs as a cheap pre-compaction layer.
items.done: Superseded tool outputs are masked from context (calls retained); reduces tokens before heavier compaction runs.

items.title: Per-task tool scoping — expose the minimum tool set per step
items.summary: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness (Pachaar, 2026) decision 6: more tools = worse performance (Vercel removed 80% of tools and improved; Claude lazy-loads for 95% context reduction). Vanta exposes all 65 tools every turn. Scope the exposed set to the task/step (e.g. by goal lens or a relevance pass), revealing more on demand. Complements PAPER-DEFERRED-SCHEMAS (lazy schemas) with subsetting.
items.done: The model sees a task-relevant tool subset per turn, not all 65; the full set is reachable on demand; measured context reduction.

items.title: Visual + computational verification loop (verify improves quality 2-3x)
items.summary: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness (Pachaar, 2026) component 10: verification separates toy demos from production — rules-based (tests/linters/types = ground truth), visual (screenshot the result via the browser), LLM-as-judge. Vanta has /verify + advisor; add screenshot-based visual verification for UI changes and make 'run it and observe' a first-class close-out step. Complements PAPER-OBSERVABILITY (generator-evaluator split).
items.done: A change can be closed only after a verification step: tests/types for logic, a screenshot for UI; the result is cited, not asserted.

items.title: Harness-thickness future-proofing audit — prune scaffolding as models improve
items.summary: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness (Pachaar, 2026) decision 7 + co-evolution: scaffolding should be removed as the building completes; harness complexity should DECREASE as models improve (Manus rebuilt 5x removing complexity; Anthropic deletes planning steps as models internalize them). Future-proofing test: performance scales with stronger models WITHOUT added harness complexity. For Vanta: a recurring audit that prunes prompt scaffolding / planning steps a stronger model no longer needs — not only adds them.
items.done: A periodic audit identifies harness scaffolding (prompt rules, planning steps) a current model no longer needs and removes it; complexity trends down, not up.

items.title: Carried goal starts paused — pick up or drop on launch
items.summary: A goal from a prior session no longer silently re-runs on a fresh launch (the weird everytime-its-an-old-task feeling, esp. when testing). The carried goal is framed PAUSED in the system prompt (prompt.ts goalsPaused), surfaced as a one-time TUI notice, and activated only by /goal resume (re-injects) or referenced by the user. /goal clear|drop drops it. VANTA_GOAL_RESUME=auto restores the old always-active behavior. Realizes part of HARNESS-RALPH-LOOP (durable-state continuity you opt into).
items.done: Fresh launch does not auto-run last session goal; /goal resume activates, /goal clear drops; prompt framing + resume/drop tested; 149 tests green.

items.title: Self-correcting-harness — reference
items.summary: docs/research/self-correcting-harness.md — extract of Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026): the self-correction flywheel (bad trace to regression-locked) + its four layers + principles. Third in the harness series; SELFHARNESS-* cards trace here.
items.done: The summary doc exists; SELFHARNESS-* cards reference it.

items.title: Self-correction loop — failure to diagnosed fix to regression-locked
items.summary: Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026): the umbrella flywheel — a captured failure is diagnosed (causal chain from the trace), a kernel-gated diff is proposed, the human approves, the agent reruns the exact failing input for side-by-side comparison, and the original failure is locked as a regression test. Every cycle the harness gets harder to break. Vanta has the kernel + ERRORS.md + /verify + delegate; this composes them into one self-repair loop. Umbrella for the SELFHARNESS-* cards.
items.done: A failure can be run end-to-end: diagnose, propose a gated diff, approve, rerun the failing input, lock a regression test — one loop.

items.title: Failing run to regression test — the trace-as-asset principle
items.summary: Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026): every debugged failure auto-becomes a regression test, so the suite grows from REAL production failures not synthetic scenarios; diagnosis without a locked test just means debugging the same thing next month. Vanta logs failures to ERRORS.md (passive); extend it so a resolved failure auto-emits a regression case (the failing input + the expected behavior) into a growing suite.
items.done: Resolving a failure produces a regression test (failing input + expected behavior) added to a suite; the suite grows from real failures.
items.note: Verification organ keystone: regression_lock tool (lock {claim, command, expect} = a regression case; check re-runs the locked commands, flags a regression if the substring is gone or the command fails, each run approval-gated; list shows status) + verify/store.ts (verify.jsonl) + pure verify/check.ts grading + /locks view. A debugged failure becomes a locked, re-runnable regression case; the suite grows from real failures. 14 tests.

items.title: Plain-English assertion suite (LLM-as-judge), not numeric metrics
items.summary: [/skeptic shipped: adversarially-verify-a-claim command (refute by default, demand run-it/read-file/cite-test evidence) — the verification-organ first slice.] Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026): eval via natural-language assertions ("the response must never reveal unauthorized info") converted to LLM-as-judge clean pass/fail per case — matches how engineers think about quality, unlike numerical-metric datasets. Vanta has /verify + advisor; add a test-suite layer of plain-English assertions judged by a separate critic. Complements PAPER-OBSERVABILITY (generator-evaluator split).
items.done: A suite of plain-English assertions runs as LLM-as-judge pass/fail per case against a captured input.

items.title: Human only at diff approval — automate the low-signal loops
items.summary: Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026): put the human ONLY where full automation costs more trust than it saves. Root-cause and regression-locking are low-signal (automate); the DIFF APPROVAL is the high-signal/high-trust step (keep the human). A "where to put the human" rule that refines PAPER-REVERSIBILITY-RISK: the agent may diagnose + draft the fix autonomously, but the diff is kernel-gated to a human approve. Auto-merge would break trust fast.
items.done: Self-correction runs diagnosis + draft autonomously; only the diff application is human/kernel-gated; nothing auto-merges.

items.title: Capture the active config per trace for reproducible reruns
items.summary: Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026): every trace records which agent config (prompt/model/tools) was active, so a failing input can be re-run reproducibly later. Vanta has events.jsonl; capture the resolved config (provider, model, tool set, prompt hash) alongside an event so a failure can be reproduced under the same config. Prerequisite for the self-correction rerun step.
items.done: prepareRun logs a session_config event (provider/model/contextWindow/tools/promptChars/promptHash) to events.jsonl at startup, best-effort — a past failing input can be re-run under the same setup. Pure module (sessions/config-event.ts) + tests.

items.title: Agent sandbox — test a config change end-to-end without touching git
items.summary: Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026): run the fully-instrumented agent end-to-end on a config change (swap prompt/model/tool) and watch the whole graph respond, producing a trace — safely, without touching the working tree; usable by a non-developer. Vanta has the TUI + mode cycle + worktree isolation (subagents); a sandbox that runs a candidate config against a saved input and shows a side-by-side result.
items.done: A config change (prompt/model/tool) can be run end-to-end against a saved input in isolation, producing a comparable trace, no git mutation.

items.title: Prune the regression suite as tools/schema evolve
items.summary: Your Agent Harness Should Repair Itself (Pachaar, 2026) (comment thread): a locked regression input goes STALE when the tools or schema move under it — the suite needs pruning to stay honest, or it just gets better at explaining yesterday bug. A maintenance pass that detects locked inputs whose tool/schema assumptions no longer hold and flags them for refresh/removal.
items.done: Stale locked inputs (tool/schema drift) are detected and flagged for refresh or removal; the suite stays honest.

items.title: Keyless web search is standard — Bing + Jina-over-DDG fallbacks
items.summary: Vanta added two no-API-key web-search fallbacks (BingProvider HTML scrape, JinaDdgProvider via Jina Reader over DuckDuckGo) into the AUTO provider chain, so web search works out of the box without SERPAPI/BRAVE keys. Built in-session by Vanta, reviewed + committed; 32 search tests green. Named via VANTA_SEARCH_PROVIDER=bing|jina_ddg.
items.done: search/index.ts auto chain includes Bing + Jina keyless providers; web_search works with no keys; tests green; documented in .env.example.

items.title: UI-TARS-desktop — desktop-operator reference
items.summary: docs/research/ui-tars-desktop.md — github.com/bytedance/UI-TARS-desktop (TS, Apache-2.0, ~29k stars): multimodal desktop/browser agent (screenshot perception + keyboard/mouse + local control). Reference for the DESKTOP-* action schema + vision grounding + safety boundary. Inspect before Vanta DESKTOP work; not a dependency.
items.done: Reference doc captured; DESKTOP-* cards trace to it.

items.title: Desktop action schema — click/type/scroll/observe primitives
items.summary: From UI-TARS: an action model for OS control — click/double/right/drag, type, key, scroll, wait, screenshot — with targets specified by coords / accessibility tree / vision grounding. Vanta has vision-IN (screenshot, look_at_screen, describe_image) but no action-OUT; define a kernel-gated desktop action schema as the foundation of DESKTOP work.
items.done: A typed desktop-action schema exists, each action describeForSafety-able and kernel-assessable.
items.note: DONE (browser surface): typed BrowserAction schema (navigate/click/type/press/scroll/wait), each describeForSafety-able + kernel-assessable. OS-level coord/a11y targeting tracked under DESKTOP-VISION-TO-ACTION.

items.title: Vision-to-action loop — screenshot → grounded, safe action
items.summary: From UI-TARS: turn a screenshot into a grounded action (locate the target element, choose the action) with a re-observe cadence (act, screenshot, verify the screen changed as expected, recover on mis-click). Vanta has the perception tools; build the perceive→ground→act→verify loop, kernel-gated per action.
items.done: Vanta can locate a UI target from a screenshot and execute one grounded action, re-observe, and detect a mis-click.
items.note: In progress: element-grounding shipped (browser/observe.ts — the perceive→ground step). HORIZON: the screenshot-grounded act→re-observe→mis-click-detect loop + OS-level (non-browser) control need a desktop driver (UI-TARS coords/a11y tree). See docs/research/ui-tars-desktop.md + rocks-completion-ledger.md.

items.title: Local desktop control within the kernel boundary (no feral agent)
items.summary: From UI-TARS: let an agent drive a real desktop WITHOUT going feral — scope which apps/regions it may touch, confirm irreversible UI actions, a kill-switch. This is exactly where Vanta's kernel assess() belongs: every click/type assessed, destructive/irreversible UI actions (close-without-save, send, delete, purchase) escalate to ask/block.
items.done: Desktop actions route through the kernel; irreversible UI actions escalate; a kill-switch halts; scope limits which surfaces are touchable.
items.note: DONE (browser surface): browser_act routes through the kernel approval queue, irreversible controls/secret/Enter escalate with a masked dry-run preview, kill-switch VANTA_BROWSER_DISABLED halts before launch, domain-allowlist scopes reach. OS-level region-scope is horizon (needs a desktop driver).

items.title: Ultrathink — extended-reasoning / thinking-budget mode
items.summary: Give Vanta a deliberate deep-thinking mode (cf. extended thinking): a larger reasoning budget for hard problems, surfaced as a mode/flag (/ultrathink or an effort level) that raises the thinking allowance + prompts for a plan-before-act pass on complex tasks. Vanta has onThinking + the mode cycle; add an explicit high-effort thinking mode.
items.done: /ultrathink <task> — wraps the task with a deep-reasoning methodology (restate goal+criteria → plan+tradeoffs → edge cases+alternative → act+verify) and re-runs it. Shipped as a slash command (think-cmd.ts).

items.title: Ultracode — multi-agent coding orchestration
items.summary: A deep coding mode that fans work across subagents deterministically (decompose → parallel build → adversarial verify → synthesize), like a workflow harness. Vanta has delegate/swarm/loops/compose_workflow; package them into an /ultracode flow that orchestrates a multi-agent coding push with an eval gate.
items.done: /ultracode <task> — injects a multi-agent coding orchestration directive (decompose → delegate/swarm parallel subagents → adversarial verify → synthesize + full suite). Shipped as a slash command.

items.title: Deep-research harness — fan-out search, verify, synthesize
items.summary: A research mode: fan-out web searches across angles, fetch + read sources, adversarially verify claims (default-skeptic), synthesize a cited report. Vanta has web_search/web_fetch + swarm + the keyless providers; package into a /deep-research flow with a completeness critic. Mirrors the deep-research pattern.
items.done: /deep-research <question> — injects a fan-out research methodology (decompose → multi web_search/web_fetch → skeptic-verify with a 2nd source → cited synthesis). Shipped as a slash command.

items.title: Vanta wanted-abilities wishlist — capability north-star
items.summary: vanta-wanted-abilities.md — Vanta's own 17-area capability wishlist + a top-10 priority + an explicit do-NOT-want list. WANT-* cards capture the new areas; many overlap existing PAPER-*/HARNESS-*/SELFHARNESS-*/DESKTOP-*/TUI-* cards (noted per card).
items.done: Wishlist captured; WANT-* cards trace to it; overlaps cross-referenced.

items.title: Opportunity radar — native business/opportunity engine
items.summary: [SLICE 1 SHIPPED: radar store (~/.vanta/radar.jsonl: scored opportunities) + radar tool (record/score/list, pain+buyer scoring) + /radar ranked view. Remaining: continuous free-source scanning, evidence-tied offer gen, productization detector.] vanta-wanted-abilities.md §2 (top priority): continuous free-source scanning (markets, GitHub, jobs, grants/RFPs, forums, trends) → pain scoring (expensive/urgent/repeated/reachable) → buyer scoring → evidence-tied offer generator → artifact generator → market-test tracker → productization detector. Builds on web_search + swarm + the keyless providers.
items.done: A /radar mode scans free sources, scores opportunities by pain+buyer signal, and generates an evidence-tied offer + artifact.
items.note: DONE. scan_web (radar/extract.ts: extractOpportunities scores pain+buyer signals from live search results via resolveSearchProvider, degrades gracefully when the keyless backend 403s) + ranking (radar/scan.ts) + evidence-tied offer drafting + promote→Money-OS prospect. All done-criterion clauses met (live result quality depends on a reachable search backend — keyed provider/Searxng recommended).

items.title: Money OS — structured money-making operating system
items.summary: [SLICE 1 SHIPPED: money store (~/.vanta/money.jsonl: offers/prospects/revenue) + money tool (offer/prospect/revenue/review) + /money ledger. Remaining: pricing experiments, deliverable templates, case-study gen, weekly review.] vanta-wanted-abilities.md §16 (top priority): offer library + pricing experiments, prospect/reply pipeline, deliverable templates (reports/audits/demos/microtools), revenue ledger, follow-up scheduler, case-study generator, weekly business review. Ethical guardrails: no deception/spam/fake identity. Pairs with WANT-OPPORTUNITY-RADAR + WANT-OUTREACH-WORKSPACE.
items.done: Machinery (not vibes) for: offers, prospects, deliverables, revenue ledger, follow-ups, weekly review — ethically guardrailed.
items.note: DONE (S1–S4). offers + prospects + revenue ledger + pricing (money/review.ts suggestPrice) + weekly review + deliverables + follow-ups (money/work.ts) + radar→prospect inflow. Every mutation kernel-gated (the ethical guardrail). All six pillars present.

items.title: Authorized brand/outreach workspace — draft-only, approval-gated
items.summary: vanta-wanted-abilities.md §1 (top priority): an authorized brand workspace SEPARATE from Jason's personal identity; draft-only outbound (email/DM/forms); approval queue for message batches; reply watcher (interest/objection/opportunity); CRM memory (people/companies/promises/follow-ups); next-best-action; proof ledger. Hard line (do-not-want §18): never send without approval, never a fake identity.
items.done: Outbound is draft-only + batch-approved via the kernel; replies watched; a proof ledger records sent/received/changed. No autonomous sending or fake identity.

items.title: World model graph — entities + relationships across Jason's systems
items.summary: [SLICE 1 SHIPPED: append-only world store (~/.vanta/world.jsonl) + a kernel-gated `world` tool (record/relate/query) + a /world view; latest-write-wins per entity, relations, confidence. Remaining: conflict detection, cited what-do-I-know-about-X, approval-sensitive auto-updates from email/calendar/files, project dashboards.] vanta-wanted-abilities.md §4 (top priority): a coherent graph (people/projects/repos/companies/goals/assets/accounts/commitments) with relationships (owns/depends-on/blocked-by/promised-to/next-action-for), freshness timestamps + confidence labels, conflict detection on contradicting facts, cited 'what do I know about X?', approval-sensitive auto-updates from email/calendar/files/repos. Beyond the current brain (snippets) → a reasoned map. Overlaps HARNESS-MEMORY-GUARDRAILS.
items.done: A queryable entity-relationship graph with freshness/confidence + conflict detection; 'what do I know about X' returns cited facts with uncertainty.
items.note: DONE (S1–S4). Queryable entity-relationship graph (record/relate/query) + conflict detection (world/conflicts.ts) + cited recall + entity merge/dedup (world/merge.ts) + freshness/confidence/uncertainty (world/confidence.ts: recall returns [likely · 62% · source:ts]). All done-criterion clauses met.

items.title: Life-wide semantic search — unified, permission-aware index
items.summary: [SLICE 1 SHIPPED: unified keyword search across the local stores (world/money/radar/team JSONL + ERRORS.md) with source-cited hits, via a life_search tool + /lifesearch. Local-only/permission-aware. Remaining: semantic embeddings, files/mail/calendar/drive index, refresh scheduler.] vanta-wanted-abilities.md §5 (top priority): a unified semantic index over repos/notes/docs/transcripts/email/calendar/drive/screenshots/reports; permission-aware retrieval (only what's allowed for the task); source-cited answers across stores; private/local embedding option; refresh scheduler with change detection. 'Find the thing I saw/wrote/talked about.'
items.done: One semantic index spanning Jason's stores; permission-aware, source-cited retrieval; local-embedding option; change-detecting refresh.
items.note: DONE. Semantic search via LOCAL embeddings (search/embed.ts: ollama /api/embeddings + cosineSim, zero new dep, VANTA_EMBED_MODEL default nomic-embed-text, graceful lexical fallback when ollama is down) + source-cited cross-store retrieval + lexical ranker + change-detecting refresh (search/refresh.ts djb2 digests). Local-embedding option + all other clauses met.

items.title: Self-repair compartment model — body boundaries + limb sandbox
items.summary: [SLICE 1 SHIPPED: a body compartment map (brainstem/skeleton/reflexes/limbs/memory) with per-compartment max-autonomy, classifying any path; /compartments view. Brainstem(kernel/manifesto)+skeleton(factory) = never self-edit. Remaining: limb sandbox, auto-rollback, broken-capability detector.] vanta-wanted-abilities.md §8: an explicit compartment map (skeleton/brainstem/limbs/reflexes/memory) with per-compartment max-autonomy; a limb sandbox to build/replace/test a tool in isolation before attaching; automatic rollback for failed self-edits; a broken-capability detector that opens a repair loop when a tool fails repeatedly. Builds on the factory + protected-paths. Do-not-want §18: never edit kernel/manifesto/brainstem autonomously.
items.done: Self-edits target only non-protected compartments; a new/replaced tool is sandbox-tested before attach; failed edits auto-rollback; repeated tool failure opens a repair loop.
items.note: Auto-rollback NOW SHIPPED (the named blocker): self_repair tool — mark (record HEAD as a compartment's last-known-good) + rollback (kernel-gated git checkout of the compartment's paths, approval-gated with a discards-changes warning, REFUSES protected brainstem/skeleton + unscoped limbs) + status. Plus S1–S3: body map + health detection + propose-only view. LAST CLAUSE remaining: sandbox-test a new/replaced tool before attach (a distinct build atop run-code isolation). See docs/research/rocks-completion-ledger.md.

items.title: Personal taste engine — Jason-specific design preference model
items.summary: vanta-wanted-abilities.md §11: a persistent Jason-specific design preference model; before/after visual critique memory; brand-safe image/icon/layout defaults; artifact scoring (clarity/usefulness/beauty/credibility/actionability); visual regression snapshots; reusable design systems per project. So generated artifacts aren't generic/disposable.
items.done: Generated artifacts are scored + critiqued against a persisted taste model; brand-safe defaults; visual-regression snapshots for generated apps.

items.title: Setup assistant — frictionless OAuth / provider / MCP / secrets
items.summary: vanta-wanted-abilities.md §13: guided OAuth provisioning (Google/Gmail/Calendar/Drive); provider-key wizard with validation + live test; MCP server install flow; a health dashboard with EXACT missing capabilities; secret-manager integration (Bitwarden Secrets Manager); isolated profiles per project/identity. Capabilities that need setup stay theoretical until setup is painless. WANT-CAPABILITY-HEALTH is the first slice.
items.done: A wizard guides OAuth + key + MCP setup with live validation; a health dashboard names exactly what's missing + the fix; secrets via a manager.

items.title: Capability health view — what works, what's missing, the exact fix
items.summary: vanta-wanted-abilities.md §13 (first tractable slice): a /health view listing each capability (gmail/calendar/drive, web search, vision, browser, kernel, provider key) with ✓ ready / ✗ missing + the EXACT enabling step (e.g. 'run vanta auth google'). Directly fixes the friction of hitting 'not authorized' walls mid-task. Builds on /doctor + /status.
items.done: /health shows each capability (kernel, model+key, web search, gmail/calendar/drive, vision, browser, mcp) with ✓ ready / ✗ missing + the exact enabling command. Pure formatHealth + per-cap checks, tested.

items.title: Reach capability layer — channels with probed backends + doctor
items.summary: From Agent-Reach (MIT, docs/research/agent-reach-eval.md): a `reach/` layer where each platform is a channel with an ORDERED backend list (primary + fallbacks), a real-probe check() that sets the active backend, an env override, and a fix prescription. Mirrors Vanta's resolveSearchProvider/provider pattern. Generalize search+web+video+browser under one reach contract so adding a platform = one channel file.
items.done: A reach channel contract (canHandle/read/search/check + ordered backends + override) exists in TS, kernel-gated, with the existing web/search/video tools expressible as channels; one new channel proves the pattern.
items.note: DONE. reach/ layer: ReachChannel contract (canHandle/check/ordered backends + <NAME>_BACKEND override), probe.ts (really-executes not which()), registry (resolveChannel + checkAll), web + search seed channels, /reach doctor. 9 tests.

items.title: Per-channel reach doctor — active backend + exact fix
items.summary: From Agent-Reach `doctor`: upgrade /health into a per-channel reach report — for each channel show the active backend, ok/warn/off status from a real probe, and the EXACT enabling command on a gap. Extends the existing /health capability view.
items.done: /health (or /reach) reports each reach channel's active backend + status + the exact fix command for any gap.
items.note: DONE (folded into REACH-LAYER). /reach: per-channel status + active backend + exact fix command.

items.title: RSS/Atom channel — subscribe + read feeds
items.summary: From Agent-Reach rss.py (feedparser): a zero-config, pure-TS RSS/Atom read tool (native fetch + parse, no external CLI). First reach channel to build — cheapest, and feeds Opportunity-Radar's scan_web with curated pain-signal feeds (subreddits-as-RSS, HN, indie-hacker blogs).
items.done: An rss_read tool fetches + parses an RSS/Atom feed to clean items (title/link/date/summary); errors-as-values; tested with an inline fixture (no network).
items.note: DONE. reach/rss-parse.ts — dependency-free RSS 2.0 + Atom parser (block+tag extractor, CDATA/entity clean, tolerant). rss_read tool (native fetch, limit, errors-as-values) + rss reach channel (canHandle feed URLs, routes before web). 20 tests. 74 tools. + feed auto-discovery (Agent-Reach #322): fetchFeed follows a site page's <link rel=alternate> feed.

items.title: Reddit channel — search + read posts/comments
items.summary: From Agent-Reach reddit.py (OpenCLI ▸ rdt-cli, login-state required — anonymous API is blocked). Highest-leverage new channel: feeds Opportunity-Radar (pain signals) AND the gripe project (Reddit complaint DB). Shell out to the upstream CLI (kernel-gated), cookie/login config, degrade gracefully when absent (like the browser tools).
items.done: A reddit_read tool searches + reads posts/comments via the upstream CLI when present, returns clean cited results, and gives the exact setup step when not configured.
items.note: DONE. reddit_read tool (search + read post/comments) via Reddit's .json API authenticated with the stored cookie (reach/cookie.ts) — no external CLI; anonymous is blocked, so returns the exact cookie_import setup step when none configured. Pure reach/reddit-parse.ts (parseListing/parseThread/format, tolerant). reddit reach channel routes reddit.com URLs, check() reports cookie status + fix. Source-cited (permalinks). 19 tests. 76 tools.

items.title: Twitter/X channel — native GraphQL (no Python), search + bookmarks, self-healing query IDs
items.summary: Native TS X GraphQL client (reach/twitter.ts) — NO Python, no upstream CLI: public bearer + ct0 CSRF from the stored cookie + rotating query IDs. twitter_read search + bookmarks. Self-heals query-id rotation by capturing live IDs from a real browser (reach/twitter-capture.ts healTwitter, via browser-session), falling back to a static bundle scrape. Bearer/features/query-ids env-overridable. Extracted as the standalone MIT repo 'x-native' (github.com/jpoindexter/x-native).
items.done: twitter_read bookmarks/search return real results with a cookie + captured query IDs; query-id rotation self-heals via browser capture; live-verified (67 bookmarks pulled).
items.note: PORTED TO NATIVE TS (no Python — twitter-cli dropped per 'we aren't gonna use python in this agent'). reach/twitter.ts native X GraphQL client (bearer + ct0 CSRF + cookie), reach/twitter-parse.ts pure parsers (extractAuth/parseTimeline tree-walk/extractQueryIds/graphqlError). twitter_read: search + bookmarks. Query ids resolved env → ~/.vanta/twitter-qids.json cache → scrape. SELF-HEAL works live: reach heal twitter scraped 157 query ids incl SearchTimeline. OPEN: Bookmarks query id is in a lazy chunk not on the homepage — set VANTA_TWITTER_QID_BOOKMARKS (devtools) or build a deeper chunk-scraper. Caveat: native fetch lacks TLS-impersonation so X may 403 some IPs.

items.title: LinkedIn channel — public pages via Jina Reader
items.summary: From Agent-Reach linkedin.py (linkedin-mcp ▸ Jina Reader). The Jina-Reader path reads public LinkedIn profile/company pages with zero auth — Vanta already has Readable web fetch, so this is mostly a canHandle + read mapping. Deeper profile/job-search needs the MCP backend (later).
items.done: A LinkedIn reach path reads public profile/company pages via the Readable fetch backend; deeper access documented as the MCP-backed follow-up.
items.note: DONE. linkedin reach channel + linkedin_read tool, powered by the browser-session capability (reach/browser-session.ts openWithSession + browser-cookies). Reads login-walled/JS LinkedIn (profiles/companies/posts) via a headless browser using your logged-in session (browser:'brave' auto-reads cookies, or cookie_import linkedin). Routes linkedin.com URLs; /reach reports it. 80 tools.

items.title: Cookie import flow — browser-exported cookie → channel auth
items.summary: From Agent-Reach cookie_extract.py: the shared auth path for login-walled channels (Reddit, Twitter, Xiaohongshu). User exports a cookie via the Cookie-Editor extension and hands it to Vanta; stored 0600 in ~/.vanta, never uploaded. Underpins REACH-REDDIT + REACH-TWITTER — build before them. (Agent-Reach prefers cookie export over QR scan: simpler + more reliable.)
items.done: A cookie import command stores a browser-exported cookie for a named channel at ~/.vanta (0600), validated, with a clear export-and-paste guide; the auth'd channels read it.
items.note: DONE. reach/cookie.ts — parseCookieInput (Cookie-Editor JSON export OR k=v header → header, pure), slug-validated channel names (no traversal), 0600 store at ~/.vanta/cookies/<channel>.cookie. cookie_import tool (kernel-gated via credential-signaling describeForSafety; never echoes the value) + /cookie guide. 17 tests. 75 tools. Underpins REACH-REDDIT/REACH-TWITTER.

items.title: Podcast channel — episode audio → transcript (Whisper)
items.summary: From Agent-Reach xiaoyuzhou.py (小宇宙 + Whisper): fetch a podcast episode's audio and transcribe it to text for the agent to read/summarize. Vanta already has a `transcribe` tool (audio→text); this channel adds episode resolution + the podcast source. Generalize beyond 小宇宙 to any podcast/audio URL.
items.done: A podcast reach path resolves an episode to audio and returns a transcript via the existing transcribe tool; degrades clearly when the transcribe backend is unavailable.

items.title: V2EX channel — hot/node threads + replies (zero-config)
items.summary: From Agent-Reach v2ex.py: zero-config read of V2EX hot posts, node posts, thread detail + replies, user info (public API, no auth). Cheap pure-TS channel (native fetch + parse). Niche for Jason but zero-config, so low-cost to add for dev-community signal.
items.done: A v2ex reach path reads hot/node threads + a thread's detail and replies via the public API; errors-as-values; tested with an inline fixture.

items.title: Bilibili channel — search + video detail + subtitles
items.summary: From Agent-Reach bilibili.py (bili-cli ▸ OpenCLI ▸ search API; yt-dlp retired after Bilibili 412 anti-scrape). Search + video detail with no login; subtitles via OpenCLI. Shows the multi-backend re-routing value (the channel survived an upstream break by swapping backends). China-centric — defer unless a concrete need.
items.done: A bilibili reach path searches + reads video detail via the upstream CLI when present, with the documented fallback order and a fix prescription on a gap.

items.title: Xiaohongshu (小红书) channel — search + read notes
items.summary: From Agent-Reach xiaohongshu.py (OpenCLI desktop ▸ xiaohongshu-mcp server ▸ xhs-cli). Login-state required; reuses browser login via OpenCLI or scan-login via the MCP server. China-centric consumer-product sentiment source. Defer unless a concrete need; needs REACH-COOKIE-IMPORT / OpenCLI.
items.done: A xiaohongshu reach path searches + reads notes via the upstream backend when configured, with the setup guide and graceful degradation otherwise.

items.title: Xueqiu (雪球) channel — quotes, stock search, hot posts
items.summary: From Agent-Reach xueqiu.py: stock quotes, search, hot posts, hot-stock ranking (needs light config). Finance/market signal source, China-centric. Defer unless a concrete need (e.g. a markets/finance use case appears).
items.done: A xueqiu reach path returns quotes + stock search + hot posts when configured; errors-as-values; setup guidance on a gap.

items.title: Self-healing reach backends — rebuild when the platform changes
items.summary: A reach channel declares heal(); the kernel-gated `reach` tool runs `reach doctor` + `reach heal <channel>` to rebuild a broken CLI-backed channel and re-check. twitter heal re-pulls twitter-cli (uv ▸ pipx ▸ pip ladder) since the maintainer tracks X's rotating GraphQL query IDs. The reach analogue of the self-repair organ (detect off → heal → re-check). reach/heal.ts tryUpgrade + channel.heal contract.
items.done: A broken CLI-backed reach channel can rebuild its backend via reach heal (kernel-gated), then re-check; built-in channels correctly refuse. Tested.
items.note: DONE. reach/heal.ts (tryUpgrade + pyToolUpgradeCommands), channel.heal contract, twitter channel heal, reach tool (doctor+heal). Answers 'if Vanta can fix itself it should rebuild the twitter thing every time it changes.' 9 tests.

items.title: Auto-minimalism mode — /auto command + skill (do the least that works)
items.summary: Rewritten from ponytail (MIT) as `auto`: a 'do the least that works' coding discipline (YAGNI→stdlib→native→installed-dep→one-line ladder; deletion over addition; `auto:` ceiling-comments naming the upgrade path). Vanta: /auto [lite|full|ultra|off|review] injects a live system-prompt directive (mirrors /planmode's marker); review resends a deletion-accounting rubric, distinct from /simplify's general cleanup. Skill skills-library/auto auto-installs to ~/.vanta + ~/.claude. Also shipped GLOBAL for Claude Code: ~/.claude/commands/auto.md (a /auto command in any project) + ~/.claude/skills/auto.
items.done: /auto toggles minimalism mode live (7 unit tests); /auto review audits a diff; the global /auto works in any Claude Code project; full suite green, size gate clean.

items.title: Authenticated browser read — browser_read + browser-session primitive (any login-walled/JS page)
items.summary: reach/browser-session.ts openWithSession(url, cookie): opens ANY url in a headless browser with session cookies injected (url-scoped, handles __Host-/__Secure-, drops control-char values), returns rendered text + every request the page made. browser_read {url, browser:'brave'} wraps it — reads login-walled / JS-rendered pages (x.com, reddit, linkedin, internal apps) by auto-injecting your logged-in cookies for that domain. General primitive; the X query-id capture + the reddit fallback are consumers. Distinct from AUTH-BROWSER (persistent manual-login profile). Extracted as the standalone MIT repo 'browser-session' (github.com/jpoindexter/browser-session).
items.done: browser_read renders any logged-in page headlessly with the injected session; proven live on X / Reddit / HN / vercel; wired as the reddit channel's fallback + the linkedin channel's backend.

items.title: Zero-paste cookie import — auto-read the live browser cookie store
items.summary: reach/browser-cookies.ts: cookie_import {channel, browser:'brave'} reads logged-in cookies straight from the browser's encrypted store (macOS Keychain key → PBKDF2 → AES-128-CBC decrypt, 32-byte SHA256(host)-prefix handling, zero deps) — no Cookie-Editor export/paste. One Keychain approval the first time; falls back to the paste/file flow (REACH-COOKIE-IMPORT) on any other browser/OS. Channel→host map (twitter→x.com, reddit→reddit.com).
items.done: cookie_import {browser:'brave'} stores a channel cookie with no manual export (verified: 35 x.com cookies auto-read from Brave); kernel-gated, stored 0600, never echoed.
